Table member.subscriber {
  subscriber_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for subscriber']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'FK to member.contact']
  group_division_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_division. Business justification: Subscribers (employees) belong to a specific employer group division, which determines their benefit eligibility, contribution amounts, and plan offerings. HR/benefits administration requires division']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Required for payroll‑deduction enrollment reports linking each employee subscriber to their employer group for contribution calculations and ERISA compliance.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health plan product.']
  primary_care_coordinator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_coordinator. Business justification: Primary Care Coordinator Assignment: case‑management reports need each subscriber linked to their assigned care coordinator.']
  primary_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: REQUIRED: Primary Care Provider assignment for each subscriber is needed for care coordination, network adequacy reporting, and member portal display.']
  annual_income decimal(12,2) [note: 'Subscriber\'s reported annual income (for subsidy eligibility).']
  chip_number varchar [note: 'Children\'s Health Insurance Program identifier.']
  citizenship_status varchar [note: 'Legal citizenship or residency status.']
  cob_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates subscriber\'s position in coordination of benefits.']
  consent_to_electronic_communication boolean [note: 'Subscriber\'s consent to receive electronic communications.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Type of health plan coverage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscriber record was created.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Subscriber\'s birth date.']
  disability_status varchar [note: 'Indicates if subscriber has a disability.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when subscriber coverage becomes effective.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which subscriber enrolled.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s given name.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s gender as reported.']
  hcc_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated HCC score for the subscriber.']
  is_deceased boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if subscriber is deceased.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s family name.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line the subscriber belongs to.']
  marital_status varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s marital status.']
  medicaid_number varchar [note: 'State Medicaid identifier.']
  medicare_beneficiary_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for Medicare beneficiaries.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s middle name (if any).']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for contacting subscriber.']
  race_ethnicity varchar [note: 'Self-reported race/ethnicity for reporting.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Factor used for risk adjustment calculations.']
  ssn varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s Social Security Number.']
  subscriber_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned to the subscriber.']
  subscriber_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subscriber.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when subscriber coverage ends (if applicable).']
  tobacco_use_status varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s tobacco use status.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subscriber record.']
  veteran_status boolean [note: 'Indicates if subscriber is a veteran.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing the primary insured individual (subscriber/policyholder) who holds the health insurance contract. Captures full demographic identity including SSN, date of birth, gender, marital status, language preference, race/ethnicity (for HEDIS/CMS reporting), tobacco use status, disability status, and Medicare/Medicaid beneficiary identifiers. Serves as the SSOT for subscriber identity across commercial, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, and CHIP lines of business. All dependent, eligibility, and benefit records reference this entity. Supports HIPAA PHI/PII obligations, CMS enrollment reporting, and X12 834 subscriber loop requirements.'
}

Adding member_address_id FK normalizes dep']

Business justification: Dependent has inline contact fields (email_address, phone_number) that duplicate the authoritative member_contact table. Adding member_contact_id FK normalizes dependent contact data to the canonical ']
  identity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.identity. Business justification: The identity product is the authoritative identity resolution record for each unique insured individual — explicitly including both subscribers AND dependents. Currently identity.subscriber_id links i']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary member (subscriber) to whom this dependent is attached.']
  age_out_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'True if the dependent is eligible for age-out coverage under plan rules.']
  chip_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates eligibility for the Children\'s Health Insurance Program.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Date when dependent\'s coverage terminated or is scheduled to end.']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'Date when dependent\'s coverage became effective.']
  coverage_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the dependent\'s coverage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dependent record was first created in the system.']

  disability_status boolean [note: 'Indicates if the dependent has a documented disability.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the dependent.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the dependent as reported.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates if this dependent is the primary contact for the subscriber\'s household.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications with the dependent.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the dependent.']
  medicaid_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates eligibility for Medicaid program.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the dependent.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the record itself.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date when the dependent relationship ended, if applicable.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when the dependent relationship became effective.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Legal relationship of the dependent to the subscriber.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal number to differentiate multiple dependents of same relationship type.']
  ssn varchar [note: 'Government-issued identifier for the dependent.']
  student_status boolean [note: 'Indicates if the dependent is currently a student (True) or not (False).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dependent record.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing individuals covered under a subscriber\'s health plan — spouses, domestic partners, children, and other qualifying dependents. Tracks relationship type to subscriber, dependent sequence number, student status, disability status, age-out eligibility rules, and CHIP/Medicaid eligibility indicators. Maintains its own demographic profile including DOB, gender, SSN, and language preference. Supports dependent verification workflows, age-out processing, and CMS CHIP enrollment reporting. Each dependent record is linked to a subscriber and an enrollment span.'
}

Table member.identity {
  identity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for identity']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Business process: Billing Account Management – each member identity must be linked to a billing account for premium collection, payment processing, and regulatory reporting.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'FK to member.member_address']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan to which the member is assigned.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the health plan to the member (e.g., commercial member ID, MBI for Medicare).']
  citizenship_status varchar [note: 'Citizenship or residency status relevant for eligibility and reporting.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Date when the member\'s coverage terminated or is scheduled to end.']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'Date when the member\'s coverage became effective.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the identity record was first created in the system.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Member\'s birth date, used for age‑based eligibility and risk calculations.']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Current eligibility determination for benefits coverage.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for member communications and portal login.']
  enrollment_effective_date date [note: 'Date the member\'s enrollment into the plan became effective.']
  ethnicity varchar [note: 'Member\'s ethnicity as reported for demographic reporting.']
  external_subscriber_number varchar [note: 'Identifier from an external source such as employer payroll or third‑party administrator.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Member\'s given (first) name.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete legal name of the member as it appears on official documents.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑identified gender of the member.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Primary language the member prefers for communications.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Member\'s family (last) name.']
  lob varchar [note: 'Business line (e.g., commercial, Medicare) to which the member belongs.']
  marital_status varchar [note: 'Member\'s marital status for demographic and eligibility purposes.']
  member_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the member within the health plan.']
  member_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record represents a primary subscriber or a dependent.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Member\'s middle name(s), if any.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for member contact.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for member communications.']
  race varchar [note: 'Member\'s race classification for HEDIS and regulatory reporting.']
  relationship_to_subscriber varchar [note: 'Describes the member\'s relationship to the primary subscriber.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score used for underwriting and care management.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the identity data.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the source record in the originating system.']
  ssn_hash varchar [note: 'Hashed Social Security Number used for identity matching while protecting PII.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the member\'s relationship with the plan was terminated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the identity record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the member\'s identity was successfully verified.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the member\'s identity verification process.']

  Note: 'Authoritative identity resolution record for each unique insured individual (subscriber or dependent) assigned a unique member ID by the health plan. Stores the plan-assigned member ID (MBI for Medicare, Medicaid ID, commercial member ID), cross-reference IDs from source systems (Facets member ID, QNXT ID), alternate IDs (SSN hash, external subscriber ID from employer), and identity verification status. Manages member ID history and superseded IDs. This is the enterprise golden record for member identity used by all downstream domains (claims, pharmacy, care management) to resolve member identity.'
}

Table member.address {
  address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the address record.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the member to whom this address belongs.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of address purpose.']
  census_tract varchar [note: 'Census tract identifier for demographic analysis.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for address change (e.g., member request, system update).']
  city varchar [note: 'City name of the address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code.']
  county_fips varchar [note: 'Federal Information Processing Standard code for the county.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the address record was first created in the data lake.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the address became effective for the member.']
  external_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for the address from source system.']
  geocode_accuracy_meters varchar [note: 'Accuracy of the geocoding result in meters.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates if this address is the primary address for the member.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude in decimal degrees.']
  line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line.']
  line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (apartment, suite, etc.).']
  line3 varchar [note: 'Additional address line if needed.']
  line4 varchar [note: 'Additional address line if needed.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude in decimal degrees.']
  member_address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the address.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit ZIP code, optionally with ZIP+4.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Rank indicating priority among multiple concurrent addresses for the member (1 = highest).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that supplied the address record (e.g., Facets, CRM).']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state abbreviation.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the address was terminated or superseded.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the address record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when address verification was performed.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'USPS CASS certification status of the address.']

  Note: 'Manages all physical and mailing addresses associated with a member, including home address, mailing address, temporary address, and employer address. Tracks address type, effective and termination dates, address verification status (USPS CASS-certified), county FIPS code, census tract, and geographic coordinates for network adequacy analysis. Supports multiple concurrent addresses with priority ranking. Used for premium billing, EOB mailing, provider directory access, and CMS geographic reporting. Maintains address history for audit and compliance purposes.'
}

Table member.contact {
  contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each contact record associated with a member.']
  address_cass_certified boolean [note: 'True if the address has passed USPS CASS certification.']

  address_geocode_source varchar [note: 'System used to generate latitude/longitude for the address.']

  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary street address line (apartment, suite, etc.).']
  address_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank indicating the order of address preference for the member.']
  address_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that originally supplied the address record.']
  address_termination_date date [note: 'Date when the address was terminated or superseded.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Purpose of the address (e.g., home, mailing, temporary).']
  address_verification_status varchar [note: 'USPS CASS certification status of the address.']
  census_tract varchar [note: 'Eleven‑digit census tract identifier for geographic analysis.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the address.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the contact person (member, dependent, or other party).']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact relationship to the member.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the address.']
  county_fips varchar [note: 'Five‑digit Federal Information Processing Standard code for the county.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contact record was first created.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary electronic mail address for the contact.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the contact.']
  id_type varchar [note: 'Type of identifier used for the contact (e.g., NPI, SSN).']
  id_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual identifier value corresponding to contact_id_type.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record is the member\'s primary contact.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Member\'s preferred language for communications.']
  last_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent address verification event.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the address in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the address in decimal degrees.']
  member_contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes related to the contact (e.g., preferred call times).']
  opt_in_email boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive email communications.']
  opt_in_robocall boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member permits automated voice calls.']
  opt_in_sms boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive SMS messages.']
  phone_home varchar [note: 'Primary residential telephone number.']
  phone_mobile varchar [note: 'Cellular telephone number for mobile contact.']
  phone_work varchar [note: 'Telephone number associated with the contact\'s place of work.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the address.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'Contact channel the member prefers for communications.']
  relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the contact to the member.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the address.']
  tcp_a_consent_flag boolean [note: 'True if the contact has provided consent under the Telephone Consumer Protection Act.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the contact record.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all member contact information and addresses. Manages physical and mailing addresses (home, mailing, temporary, employer), phone numbers (home, mobile, work), email addresses, and fax numbers. Tracks contact type, address type, effective and termination dates, address verification status (USPS CASS-certified), county FIPS code, census tract, geographic coordinates for network adequacy analysis, opt-in/opt-out status per channel (SMS, email, robocall), TCPA consent flags, and preferred contact method and language. Supports multiple concurrent addresses with priority ranking. Used for premium billing, EOB mailing, provider directory access, CMS geographic reporting, member services outreach, care management communications, and CAHPS survey administration. Maintains full address and contact history for audit and compliance purposes.'
}

Tracks eligibility status (active, terminated, suspended, COBRA), retroactive eligibility adjustments, and gap-in-coverage periods. The SSOT for answering \'Is this member eligible on date X?\' — used by claims adjudication, pharmacy, and provider eligibility verification (270/271 transactions).'

  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: COBRA administration requires tracking the specific benefit package from prior coverage to calculate correct COBRA premiums (102% of plan cost per ERISA). cobra_continuant only links to health_plan_id']
  cobra_election_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.cobra_election. Business justification: cobra_continuant (member domain) and cobra_election (enrollment domain) are complementary records of the same COBRA event.   disenrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.disenrollment. Business justification: COBRA continuation is triggered by a qualifying disenrollment event (loss of employer-sponsored coverage). Linking cobra_continuant to the triggering disenrollment record via disenrollment_id establis']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group that sponsored the original coverage.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan under which COBRA coverage is provided.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member who is the COBRA continuant.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.policy. Business justification: A COBRA continuant record represents continuation of a specific policy after loss of employer-sponsored coverage. Linking cobra_continuant to policy via policy_id enables traceability to the underlyin']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. COBRA system']
  qualifying_life_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.qualifying_life_event. Business justification: COBRA eligibility is triggered by qualifying life events. cobra_continuant has denormalized qualifying_event_type and qualifying_event_date but no FK to the QLE record. Linking to qualifying_life_even']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.rate. Business justification: COBRA premiums are calculated as 102% of the applicable plan rate per ERISA. Linking cobra_continuant to the specific plan.rate record enables COBRA premium notice accuracy, regulatory audit trails, a']
  tpa_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.tpa_arrangement. Business justification: COBRA administration for self-funded groups is governed by the TPA arrangement, which defines premium billing, stop-loss coverage, and administrative fees.   transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original enrollment that triggered COBRA eligibility.']
  cobra_continuant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the COBRA coverage.']

  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Date coverage under COBRA ends, either by max period or termination.']

  is_exhausted boolean [note: 'True when the maximum COBRA coverage period has been reached.']
  max_coverage_end_date date [note: 'Calculated date when the maximum allowable COBRA period expires.']
  max_coverage_months varchar [note: 'Maximum number of months the member may receive COBRA coverage (e.g., 18, 29, 36).']
  member_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the continuant to the primary employee (e.g., employee, spouse, child).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for any additional comments or audit notes.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the premium is billed.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to collect the COBRA premium.']
  premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monthly premium amount (typically 102% of the group rate).']
  premium_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the premium amount.']
  premium_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the premium must be paid for the coverage month.']
  premium_payment_date date [note: 'Date the premium payment was actually received.']
  premium_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of premium payment for the coverage period.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when COBRA coverage was terminated before the scheduled end.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for early termination of COBRA coverage, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Manages COBRA continuation coverage records for members who have lost employer-sponsored coverage due to qualifying events. Tracks qualifying event type (termination, reduction in hours, divorce, death, Medicare entitlement, dependent status loss), COBRA election date, election deadline, coverage start and end dates, maximum coverage period (18/29/36 months), premium amount (102% of group rate), payment status, and exhaustion date. Supports COBRA notice generation, election tracking, premium payment monitoring, and qualifying event triggering for the billing domain.'
}

  provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Required for compliance check that a PCP assignment respects an active provider contract; ensures network participation rules are enforced during PCP selection.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network to which the PCP belongs.']

  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the assignment data.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the PCP assignment ends or is scheduled to end; null if still active.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the PCP assignment record.']

  Note: 'Tracks Primary Care Provider (PCP) assignments for members enrolled in HMO, POS, and other gatekeeper plan types. Records assigned PCP NPI, PCP effective and termination dates, assignment type (member-selected, auto-assigned, plan-assigned), PCP change reason, PCP panel status at time of assignment, and PCP network tier. Manages PCP change request history and auto-assignment rules for newborns and new enrollees. Used for care coordination, referral authorization, capitation payment calculation, and HEDIS PCP attribution. Integrates with provider and network domains.'
}

  member_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.member_eligibility_span. Linking cob_record to member_e']

  cob_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the COB record.']
  cob_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the COB information was verified.']

  is_msp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with Medicare Secondary Payer rules.']

  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the other carrier policy.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the COB data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Facets|QNXT|Cactus|ProviderSource|RxClaim|Argus|InterQual|MCG|Casenet|TruCare|Altruista|Salesforce|Pega|HealthEdge|CustomBilling|OracleFinancials|Milliman|Availity|ChangeHealthcare|Snowflake — promote to reference product]']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the COB coverage ends or is terminated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the COB record.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the COB information.']

  Note: 'Coordination of Benefits (COB) record tracking a member\'s other insurance coverage to prevent duplicate payment and ensure correct primary/secondary/tertiary payer sequencing. Captures other carrier name, other carrier member ID, other carrier group number, policy type (commercial, Medicare, Medicaid, auto, workers comp), COB order (primary/secondary/tertiary), effective and termination dates, birthday rule applicability, and COB verification date. Supports COB inquiry workflows, subrogation identification, and Medicare Secondary Payer (MSP) compliance. Used by claims adjudication for COB calculation.'
}

Business justification: Consent records (electronic communication consent, 42 CFR Part 2, HIPAA authorizations) are frequently captured during enrollment transactions. Table enrollment.enrollment_eligibility_span {
  enrollment_eligibility_span_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the enrollment eligibility span record.']

  capitation_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.capitation_arrangement. Business justification: In capitation-based plans, each member eligibility span is attributed to a capitation arrangement driving PMPM payments. Capitation payment processing requires this link to calculate attributed_member']
  delegation_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.delegation_agreement. Business justification: Delegation agreements govern which entity performs UM, credentialing, or claims functions for enrolled members. An eligibility span subject to a delegated IPA or medical group requires this link for N']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.fee_schedule. Business justification: Claims adjudication for an enrolled member requires knowing which fee schedule governs allowed amounts for their eligibility span. Health plan operations teams and actuaries use this link to determine']
  group_division_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_division. Business justification: Large employers apply division‑specific eligibility rules; linking enables division‑level eligibility analytics.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group for group enrollments.']
  group_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_location. Business justification: Eligibility calculations depend on employer\'s primary location to apply network rules and state‑specific regulations.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the health plan associated with this eligibility span.']
  member_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.member_eligibility_span. Business justification: Eligibility reconciliation report requires linking each enrollment eligibility span to the member\'s eligibility span to compare periods and resolve discrepancies.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member to whom this eligibility span applies.']
  primary_care_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Needed to assign a primary care provider to each member’s coverage span for network compliance and care coordination reports.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network associated with the plan.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: A member\'s eligibility span is bound to a specific network tier (e.g., Tier 1 preferred, Tier 2 standard) that governs cost-sharing differentials (copay, coinsurance) applied during claims adjudicatio']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: benefit_year on enrollment_eligibility_span is a denormalized plan.year reference. The year_id FK enables accumulator reset scheduling (deductible_reset_date, moop_reset_date, oop_reset_date), MLR rep']
  benefit_designation varchar [note: 'Design of the benefit plan (e.g., HMO, PPO).']
  benefit_period_end date [note: 'End date of the benefit period (often same as termination_date).']
  benefit_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the benefit period (often same as effective_date).']
  coverage_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the covered individual (e.g., employee, spouse).']
  coverage_limit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable amount for a specific benefit within the span.']
  coverage_limit_units varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the coverage limit (e.g., visits, days).']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Type of coverage provided in this span (e.g., medical, dental).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the eligibility span record was first created.']
  deductible_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual deductible amount applicable for this span.']
  deductible_reset_date date [note: 'Date when the deductible accumulator resets (usually start of benefit year).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the eligibility span becomes active.']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Eligibility determination for the member during this span.']
  enrollment_eligibility_span_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the eligibility span.']
  enrollment_event_type varchar [note: 'Category of enrollment event that created or modified this span.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'System or channel through which the enrollment was received.']
  is_primary_coverage boolean [note: 'True if this span represents the member\'s primary coverage.']
  is_waiver_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates if a cost‑sharing waiver (e.g., HSA) is applied to this span.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the status field last changed.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line under which the enrollment is processed.']
  moop_reset_date date [note: 'Date when the MOOP threshold resets.']
  moop_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Combined deductible and OOP limit for this span.']
  oop_maximum decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum out‑of‑pocket expense the member can incur.']
  oop_reset_date date [note: 'Date when the OOP accumulator resets.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External plan identifier used in contracts and communications.']
  prior_coverage_end_date date [note: 'Termination date of the preceding coverage period, if any.']
  prior_coverage_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the member had coverage immediately before this span.']
  qualifying_life_event_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the life event that triggered a special enrollment.']
  retroactive_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the span was created or modified retroactively.']
  retroactive_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for a retroactive change to the eligibility span.']
  subscriber_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the covered individual to the subscriber.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the eligibility span ends; null if still active.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  utilization_reset_date date [note: 'Date when utilization counters (e.g., visit counts) reset.']
  waiver_type varchar [note: 'Type of waiver applied, if any.']

  Note: 'Core master record representing a member\'s continuous coverage period under a specific health plan, including benefit year boundaries, deductible/OOP accumulator reset dates, and MOOP thresholds. Captures effective date, termination date, coverage type, line of business (commercial, Medicare, Medicaid), plan code, subscriber relationship, eligibility status, and benefit period attributes. This is the authoritative SSOT for \'is this member covered?\' and \'what benefit period applies?\' at any point in time. One enrollment_transaction may produce one or more eligibility_spans. Sourced from Facets/QNXT enrollment module and CMS EDPS for government programs.'
}

Table enrollment.transaction {
  transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for transaction']
  capitation_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.capitation_arrangement. Business justification: Enrollment transactions (new enrollment, termination, plan change) trigger capitation payment adjustments. The capitation payment run must reference the enrollment transaction that caused the member c']
  enrollment_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the eligibility span generated from this transaction.']
  enrollment_open_enrollment_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.open_enrollment_period. Business justification: Each enrollment transaction occurs within a defined open enrollment period; linking transaction to period enables period‑based reporting and ensures the period is not a silo.']
  group_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_contact. Business justification: Required for audit of enrollment approvals: employer\'s designated contact must be recorded per ACA compliance reporting.']
  group_renewal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_renewal. Business justification: Financial reconciliation requires associating each enrollment transaction with the employer\'s renewal cycle.']
  member_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group or line of business to which the member belongs.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member associated with this enrollment transaction.']
  open_enrollment_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.open_enrollment_window. Business justification: Group market enrollment transactions processed during an employer OE window must reference that window for participation threshold monitoring, submission deadline compliance, and group enrollment audi']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Network enrollment reporting for quarterly compliance requires linking each transaction to the provider network.']
  qualifying_life_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.qualifying_life_event. Business justification: An enrollment transaction triggered by a Special Enrollment Period must reference the qualifying life event (QLE) that opened that SEP window. This links the transactional action back to its triggerin']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.plan_rate. Business justification: Required for premium audit reports that must trace each enrollment transaction to the exact rate applied for regulatory compliance.']
  risk_underwriting_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_underwriting_case. Business justification: Group enrollment transactions directly implement underwriting case decisions on rates and coverage terms. Operations and compliance teams require this link to reconcile enrollment transactions against']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustment (e.g., discount, surcharge) applied to the gross premium.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code describing why a financial or coverage adjustment was made.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual or system that approved the enrollment change.']
  audit_user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the audit user (e.g., admin, operator, system).']
  claims_reprocess_flag boolean [note: 'True if the enrollment change requires downstream claims to be re‑processed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the transaction with applicable regulations.']
  coverage_period_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the coverage is continuous, intermittent, or contains a gap.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment transaction record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values in this transaction.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the member\'s coverage under the new enrollment becomes effective.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date of the coverage period associated with this enrollment (may differ from termination_date).']
  enrollment_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for notes or comments entered by the processor.']
  enrollment_origin varchar [note: 'Channel through which the enrollment was initiated.']
  enrollment_transaction_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the enrollment transaction, used for tracking and reference.']
  enrollment_type varchar [note: 'Category of enrollment event: open enrollment, special enrollment period, automatic enrollment, special manual enrollment, or other.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the enrollment event occurred in the source system.']
  financial_impact_flag boolean [note: 'True when the enrollment change has a direct impact on premium billing or financial reporting.']
  grace_period_end_date date [note: 'Date when the applicable grace period expires.']
  gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total premium amount before any adjustments for the enrollment period.']
  health_plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the health plan (e.g., HMO, PPO, EPO, POS, HDHP).']
  is_grace_period boolean [note: 'True if the enrollment is being processed within a grace period after termination.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final premium amount after adjustments, representing the amount to be billed.']
  original_termination_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the prior termination transaction that is being reinstated.']
  prior_authorization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enrollment change triggers a prior authorization requirement.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing stage of the transaction within the enrollment workflow.']
  reactivation_date date [note: 'Effective date of coverage reactivation for reinstatement events.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction must be reported to a regulatory body (e.g., CMS, NAIC).']
  retroactive_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the transaction represents a retroactive adjustment to prior coverage dates.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the enrollment transaction record.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which coverage ends for a disenrollment or termination event (nullable if not applicable).']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Standardized reason why the enrollment was terminated.']
  transaction_source varchar [note: 'Source of the enrollment transaction record, e.g., EDI 834 feed, web portal, batch load, or API.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the enrollment transaction record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every enrollment action for a member — initial enrollment, re-enrollment, plan change, disenrollment (voluntary/involuntary termination), reinstatement, and retroactive adjustment. Tracks enrollment source, enrollment period type (open enrollment, SEP, auto-enrollment), effective date, submitted date, processing status, and transaction-type-specific attributes: termination reason/last day of coverage/grace period status for disenrollments; original termination reference/reinstatement effective date/approving authority for reinstatements; adjusted dates/financial impact flag/claims reprocessing trigger for retro adjustments. One enrollment_transaction may produce one or more eligibility_spans. Sourced from Facets/QNXT and 834 EDI transactions via Availity/Change Healthcare.'
}

Table enrollment.event {
  event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each enrollment event record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group linked to the enrollment, if applicable.']
  group_plan_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_plan_offering. Business justification: Event tracking reports must reference the exact plan offering the change relates to for employer‑level reporting.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member associated with the enrollment event.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Network change audit log records which network triggered an enrollment event for regulatory audit.']
  risk_underwriting_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_underwriting_case. Business justification: Event‑driven underwriting decisions are recorded; linking supports the \'Underwriting Decision Audit\' report.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the enrollment to which this event belongs.']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the party that triggered the event.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the new enrollment status becomes effective.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text narrative providing additional context for the event.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'System or interface that generated the event record.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the enrollment event occurred in the source system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of the state transition (e.g., submitted, approved).']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event was entered manually (True) or generated automatically (False).']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Enrollment status after the event was applied.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'Enrollment status before the event was applied.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining why the event was generated (e.g., QLE‑DIVORCE, SYSTEM‑ERROR).']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first persisted in the data lake.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the event record.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the enrollment was terminated, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Granular audit log of every state transition in the enrollment lifecycle — submitted, pended, approved, rejected, effectuated, terminated, reinstated, retroactively adjusted. Each event captures the triggering action, actor (member, employer, CMS, system), timestamp, reason code, and before/after state. Supports regulatory audit trails required by CMS, state DOI, and HIPAA administrative simplification. Distinct from enrollment_transaction (which is the business action) — this is the immutable event history of that action\'s processing lifecycle.'
}

Table enrollment.qualifying_life_event {
  qualifying_life_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the qualifying life event record.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member who reported the qualifying life event.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: SEP processing is directly governed by ACA/CMS regulatory obligations defining valid SEP categories, 60-day election windows, and documentation requirements. Compliance teams audit QLE processing agai']
  appeal_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any appeal filed against a denial.']
  cms_sep_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the CMS SEP eligibility determination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualifying life event record was first created in the system.']
  denial_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation why the SEP request was denied, if applicable.']
  documentation_type varchar [note: 'Type of supporting document submitted for the event.']
  event_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the qualifying life event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the life event that triggers a special enrollment period.']
  qualifying_life_event_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the qualifying life event record.']
  sep_category_code varchar [note: 'CMS‑defined code that classifies the SEP category for the qualifying life event.']
  sep_window_end date [note: 'Last date the special enrollment period (SEP) is open for this event.']
  sep_window_start date [note: 'First date the special enrollment period (SEP) is open for this event.']
  sep_window_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the SEP window based on dates and actions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the qualifying life event record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date the verification decision was recorded.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the CMS SEP verification process.']

  Note: 'Master record of a qualifying life event (QLE) that triggers a special enrollment period (SEP), including the full SEP verification lifecycle. Captures event type (marriage, birth, loss of coverage, relocation, divorce, adoption), event date, SEP window open/close dates, CMS SEP category codes, submitted documentation type and artifacts (birth certificate, marriage license, loss of coverage letter), verification status, verifier ID, verification date, CMS SEP verification outcome, pend/denial reason, and appeal reference. This is the authoritative SSOT for both QLE registration and SEP verification — no separate verification record exists. Required for ACA compliance, CMS SEP eligibility determinations, and SEP integrity program compliance. Sourced from Facets/QNXT pend workflow and member portal document uploads.'
}

  period_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the enrollment window, e.g., "2025 Individual Marketplace OE".']

  start_date date [note: 'First calendar day on which members may submit enrollment applications.']

  volume_target_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the actual enrollment volume met or exceeded the target.']

  Note: 'Master record defining each open enrollment window — annual employer group OE, ACA marketplace OE, Medicare Annual Enrollment Period (AEP), and Medicaid continuous enrollment periods. Captures period start/end dates, eligible population segment, LOB, exchange type (SHOP, individual marketplace, off-exchange), and enrollment volume targets. Used to govern when enrollment_records are accepted without a QLE. Sourced from CMS plan year calendars and employer group contracts.'
}

Table enrollment.eligibility_verification {

  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan under which the member is enrolled.']

  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Required for automated provider credential checks during eligibility verification; the process matches providers to member eligibility inquiries.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: HIPAA 270/271 eligibility responses return the member\'s active network tier so providers know which cost-sharing schedule applies at point of care. This link is essential for real-time benefit verific']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the source system for the eligibility transaction.']
  authorization_number varchar [note: 'Authorization number returned when prior authorization is required.']
  authorization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether prior authorization is required for the service.']

  benefit_limit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount allowed for the queried benefit.']
  benefit_remaining decimal(10,2) [note: 'Remaining dollar amount of the benefit available.']
  benefit_used decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the benefit already utilized in the current period.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Level of coverage returned (full, partial, or none).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the eligibility record was first persisted in the data lake.']
  deductible_remaining decimal(10,2) [note: 'Member\'s remaining deductible balance for the benefit period.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'ICD diagnosis code associated with the request, if applicable.']

  inquiry_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the source system (e.g., Availity) for the eligibility request.']
  inquiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the eligibility request was sent to the payer.']
  member_identifier_type varchar [note: 'Type of identifier supplied for the member (SSN, MRN, or internal member ID).']
  oop_remaining decimal(10,2) [note: 'Remaining out‑of‑pocket amount before maximum OOP is reached.']
  response_message varchar [note: 'Free‑form message returned by the payer providing additional context.']
  response_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elapsed time between request and response, measured in seconds.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the eligibility response was received.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'CPT code of the service/procedure for which eligibility is being checked.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'EDI gateway or clearinghouse that supplied the eligibility response.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the eligibility record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of real-time 270/271 eligibility verification requests and responses. Captures requesting provider NPI, member identifier used, inquiry date/time, response status, coverage details returned, deductible/OOP balances returned, and response time SLA. Supports provider-facing eligibility verification at point of care. Sourced from Availity/Change Healthcare 270/271 gateway and Facets real-time eligibility API.'
}

Members designate their Primary Care Provider when electing coverage. This drives care coordination, network ']
  prior_plan_election_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous election record when this election is a change or reinstatement.']
  qualifying_life_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.qualifying_life_event. Business justification: When a plan election is made during a Special Enrollment Period, it is triggered by a qualifying life event. Linking plan_election to qualifying_life_event captures the SEP trigger for the election, e']
  risk_underwriting_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_underwriting_case. Business justification: Group plan elections are executed based on underwriting case decisions that set rates and risk classification. Underwriters and actuaries must trace which underwriting case authorized a plan election\'']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: During open enrollment or SEP, members elect a plan at a specific network tier (tiered HMO/PPO). The plan election must reference the tier to calculate premium contributions, cost-sharing obligations,']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.transaction. Business justification: A plan election generates an enrollment transaction that processes the election through the enrollment lifecycle. Linking plan_election to transaction connects the plan selection record to its process']
  vbc_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vbc_contract. Business justification: In ACO and VBC programs, member attribution flows from plan election to a VBC contract for performance period measurement. Health plans use this link to attribute members to VBC arrangements and calcu']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: Plan elections are scoped to a plan year for ACA annual reporting, employer W-2 reporting, and premium reconciliation. Enrollment reporting systems require a direct plan year reference on elections to']
  cobra_eligibility_end_date date [note: 'Date when COBRA eligibility expires.']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'Level of coverage elected (e.g., employee only, employee + spouse, employee + children, family).']
  dental_rider_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a dental coverage rider was elected.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the elected coverage becomes effective.']
  election_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the election by the enrollment system.']
  election_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the election event (new enrollment, change, termination, reinstatement).']
  enrollment_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of enrollment event that triggered the election.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the election was submitted.']
  fsa_election_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a Flexible Spending Account was elected.']
  hra_election_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a Health Reimbursement Arrangement was elected.']
  hsa_election_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a Health Savings Account was elected.']
  is_cobra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the election is eligible for COBRA continuation coverage.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or special instructions related to the election.']
  plan_election_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the election record.']
  premium_contribution_employee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the premium paid by the employee.']
  premium_contribution_employer decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the premium paid by the employer.']
  premium_frequency varchar [note: 'Billing frequency for the premium (e.g., monthly).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the election record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the election record.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the elected coverage ends, if applicable.']
  total_premium decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total premium amount for the elected coverage (employer + employee).']
  vision_rider_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a vision coverage rider was elected.']

  Note: 'Master record of a subscriber\'s specific plan selection, coverage tier, and covered member roster during an enrollment or plan change event. Captures elected plan ID, coverage tier (employee-only, employee+spouse, employee+children, family), effective date, prior plan reference, premium contribution split (employer vs. employee), HSA/HRA/FSA election flag, dental/vision rider elections, and the roster of covered individuals with their relationship codes, age-out dates, and COBRA continuation eligibility. References member domain for person identity — this product owns the enrollment-specific election and roster, not the person demographics. Represents the member\'s choice artifact — distinct from eligibility_span (the resulting coverage). Sourced from Facets/QNXT benefits election module.'
}

Interfaces with state Medicaid agency eligibility systems and CMS EDPS. Sourced from state 834 files and Facets Medicaid module.'
}
