Interfaces with CMS FFM enrollment data feeds. Critical for ACA compliance, APTC reconciliation with IRS, and annual 1095-A generation. Sourced from Availity/Change Healthcare exchange gateway and CMS 834 files.'
}

Table plan.health_plan {
  health_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the health plan record.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider network associated with the plan.']
  benefit_design_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the benefit design configuration.']
  cms_plan_code varchar [note: 'CMS plan identifier used for regulatory reporting.']
  coinsurance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of allowed amount the member pays after deductible is met.']
  copay_primary_care decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed copayment amount for a primary care office visit.']
  copay_specialist decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed copayment amount for a specialist office visit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan record was first created in the system.']
  deductible_family decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual amount a family must pay before cost‑sharing begins.']
  deductible_individual decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual amount an individual member must pay before cost‑sharing begins.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the plan becomes binding for members.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the care program coverage ends for the health plan']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the care program becomes effective for the health plan']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Last date members may enroll in the plan for the plan year.']
  enrollment_start_date date [note: 'First date members may enroll in the plan for the plan year.']
  hcc_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated HCC score for the plan population used in risk adjustment.']
  hios_plan_code varchar [note: 'CMS‑assigned HIOS identifier for the plan.']
  is_exempt_from_mlr boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is exempt from Medical Loss Ratio reporting requirements.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date the plan design was last reviewed for compliance or pricing updates.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Broad business segment the plan belongs to, such as commercial or government.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Target market for the plan: individual, small group, large group, Medicare, or Medicaid.']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Tier classification of the network (e.g., Tier1, Tier2).']
  out_of_pocket_max_family decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum amount a family pays in a year before the plan pays 100% of covered services.']
  out_of_pocket_max_individual decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum amount an individual pays in a year before the plan pays 100% of covered services.']
  plan_aca_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the plan meets all Affordable Care Act regulatory requirements.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category indicating commercial, government, or special market plans.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used in core administration platforms to reference the plan.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the plan\'s benefits and target audience.']
  plan_marketplace_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the plan can be sold on the ACA Health Insurance Marketplace.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the health plan as marketed to members.']
  plan_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region (e.g., Northeast, Midwest) for reporting and network adequacy.']
  plan_state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter US state abbreviation where the plan is offered.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan product (e.g., HMO, PPO, EPO, POS, HDHP, QHP, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid).']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the plan design is effective.']
  premium_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base premium amount charged to the member or group for the plan year.']
  premium_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the premium amount.']
  premium_frequency varchar [note: 'Billing frequency for the premium (monthly, quarterly, annually).']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory category of the plan (e.g., ACA, Medicare, Medicaid).']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Factor used for risk‑adjusted payments under CMS risk adjustment models.']
  sbc_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic Summary of Benefits and Coverage document for the plan.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the plan is terminated or expires; null if open‑ended.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the plan record.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all health plan products offered by the insurer — HMO, PPO, EPO, POS, HDHP, QHP, Medicare Advantage, and Medicaid managed care plans. Serves as the SSOT for plan identity, plan type classification, line of business (LOB), market segment (individual, small group, large group, Medicare, Medicaid), regulatory classification, CMS plan ID, HIOS plan ID, plan-year lifecycle, and provider network configuration (network ID, tiered network designations, access type, out-of-network coverage rules, network adequacy standards). Foundational entity for all benefit configuration, enrollment, and claims adjudication in Facets/QNXT.'
}

Table plan.benefit_package {
  benefit_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the benefit package record.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: A benefit package is defined for a specific health plan. Adding health_plan_id to benefit_package creates the necessary parent relationship and prevents the package from being a silo.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Benefit packages are designed around specific provider networks — a narrow-network package has different cost-sharing than a broad-network package. Benefit design reporting and member enrollment requi']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: Benefit packages define cost-sharing (copay_primary_care, copay_specialist) that varies by network tier. Member-facing SBC documents and EOB generation require linking a benefit package to its applica']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: A benefit package is defined for a specific plan year. The year table is the authoritative plan year configuration record (start/end dates, open enrollment windows, ACA compliance indicators). Linking']
  actuarial_value_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total average costs the plan is expected to cover, expressed as a percent.']
  benefit_package_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benefit package.']
  coinsurance_inpatient decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of allowed amount the member pays for inpatient services after deductible.']
  coinsurance_outpatient decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of allowed amount the member pays for outpatient services after deductible.']
  copay_primary_care decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar copay for a primary care office visit.']
  copay_specialist decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar copay for a specialist office visit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the benefit package record was first created in the system.']
  deductible_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the deductible is embedded (per member) or aggregate (family).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the benefit package expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the benefit package becomes effective.']
  family_deductible_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount a family must pay before cost‑sharing begins for the family unit.']
  generic_substitution_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether generic substitution is mandatory when a generic equivalent is available.']
  individual_deductible_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount an individual must pay before cost‑sharing begins.']
  mail_order_copay_brand decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fixed copay for brand‑name drugs dispensed via mail‑order.']
  mail_order_copay_generic decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fixed copay for generic drugs dispensed via mail‑order.']
  metal_tier varchar [note: 'ACA metal tier designation indicating the level of coverage.']
  out_of_pocket_max_family decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum amount a family will pay in a plan year for covered services.']
  out_of_pocket_max_individual decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum amount an individual will pay in a plan year for covered services.']
  package_code varchar [note: 'External business code or number used to identify the benefit package in plan documents and communications.']
  package_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the benefit package (e.g., "Silver 2024 PPO").']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the health plan product type.']
  prior_auth_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a prior authorization is required for services covered under this package.']
  retail_copay_brand decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fixed copay for brand‑name drugs dispensed at retail pharmacies.']
  retail_copay_generic decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fixed copay for generic drugs dispensed at retail pharmacies.']
  specialty_copay decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fixed copay for specialty pharmacy drugs.']
  specialty_drug_management_program varchar [note: 'Name or description of the management program applied to specialty drugs (e.g., step therapy, limited distribution).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the benefit package record.']

  Note: 'Defines the structured benefit package associated with a health plan and plan year, grouping all covered services into a coherent benefit design. Captures package name, effective dates, plan-year, benefit tier (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum for ACA QHPs), actuarial value percentage, metal tier classification, embedded vs. aggregate deductible design, and full pharmacy benefit configuration — PBM vendor, formulary ID, retail vs. mail-order copay differentials, specialty pharmacy requirements, 90-day supply rules, mandatory generic substitution flags, specialty drug management programs, and retail/mail-order/specialty pharmacy network designations. One health plan may have multiple benefit packages across plan years. Sourced from benefits administration and PBM integration (RxClaim/Argus).'
}

Table plan.benefit {
  benefit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each benefit record.']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: A benefit is a granular covered service defined within a benefit package. benefit_package groups all covered benefits for a health plan and plan year. Adding benefit_package_id to benefit establishes ']
  drug_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.drug_master. Business justification: Benefit records for drug-specific benefits (specialty, preventive) must reference drug_master for EHB classification, regulatory filings, and SBC generation. drug_ndc on benefit is a denormalized drug']
  formulary_drug_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.formulary_drug_tier. Business justification: A drug benefit record must reference the specific formulary_drug_tier governing its cost-sharing rules (copay, coinsurance, PA requirements). This supports adjudication accuracy and SBC generation. fo']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan to which this benefit belongs.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Individual benefits must comply with specific regulatory mandates: ACA EHB requirements, state mandated benefit laws, MHPAEA mental health parity. Linking each benefit to its governing regulatory obli']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: Benefits in health insurance are network-tier-specific — preventive services may be covered at 100% for Tier 1 providers but subject to cost-sharing for Tier 2. Benefit adjudication and SBC disclosure']
  accumulation_rule varchar [note: 'Rule governing how limits accumulate across periods.']
  authorization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the benefit requires prior authorization.']
  authorization_type varchar [note: 'Timing of the required authorization relative to service delivery.']
  benefit_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the benefit type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical|dental|vision|pharmacy|behavioral|dme|snf|home_health|wellness|ancillary — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  benefit_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used in core administration platforms to reference the benefit.']
  benefit_group varchar [note: 'Member group to which the benefit applies.']
  benefit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the benefit (e.g., "Office Visit", "Generic Drug Tier 1").']
  benefit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benefit record.']
  cost_sharing_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount for copay or deductible (if applicable).']
  cost_sharing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which cost‑sharing applies.']
  cost_sharing_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage for coinsurance (e.g., 20.00 for 20%).']
  cost_sharing_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the member shares cost (deductible, copay, coinsurance, or none).']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the benefit applies to in‑network, out‑of‑network, or both provider sets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the benefit record was first created in the system.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'Diagnosis code that may trigger the benefit or exclusion.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit ceases to be active; null for open‑ended benefits.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit becomes active for the plan year.']
  ehb_classification varchar [note: 'Flag indicating if the benefit satisfies ACA Essential Health Benefit requirements.']
  exclusion_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific excluded service (e.g., CPT, HCPCS, NDC).']
  exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative reason for the exclusion (e.g., "experimental", "non‑covered diagnosis").']
  exclusion_type varchar [note: 'Category of service that is excluded from coverage.']
  is_exempt boolean [note: 'True if the benefit is exempt from certain regulations or cost‑sharing.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'True if the benefit is required by law or contract.']
  limit_period varchar [note: 'Timeframe over which the limit applies.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Type of utilization limit (e.g., number of visits, days, dollar amount).']
  limit_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Numeric value associated with the limit type.']
  moop_max_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum out‑of‑pocket expense for the entire plan year, including deductibles and copays.']
  oop_max_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum out‑of‑pocket expense a member can incur in a benefit year.']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year to which the benefit configuration applies.']
  preventive_service_flag boolean [note: 'True if the benefit is classified as a preventive service under ACA.']
  prior_auth_review_level varchar [note: 'Level of review required for the authorization (e.g., provider, clinical, pharmacy).']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory category governing the benefit (ACA Essential Health Benefit, state‑specific, etc.).']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Procedure or service code associated with the benefit.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., Facets, QNXT, Cactus).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the benefit record.']
  wellness_mandate_flag boolean [note: 'True if the benefit is mandated by wellness legislation or employer policy.']

  Note: 'Granular catalog of individual covered benefits within a benefit package — medical, dental, vision, behavioral health, pharmacy, DME, SNF, home health, preventive, wellness, and ancillary services. Each benefit record captures the benefit category, CPT/HCPCS service category, coverage type (in-network, out-of-network), and serves as the anchor for child-level structures: benefit limits (visit limits, day limits, dollar limits as child arrays), exclusions and non-covered service rules (as child arrays), ACA Essential Health Benefit (EHB) classification, wellness/preventive mandate flags, and prior authorization requirement indicators. Directly drives claims adjudication rules in Facets/QNXT. Sub-entities modeled as child arrays: benefit_limit (limit_type, limit_value, limit_period, accumulation_rule), benefit_exclusion (exclusion_type, exclusion_code, exclusion_reason), and benefit_authorization_flag (auth_required, auth_type, review_level).'
}

Table plan.cost_share_rule {
  cost_share_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cost share rule.']
  benefit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific benefit (e.g., office visit) that the rule applies to.']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the benefit package (group of benefits) containing the rule.']
  formulary_drug_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.formulary_drug_tier. Business justification: Cost share rules with applies_to_drug=true must reference the specific formulary_drug_tier they govern. Pharmacy adjudication systems use this link to apply the correct member cost share (copay/coinsu']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan to which the rule belongs.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: Cost-share rules in health insurance are explicitly differentiated by network tier (Tier 1 preferred, Tier 2 non-preferred, out-of-network). Claims adjudication systems look up the cost_share_rule for']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: Cost-sharing rules (deductibles, copays, coinsurance, OOP maxes) are plan-year specific. The year table is the authoritative plan year configuration. Adding year_id to cost_share_rule ties each cost-s']
  accumulator_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Threshold amount that triggers a change in cost‑share (e.g., after $1,000 of spend).']
  accumulator_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the accumulator threshold.']
  after_deductible boolean [note: 'True if the cost‑share applies after the deductible is satisfied.']
  applies_to_ancillary boolean [note: 'True if the rule applies to ancillary services (lab, imaging).']
  applies_to_drug boolean [note: 'True if the rule applies to prescription drug services.']
  applies_to_procedure boolean [note: 'True if the rule applies to medical procedures (CPT/HCPCS).']
  applies_to_service_category varchar [note: 'Service categories to which the rule applies.']
  coinsurance_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of allowed amount the member pays after deductible (in‑network).']
  coinsurance_rate_out_of_network decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of allowed amount the member pays after deductible (out‑of‑network).']
  copay_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar copay applied to a service when in‑network.']
  copay_amount_out_of_network decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar copay applied to a service when out‑of‑network.']
  cost_share_category varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of the rule relative to deductible and out‑of‑pocket phases.']
  cost_share_rule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the rule\'s purpose and application.']
  cost_share_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created.']
  deductible_aggregate_flag boolean [note: 'True if the deductible aggregates across multiple services or members.']
  deductible_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the deductible that must be satisfied before cost‑share applies.']
  deductible_embedded_flag boolean [note: 'True if the deductible is embedded within the cost‑share rule (e.g., for HDHPs).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the rule expires (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes effective.']
  hsa_compatible boolean [note: 'True if the rule is compatible with a Health Savings Account (HDHP).']
  is_default_rule boolean [note: 'True if this rule is the default for its benefit when no other rule matches.']
  max_benefit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount payable under this rule per service occurrence.']
  max_benefit_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units (e.g., visits) payable under this rule per benefit period.']
  member_tier varchar [note: 'Member classification for which the rule applies (individual, family, or group).']
  network_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the rule applies to in‑network, out‑of‑network, or both provider networks.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  out_of_pocket_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount an individual member can pay out of pocket in a plan year.']
  out_of_pocket_max_family decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount a family can pay out of pocket in a plan year.']
  prior_to_deductible boolean [note: 'True if the cost‑share applies before the deductible is satisfied.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the rule (e.g., ACA, CMS).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the rule in downstream systems.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the cost‑share rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of cost‑share rule (e.g., deductible, copay, coinsurance).']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rule.']

  Note: 'Defines cost-sharing parameters for a specific benefit within a benefit package — deductibles, copays, coinsurance rates, out-of-pocket maximums (MOOP), and accumulator threshold definitions. Captures in-network vs. out-of-network differentials, individual vs. family tier logic, embedded vs. aggregate deductible flags, HSA-compatibility indicators for HDHP plans, cost-share tier (preventive exempt, before-deductible, after-deductible), and whether cost share applies before or after deductible satisfaction. Authoritative source for member cost-sharing design used in EOB generation and claims adjudication configuration.'
}

Table plan.year {
  year_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each plan year configuration record.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the master health plan that this year configuration belongs to.']
  aca_compliance_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the plan year meets Affordable Care Act reporting requirements.']
  accumulator_reset_date date [note: 'Date on which annual benefit accumulators (e.g., deductibles) are reset.']
  cms_plan_submission_deadline date [note: 'Final date to submit plan year details to Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan year record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency for premium amounts.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the plan year becomes legally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the plan year ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended plans).']
  end_date date [note: 'Last day of the plan year coverage period.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after premium due date before coverage is terminated.']
  open_enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the open enrollment window for this plan year.']
  open_enrollment_start_date date [note: 'First day members may enroll or make changes for this plan year.']
  plan_year_budget_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for the plan year (used for internal financial planning).']
  plan_year_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the plan year (e.g., FY2024-01).']
  plan_year_coverage_type varchar [note: 'Design of the health plan offering for the year.']
  plan_year_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the plan year configuration.']
  plan_year_market_segment varchar [note: 'Target market segment for the plan year (e.g., individual, group, government).']
  plan_year_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments entered by administrators.']
  plan_year_premium_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base premium rate applied to members for this plan year.']
  plan_year_regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory category governing the plan year.']
  plan_year_state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state abbreviation where the plan year is offered.']
  plan_year_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan year calendar basis: calendar year, fiscal year, or custom.']
  premium_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the premium amount for the plan year takes effect.']
  regulatory_filing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory filing is required for this plan year.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for changes made within a version.']
  run_out_period_days varchar [note: 'Days after plan year end during which claims can still be submitted.']
  special_enrollment_period_rules varchar [note: 'Textual rules governing qualifying life events and special enrollment periods.']
  start_date date [note: 'First day of the plan year coverage period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the plan year record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for the plan year configuration.']
  year_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan year configuration.']

  Note: 'Defines the plan year configuration for each health plan — plan year start and end dates, open enrollment window, special enrollment period rules, grace period duration, run-out period, and plan year type (calendar year vs. non-calendar year). Governs accumulator reset logic, benefit renewal, and premium rate effective dates. Supports ACA plan year compliance and CMS reporting requirements.'
}

Table plan.rate {
  rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each plan rate record.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan to which this rate applies.']
  risk_underwriting_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_underwriting_case. Business justification: Premium rates are the direct output of underwriting case decisions. Actuaries and underwriters need to trace each rate back to the underwriting case that produced it for regulatory filings, rate justi']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: Premium rates are plan-year specific. The rate table currently stores plan_year as a raw INT (e.g., 2024), which is a denormalized reference to the year table. Adding year_id as a proper FK to the yea']
  age_rated_premium decimal(12,4) [note: 'Premium amount after applying the age factor to the base rate.']
  base_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base premium amount before age or family adjustments, expressed in the plan currency.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why the rate was created or modified.']
  cost_sharing_rule_code varchar [note: 'Code that identifies the cost‑sharing rule (deductible, copay, coinsurance) associated with the plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rate record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the premium amounts (e.g., USD).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the rate becomes effective for enrollment.']
  family_tier varchar [note: 'Category of coverage for the enrollee: employee only, employee + spouse, employee + child, or family.']
  family_tier_premium decimal(12,4) [note: 'Premium amount for the specified family tier.']
  filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for the filing submitted to the Department of Insurance.']
  is_tobacco_surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates if a tobacco surcharge is applied to this rate.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of the market the rate applies to: individual, small group, or large group.']
  max_age varchar [note: 'Inclusive upper bound of the age band for this rate.']
  min_age varchar [note: 'Inclusive lower bound of the age band for this rate.']
  plan_designation varchar [note: 'Type of health plan product (e.g., HMO, PPO).']
  premium_type varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the premium is billed.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'External code used by actuarial systems to identify the rate.']
  rate_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the rate definition.']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate.']
  rating_area_code varchar [note: 'Three‑character code representing the geographic rating area defined by CMS.']
  regulatory_filing_type varchar [note: 'Regulatory body for which the rate filing is submitted.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Origin of the rate data (e.g., actuarial model, manual entry, external system).']
  surcharge_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Monetary amount added to the premium for tobacco users.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the rate is no longer valid; null if open‑ended.']
  tobacco_use_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the member uses tobacco (yes) or not (no).']
  underwriting_class_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the underwriting class applied to the rate.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rate record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the rate definition (e.g., v1, v2).']

  Note: 'Premium rate table for each health plan by rating factors — age band, geographic rating area, tobacco use, plan year, and market segment (individual, small group, large group). Captures the base rate, age-rated premium, family tier premium (employee only, employee + spouse, employee + child, family), rating area code, effective and termination dates, and rate filing reference. Supports premium billing, quoting, and DOI rate filing compliance. Sourced from actuarial rate-setting and pricing systems.'
}

  network_service_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_service_area. Business justification: CMS and state DOI regulatory filings require mapping plan service areas to network service areas for network adequacy certification. A domain expert doing QHP or Medicare Advantage submissions would e']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: CMS service area approval requirements for Medicare Advantage and state DOI service area filing obligations are specific regulatory obligations governing service area definitions. Linking plan_service']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: A plan service area authorization is plan-year specific — geographic service areas are approved and filed with regulators on an annual basis. The plan_service_area table currently stores plan_year as ']
  county varchar [note: 'County name within the state for the service area.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Type of health plan coverage associated with this service area.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service area record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the service area ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the service area becomes effective for enrollment.']
  enrollment_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of members that can be enrolled in this service area.']
  exchange_market varchar [note: 'Name of the ACA exchange market (if applicable) where the plan is offered.']
  exchange_market_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the exchange market.']
  geographic_region_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the broader geographic region for the service area.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service area is exclusive to this plan (True) or shared (False).']
  is_federal_funded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service area receives federal funding.']
  is_medicaid_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the service area is eligible for Medicaid plans.']
  is_medicare_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the service area is eligible for Medicare Advantage plans.']
  is_regulatory_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service area meets all regulatory requirements.']
  is_state_funded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service area receives state funding.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service area record.']
  network_type varchar [note: 'Specifies if the service area applies to in‑network or out‑of‑network coverage.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes regarding the service area.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the plan associated with the service area.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approval for the service area.']

  service_area_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by CMS for the service area.']
  service_area_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the geographic service area.']
  service_area_type varchar [note: 'Geographic granularity of the service area.']
  state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter US state abbreviation where the service area is located.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the service area record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the service area record for change tracking.']
  zip_codes_excluded varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ZIP codes excluded from the service area.']
  zip_codes_included varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ZIP codes included in the service area.']

  Note: 'Defines the geographic service area in which a health plan is authorized to enroll members and provide coverage. Captures the service area name, state, county, zip code inclusions/exclusions, CMS service area ID, effective dates, and regulatory approval status. Required for CMS QHP submissions, Medicare Advantage service area filings, and state DOI market conduct compliance. Governs member eligibility for plan enrollment based on residence.'
}

Table plan.hsa_hra_config {
  hsa_hra_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the HSA/HRA configuration record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group. Business justification: Employers negotiate group-specific HSA/HRA employer contribution amounts and seed funding. Benefits administrators configure HSA/HRA parameters per employer group. The employer_contribution_amount on ']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan to which this HSA/HRA configuration applies.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: HSA/HRA configurations are directly governed by IRS IRC 223 HDHP requirements and ACA preventive care mandates, each a specific regulatory obligation. Linking HSA/HRA config to its governing obligatio']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: HSA/HRA configurations are plan-year specific — IRS contribution limits, catch-up contribution thresholds, and rollover rules change annually. Linking hsa_hra_config to the year table ties each config']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of tax‑advantaged health account covered by this configuration.']
  catch_up_contribution_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional contribution amount allowed for eligible participants.']
  catch_up_contribution_eligible boolean [note: 'True if participants age 55 or older may make catch‑up contributions.']
  contribution_frequency varchar [note: 'How often contributions are made to the account.']
  contribution_limit_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary amount defining the contribution ceiling based on the limit type.']
  contribution_limit_type varchar [note: 'Whether the contribution limit is expressed per year or per month.']
  contribution_method varchar [note: 'Tax treatment of contributions (pre‑tax, post‑tax, or after‑tax).']
  contribution_source varchar [note: 'Origin of contributions to the account.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the configuration record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this configuration ceases to be effective (null if ongoing).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this configuration becomes effective for the plan.']
  eligibility_hdpp boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the associated plan qualifies as a High‑Deductible Health Plan for HSA eligibility (true/false).']
  employee_contribution_limit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount an employee may contribute per plan year.']
  employer_contribution_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount the employer contributes to the account each contribution period.']
  grace_period_option boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a grace period is offered for eligible expenses after plan year end.']
  hsa_hra_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing special rules or notes for this configuration.']
  hsa_hra_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the configuration record.']
  irs_minimum_deductible decimal(10,2) [note: 'IRS‑mandated minimum deductible amount (in plan currency) required for HSA eligibility.']
  irs_out_of_pocket_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'IRS‑mandated maximum out‑of‑pocket amount (in plan currency) for HSA eligibility.']
  plan_year_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the plan year for this configuration (nullable for open‑ended).']
  plan_year_start_date date [note: 'First day of the plan year to which this configuration applies.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True when the configuration meets all IRS Section 223 requirements for HSA eligibility.']
  rollover_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused balances may be rolled over to the next plan year.']
  rollover_limit_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount that may be rolled over (if limited).']
  trustee_custodian_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution or custodian that holds the HSA/HRA assets.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the configuration record.']
  use_it_or_lose_it_flag boolean [note: 'True if the account follows a use‑it‑or‑lose‑it rule (applicable to FSA).']

  Note: 'Configuration record for tax-advantaged health accounts associated with a health plan — HSA (Health Savings Account), HRA (Health Reimbursement Arrangement), FSA (Flexible Spending Account), and LPFSA (Limited Purpose FSA). Captures IRS minimum deductible threshold, IRS out-of-pocket maximum threshold (per IRS Revenue Procedure annual updates), employer contribution amount, employee contribution limit, catch-up contribution eligibility (age 55+), rollover rules (HSA unlimited, HRA employer-defined, FSA $610 carryover limit), use-it-or-lose-it flag, grace period option, plan year alignment, and HSA trustee/custodian information. Ensures HDHP plans meet IRS Section 223 HSA eligibility requirements and supports benefits administration integration with HSA custodians (Optum Bank, Fidelity, HealthEquity).'
}

Table plan.rx_benefit_config {
  rx_benefit_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the pharmacy benefit configuration record.']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: Pharmacy benefit configuration is a component of the overall benefit package design. A benefit package defines the full benefit structure including pharmacy benefits, and rx_benefit_config provides th']
  formulary_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the drug formulary associated with this configuration.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan to which this pharmacy benefit configuration applies.']
  pbm_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pbm_contract. Business justification: rx_benefit_config defines how pharmacy benefits are administered under a specific PBM arrangement. Linking directly to pbm_contract enables PBM performance audits, rebate reconciliation, and contract ']
  coinsurance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Coinsurance rate expressed as a percentage of the allowed amount.']
  cost_sharing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate member cost‑sharing (copay, coinsurance, or percentage).']
  coverage_limit_per_prescription decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum reimbursable amount for a single prescription fill.']
  coverage_limit_per_year decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual dollar limit on pharmacy benefit coverage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the data lake.']
  deductible_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the pharmacy deductible.']
  deductible_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan deductible applies to pharmacy benefits.']
  drug_category_exclusions varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of drug categories that are excluded from coverage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this pharmacy benefit configuration expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this pharmacy benefit configuration becomes effective.']
  formulary_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the formulary to support change tracking and audits.']
  is_biologic_preferred boolean [note: 'True if biologic drugs are preferred within the formulary.']
  is_biosimilar_preferred boolean [note: 'True if biosimilar drugs are preferred over reference biologics.']
  is_exempt_from_mlr boolean [note: 'True if the plan is exempt from MLR reporting requirements for this pharmacy benefit.']
  is_specialty_drug_excluded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specialty drugs are excluded from coverage.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the configuration was last reviewed for compliance or policy changes.']
  mail_order_network_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the mail‑order pharmacy network.']
  max_coverage_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper limit on total pharmacy benefit coverage per benefit period.']
  ninety_day_supply_allowed boolean [note: 'True if a 90‑day supply of medication is permitted under this benefit configuration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special instructions.']
  out_of_pocket_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum out‑of‑pocket expense a member can incur for pharmacy benefits.']
  prior_auth_exemption_drugs varchar [note: 'List of NDCs for which prior authorization is waived.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory category (e.g., Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, QHP) applicable to this benefit configuration.']
  retail_network_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the retail pharmacy network (e.g., in‑network or out‑of‑network).']
  rx_benefit_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benefit configuration.']
  specialty_pharmacy_network varchar [note: 'Name or code of the specialty pharmacy network used for high‑cost drugs.']
  step_therapy_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether step‑therapy (sequential drug use) is required for covered drugs.']
  therapeutic_class_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of distinct therapeutic classes allowed per member per year.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management.']

  Note: 'Pharmacy benefit configuration record linking a health plan to its PBM formulary and defining pharmacy-specific cost-sharing rules — retail vs. mail-order copay differentials, specialty pharmacy requirements, 90-day supply rules, mandatory generic substitution flags, and specialty drug management programs. Captures the PBM vendor, formulary ID, retail network type, mail-order network, specialty pharmacy network, and drug management program flags. Sourced from RxClaim/Argus PBM integration.'
}

Table plan.network_config {
  network_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each plan‑network configuration record.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the health plan to which this network configuration applies.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider network associated with the plan.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: CMS network adequacy rules (42 CFR 438.68), state network adequacy laws, and NAIC model act requirements are specific regulatory obligations governing network configurations. Linking network_config to']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: Network configuration specifies which tier applies within a provider network for a given plan. Regulatory adequacy filings and member steerage programs require knowing the specific tier associated wit']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: Network configurations are plan-year specific — provider network contracts, adequacy standards, and tier structures are negotiated and filed annually. Linking network_config to the year table ties eac']
  access_type varchar [note: 'Access model of the plan for this network (e.g., HMO closed, PPO open).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this plan‑network configuration ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this plan‑network configuration becomes effective.']
  network_adequacy_measure varchar [note: 'Metric or description used to assess network adequacy (e.g., provider‑to‑member ratio).']
  network_adequacy_standard_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the CMS‑defined adequacy standard applied to this network.']
  network_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the configuration record.']
  network_contract_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contractual agreement governing the provider network.']
  network_coverage_type varchar [note: 'Extent of coverage provided by the network for the plan (full, partial, or none).']
  network_designation varchar [note: 'Overall designation of the network relative to the plan (in‑network, out‑of‑network, or partial).']
  network_exclusion_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of exclusions when the exclusion flag is true.']
  network_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the network has any excluded providers or services.']
  network_geography_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region code (e.g., Northeast, Midwest) where the network operates.']
  network_provider_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active providers participating in the network for the plan.']
  network_state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state abbreviation for the primary state of the network.']
  network_zip_code varchar [note: 'Primary ZIP code associated with the network\'s main service area.']
  out_of_network_benefit_type varchar [note: 'Type of benefit applied to out‑of‑network services (copay, coinsurance, or none).']
  out_of_network_coinsurance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of allowed amount the member pays for out‑of‑network services when benefit type is coinsurance.']
  out_of_network_copay_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar copay amount for out‑of‑network services when benefit type is copay.']
  out_of_network_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan provides any benefit for out‑of‑network services.']
  tier_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to order tiers when multiple tiers exist.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the configuration record.']

  Note: 'Associates a health plan with its provider network configuration — defining which provider networks are in-network for the plan, tiered network designations (Tier 1 preferred, Tier 2 standard), out-of-network coverage rules, and network adequacy standards. Captures the network ID, network tier, access type (HMO closed, PPO open, EPO exclusive), out-of-network benefit flag, and effective dates. Links the plan domain to the network domain for claims adjudication and member directory services.'
}

Table plan.offering {
  offering_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the plan_offering association']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: An employer group offering presents a specific benefit package to employees. When an employer contracts to offer a health plan, they select a specific benefit package (defining the metal tier, cost-sh']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer_group']
  group_renewal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_renewal. Business justification: During annual renewal cycles, plan offerings presented to a group are tied to a specific renewal event. Renewal management reports and open-enrollment configuration depend on linking the offering to t']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to health_plan']
  risk_underwriting_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_underwriting_case. Business justification: Employer group offerings are priced based on underwriting case decisions. Contribution amounts, contribution tiers, and offering terms are direct outputs of the underwriting case for that group. Linki']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: An employer group offering is structured around a plan year — open enrollment windows, contribution amounts, and effective periods are all plan-year aligned. Linking offering to the year table ties ea']
  contribution_amount decimal [note: 'Fixed contribution amount per employee']
  contribution_percent decimal [note: 'Percentage of premium contributed by employer']
  contribution_tier varchar [note: 'Tiered contribution rule identifier']
  contribution_type varchar [note: 'Method of employer contribution (e.g., fixed amount, percentage)']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the offering becomes active']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the offering ends']
  employee_contribution_amount decimal [note: 'Employer contribution amount for employee coverage']
  family_contribution_amount decimal [note: 'Employer contribution amount for family coverage']
  offering_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the specific plan offering']
  offering_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the offering']
  offering_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the offering']
  offering_type varchar [note: 'Type of offering (e.g., standard, pilot)']
  open_enrollment_end_date date [note: 'End date for open enrollment period']
  open_enrollment_start_date date [note: 'Start date for open enrollment period']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contract between a health plan and an employer group. It captures the specific terms of the offering, such as contribution rules, enrollment windows, and status, linking one health_plan to one employer_group.. Existence Justification: An employer group can be offered multiple health plans and a health plan can be offered to multiple employer groups. The contract between them (the plan offering) is actively created, updated, and terminated by business users and carries its own attributes such as effective dates, contribution rules, and status.'
}

Table provider.provider_provider {
  provider_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the provider_provider data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']

  languages_spoken varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of languages the provider can communicate in.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of an individual provider.']
  last_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last verification of provider data against source registries.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'State‑issued professional license identifier.']
  license_state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter US state code where the professional license was issued.']
  malpractice_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider has active malpractice insurance coverage.']
