  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'Contractually specified frequency at which the health plan may exercise audit rights over the PBM. Relevant only when audit_rights_flag is True. Drives compliance calendar and vendor oversight scheduling.']
  audit_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health plan has contractual audit rights to inspect PBM financial records, rebate calculations, MAC list pricing, and claims adjudication data. True = audit rights granted; False = not included. Critical for compliance and financial oversight.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PBM contract automatically renews at the end of the contract term unless notice of termination is provided. True = auto-renews; False = requires affirmative renewal action.']
  cms_contract_number varchar [note: 'CMS (Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services) contract number associated with the Medicare Part D plan under which this PBM contract operates (e.g., H1234 for MA-PD, S1234 for PDP). Required for CMS Part D reporting and RAPS/EDPS submissions. Null for non-Medicare LOBs.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the PBM contract (e.g., \'Express Scripts ASO Agreement FY2024\'). Used for display in reporting dashboards and contract management workflows.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number assigned by the health plan or PBM vendor at execution. Used for correspondence, audits, and regulatory filings. Maps to the contract identifier in RxClaim or Argus PBM system.']
  contract_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the health plan employee or department responsible for managing this PBM contract relationship. Used for escalation routing, audit coordination, and vendor governance.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the PBM contract. Draft indicates the contract is under negotiation; active indicates it is in force; suspended indicates temporarily halted; terminated indicates early cancellation; expired indicates natural end-of-term; pending_renewal indicates renewal is in progress.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PBM contract arrangement. ASO (Administrative Services Only) means the health plan retains financial risk; full_risk means the PBM assumes drug spend risk; carve_out means pharmacy benefits are separated from medical; pass_through means drug costs are passed at actual cost; hybrid combines elements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ASO|full_risk|carve_out|pass_through|hybrid — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the PBM contract document, incremented with each amendment or renewal (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\', \'Amendment_3\'). Supports contract lifecycle management and audit trail for regulatory examinations.']
  dur_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Drug Utilization Review (DUR) — including prospective, concurrent, and retrospective DUR — is included as a service under this PBM contract. True = DUR services included; False = not included.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the PBM contract becomes legally binding and operationally active. Governs when PBM services, pricing schedules, and rebate guarantees take effect. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  generic_dispensing_rate_guarantee decimal(7,4) [note: 'Contractually guaranteed minimum generic dispensing rate (GDR) as a percentage of total prescription claims. If the PBM fails to achieve this rate, financial penalties or credits apply. Key cost-management metric in PBM contracts.']
  governing_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the state whose laws govern this PBM contract for dispute resolution and regulatory compliance purposes. Relevant for state DOI oversight, MAC transparency laws, and PBM regulation.']
  ingredient_cost_basis varchar [note: 'The drug pricing benchmark used as the basis for ingredient cost reimbursement under this contract. AWP (Average Wholesale Price), WAC (Wholesale Acquisition Cost), MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost), NADAC (National Average Drug Acquisition Cost), or AAC (Actual Acquisition Cost). Determines the reimbursement formula for drug ingredient costs.']
  lob_scope varchar [note: 'Line of Business (LOB) covered under this PBM contract. Determines which member populations and benefit programs are governed by this agreement. A single contract may cover one or multiple LOBs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|medicare_part_d|medicaid|exchange|all — promote to reference product for multi-LOB support]']
  mac_list_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost) list applied under this PBM contract for generic and multi-source drug reimbursement. MAC lists cap reimbursement for generics below AWP. References the specific MAC list version maintained by the PBM.']
  mail_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mail-order pharmacy dispensing services are included in the scope of this PBM contract. True = mail order included; False = retail-only or carved out.']
  mail_order_penetration_guarantee decimal(7,4) [note: 'Contractually guaranteed minimum mail-order pharmacy utilization rate as a percentage of eligible maintenance drug claims. Drives cost savings through mail-order channel incentives.']
  performance_guarantee_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PBM contract includes financial performance guarantees (e.g., generic dispensing rate, mail-order penetration, member satisfaction scores). True = performance guarantees included; False = not included.']
  prior_auth_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether prior authorization (PA) requirements for specialty and non-formulary drugs are administered by the PBM under this contract. True = PBM manages PA; False = health plan manages PA internally.']
  quantity_limit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether quantity limit (QL) utilization management rules are in scope under this PBM contract. Quantity limits restrict the amount of a drug dispensed per fill or per time period. True = quantity limits apply; False = not applicable.']
  rebate_guarantee_pmpm decimal(18,4) [note: 'Contractually guaranteed minimum rebate amount expressed as Per Member Per Month (PMPM) in USD that the PBM must remit to the health plan. A key financial performance guarantee in PBM contracts. Used for actuarial reserving and financial reconciliation.']
  rebate_pass_through_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of manufacturer drug rebates that the PBM is contractually obligated to pass through to the health plan (e.g., 100% pass-through vs. spread-based retention). Critical for MLR (Medical Loss Ratio) calculations and CMS Part D compliance.']
  rebate_reconciliation_lag_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days after the close of a settlement period within which the PBM must provide rebate invoices and supporting data to the health plan. Used for financial close scheduling and IBNR reserve calculations.']
  rebate_settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'Contractually specified frequency at which the PBM remits drug manufacturer rebates to the health plan. Drives cash flow forecasting, accounts receivable management, and actuarial IBNR (Incurred But Not Reported) rebate estimates.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PBM contract record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with HIPAA record retention requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PBM contract record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change detection, SCD (Slowly Changing Dimension) processing, and audit trail requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to contract termination_date by which either party must provide written notice of intent not to renew. Operationally critical for contract management and vendor transitions.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational source system from which this PBM contract record was ingested into the lakehouse silver layer (e.g., RxClaim, Argus, manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  specialty_pharmacy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specialty pharmacy benefit management (high-cost biologics, specialty injectables) is within the scope of this PBM contract. True = specialty pharmacy included; False = carved out or not applicable.']
  step_therapy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether step therapy protocols are included in the scope of this PBM contract. Step therapy requires members to try lower-cost drugs before coverage of higher-cost alternatives is authorized. True = step therapy applies; False = not applicable.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the PBM contract ends or was terminated. Null for open-ended contracts. Distinguishes between scheduled expiration and early termination when combined with contract_status. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']

  Note: 'Master record for PBM (Pharmacy Benefit Manager) contracts governing the relationship between the health plan and the PBM (e.g., RxClaim, Argus). Captures contract name, PBM vendor, contract type (ASO/full-risk/carve-out), effective date range, rebate guarantee terms, dispensing fee schedules, MAC list reference, performance guarantees, audit rights, and LOB scope. This is the SSOT for PBM contractual terms within the pharmacy domain.'
}

Table billing.premium_invoice {
  premium_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the premium invoice record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: A premium invoice is issued to a billing account — account is the master financial relationship entity in the billing domain. Every invoice must be traceable to its billing account for AR management, ']
  cobra_election_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.cobra_election. Business justification: COBRA premium invoices are generated specifically for COBRA elections with distinct premium amounts (up to 102% of group rate), payment deadlines, and subsidy tracking. COBRA billing operations, DOL c']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.cycle. Business justification: Invoices are generated per billing cycle. Linking premium_invoice to cycle establishes which billing cycle configuration drove the invoice generation, enabling cycle-level reconciliation and reporting']
  enrollment_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_eligibility_span. Business justification: Premium invoices are generated for specific eligibility spans — billing period, coverage type, and premium amount derive from the span. Retroactive adjustment processing, termination billing, and prem']
  group_division_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_division. Business justification: Large employer groups with billing_entity_flag divisions receive separate invoices per division. Divisional billing reconciliation, contribution reporting, and cost allocation reports require invoice-']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group billed for group coverage.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan or benefit design underlying the premium.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual member associated with the invoice, if applicable.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Required for CMS risk‑adjusted premium reporting linking each invoice to the member\'s risk score at billing time.']
  network_service_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.service_area. Business justification: Invoices are generated per service area to satisfy network adequacy reporting and state filing requirements.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_method. Business justification: premium_invoice.payment_method is a denormalized STRING label. payment_method is the master record of authorized payment methods for a billing account. Replacing the STRING with a FK normalizes the re']
  plan_election_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.plan_election. Business justification: Individual market and COBRA invoices are generated directly from plan elections — the coverage tier, employee/employer contribution split, and total premium on the invoice derive from the plan electio']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.policy. Business justification: Premium invoices are issued for a specific policy. Policy-level invoice reporting, member portal display, and premium reconciliation all require knowing which policy an invoice covers. A health insura']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory filing of premium invoices required by state insurance premium reporting obligations.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Required for premium invoicing: each invoice must reference the enrollment transaction that generated the premium, enabling audit, reporting, and reconciliation.']
  billing_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the coverage period for which the premium is billed.']
  billing_period_start date [note: 'First day of the coverage period for which the premium is billed.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the collection effort for the invoice.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Channel used to deliver the invoice to the recipient.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any discount (e.g., early‑pay, promotional) applied to the invoice.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be received to avoid delinquency.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after due date before the invoice is considered delinquent.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'External invoice number assigned by the billing system, used for member and regulator reference.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the invoice.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice based on the recipient and billing arrangement.']
  is_eligible_for_subsidy boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the member/group qualifies for a premium subsidy.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the invoice was generated and issued to the recipient.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business segment to which the invoice belongs.']
  net_amount_due decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount the recipient must pay after adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional comments or remarks added by billing staff.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Interface through which the payment was made.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount returned to the payer for over‑payment or claim reversal.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the invoice must be reported to CMS or state DOI for compliance.']
  retroactive_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Adjustment for prior‑period premium changes applied to the current invoice.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating billing system that generated the invoice.']
  source_system_invoice_reference varchar [note: 'Original invoice identifier from the source billing system.']
  statement_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for an individual member statement when invoice_type is individual_statement.']
  subsidy_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of premium assistance applied (e.g., APTC, state subsidy).']
  subsidy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the subsidy applied to the premium.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax applied to the premium, if applicable.']
  total_premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross premium before any subsidies, taxes, or discounts.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the invoice record.']

  Note: 'Core billing document representing a premium invoice or statement issued to an employer group, individual member (IFP/ACA marketplace direct-pay), COBRA qualified beneficiary, or government sponsor for a specific billing period. Captures invoice number, billing period start/end, total premium due, subsidy/APTC amounts applied, net amount due, invoice status (draft, issued, paid, partially paid, void, delinquent), due date, document type (group list-bill invoice, group self-bill invoice, individual member statement, COBRA invoice, government remittance request), delivery method (mail, email, member portal), line-of-business (LOB), statement-specific fields for IFP direct-pay members (statement number, member reference, plan name, statement delivery preference), and source system reference from HealthEdge or custom billing platform. SSOT for ALL premium billing documents — both group invoices and individual member statements — in the billing domain.'
}

Table billing.invoice_line {
  invoice_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the invoice line record.']
  aptc_subsidy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.aptc_subsidy. Business justification: invoice_line has aptc_subsidy_amount and csr_adjustment_amount fields representing APTC/CSR applied to the line. Linking to aptc_subsidy provides traceability to the member\'s APTC subsidy record for C']
  auth_service_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.auth_service_line. Business justification: Auth service lines define authorized CPT codes, quantities, and prices at the service level. Linking invoice lines to auth_service_lines enables authorized-vs-billed quantity and price reconciliation ']
  contribution_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.contribution_strategy. Business justification: Invoice lines carry employee_contribution and employer_contribution amounts derived from the contribution strategy. ACA affordability compliance audits and contribution reconciliation reports require ']
  enrollment_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_eligibility_span. Business justification: Each invoice line represents a premium charge for a member\'s specific coverage period defined by the eligibility span. Billing reconciliation, retroactive adjustment line-item processing, and member-l']
  group_division_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_division. Business justification: Invoice lines represent per-member charges; each member belongs to a division. Division-level cost allocation reports, contribution reconciliation, and ACA affordability testing by division require in']
  group_plan_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_plan_offering. Billing reconciliation against offering terms and ACA affo']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health insurance plan or benefit design associated with the charge.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the covered member or subscriber for this premium charge.']
  premium_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent invoice header to which this line belongs.']
  premium_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_rate. Business justification: invoice_line.rate_type is a denormalized STRING label for the rate applied to the line. premium_rate is the authoritative APR/PMPM rate record with coverage_tier, premium_type, rate_amount, and contri']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Invoice lines represent premium charges for coverage under a specific provider network. Network-level premium revenue reporting, reconciliation, and MLR calculations require invoice lines to be direct']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: Invoice lines for tiered network plans must reference the network tier to apply correct premium differentials and cost-sharing amounts. Tiered billing reconciliation and member-facing premium statemen']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Needed for line‑level reconciliation: each invoice line reflects a specific enrollment transaction (plan tier, coverage), supporting detailed financial reporting.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the business reason for a premium adjustment.']
  adjustment_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the adjustment reason.']
  aptc_subsidy_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Advanced Premium Tax Credit amount applied to reduce the member\'s premium.']
  billing_period_end date [note: 'End date of the billing period associated with this line.']
  billing_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the billing period associated with this line.']

  coverage_start_date date [note: 'First day of coverage for which the premium is billed.']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the coverage level for the member (e.g., employee only, employee + spouse, employee + child, family).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the invoice line record was first created in the system.']
  csr_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of cost-sharing reduction (CSR) applied to the premium.']
  employee_contribution decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the premium paid by the employee (or subscriber).']
  employer_contribution decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the premium paid by the employer on behalf of the member.']
  is_refund boolean [note: 'True if this line represents a refund rather than a charge.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the invoice.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment for this premium line is due.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status for the premium line.']
  premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base premium amount before any contributions, subsidies, or adjustments.']
  premium_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the premium amounts.']

  premium_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the premium line.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount refunded to the member or employer for this line (if applicable).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if this line must be included in regulatory reports (e.g., CMS, state DOI).']
  retroactive_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this line reflects a retroactive premium adjustment.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the premium line, if applicable.']
  total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount after employer/employee contributions, subsidies, adjustments, and taxes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the invoice line record.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail within a premium invoice, representing a single member or subscriber coverage charge for a billing period. Captures member/subscriber reference, plan code, coverage tier (EE, EE+SP, EE+CH, FAM), rate type, gross premium, employer contribution, employee contribution, APTC subsidy amount, CSR adjustment, retroactive adjustment indicator, and effective coverage dates. Supports PMPM-level billing reconciliation.'
}

Table billing.premium_payment {
  premium_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the premium payment record.']

  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group responsible for the payment, when applicable.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the member associated with the premium payment.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_method. Business justification: premium_payment.payment_method is a denormalized STRING label. payment_method is the master record of authorized payment methods. Replacing the STRING with a FK normalizes the reference, enables payme']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the insurance policy to which the premium payment applies.']
  premium_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the premium invoice that this payment is applied to.']
  tpa_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.tpa_arrangement. Business justification: For self-funded groups, premium payments (stop-loss premiums, admin fees) flow under TPA arrangements. The tpa_arrangement defines stop-loss parameters and fee schedules governing payment amounts. TPA']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Payments are applied to invoices tied to an enrollment transaction; linking allows tracking payment against the originating enrollment for compliance and financial analysis.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total of fees, discounts, or other adjustments applied to the gross amount.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch that included this payment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the payment.']
  fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any processing or service fees associated with the payment.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount after adjustments, representing the amount applied to the invoice.']
  nsf_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the payment was returned due to non‑sufficient funds.']
  payer_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number or identifier used by the payer for the transaction.']
  payer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity making the payment.']
  payer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payer (e.g., employer, individual member, CMS, state Medicaid agency).']
  payment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount received before any adjustments.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Interface through which the payment was submitted.']
  payment_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the payment.']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Unique payment reference number assigned by the billing system.']
  payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment was received.']
  premium_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the payment in the accounting reconciliation process.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date the suspense item was resolved.']
  resolution_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the suspense item should be resolved.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the suspense investigation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the payment record.']
  suspense_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why the payment is in suspense (e.g., unidentified payer, amount mismatch).']
  suspense_resolver varchar [note: 'Name of the employee or system responsible for investigating the suspense item.']
  suspense_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment within the suspense workflow.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component of the payment, if applicable.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment transaction.']
  unapplied_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment that could not be matched to an invoice and is placed in suspense.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment record.']

  Note: 'Records an actual payment received against a premium invoice, capturing payment date, payment amount, payment method (ACH, check, wire, credit card, EFT), payment reference number, payer type (employer group, individual member, CMS, state Medicaid agency), applied invoice reference, unapplied/suspense balance, NSF/return indicator, reconciliation status, and full suspense management lifecycle (suspense status, suspense reason code — unidentified payer, amount mismatch, duplicate payment, missing invoice reference — received amount, receipt date, payer name, payer account reference, assigned resolver, resolution due date, resolution outcome — applied to invoice, refunded, written off — resolution date). Payments in suspense status are tracked inline with complete resolution workflow rather than in a separate suspense entity. SSOT for all inbound premium cash receipts including suspense items, their investigation, and resolution lifecycle.'
}

Table billing.payment_allocation {
  payment_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each payment allocation record.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice line to which the payment amount is allocated.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_method. Business justification: payment_allocation.payment_method is a denormalized STRING label. payment_method is the master record of authorized payment methods. Replacing the STRING with a FK normalizes the reference for allocat']
  premium_payment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the premium payment that is being allocated.']
  allocated_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the premium that is allocated to the invoice line.']
  allocated_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of premium units (e.g., member‑months) allocated to the invoice line.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the allocation was recorded.']
  allocation_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of allocation lines within the same payment.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the allocation record.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allocation purpose (e.g., standard payment, partial payment, over‑payment, suspense resolution, adjustment, refund).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for the allocated amount.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Discount or credit applied to the allocated premium.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the allocation becomes effective for accounting purposes.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the allocation ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  is_overpayment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocation represents an overpayment scenario.']
  is_suspense_resolution boolean [note: 'True when the allocation resolves a suspense balance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the allocation.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment was submitted.']
  reconciliation_period varchar [note: 'Period identifier (e.g., 2023-04) for the premium reconciliation cycle.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reconciliation record.']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Frequency classification of the reconciliation (e.g., monthly, quarterly).']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the allocated premium.']
  total_adjustments decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate of all adjustments (e.g., refunds, credits) applied during the period.']
  total_billed decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of all invoice line amounts billed for the reconciliation period.']
  total_collected decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of all payments collected for the reconciliation period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the allocation record.']
  variance_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Difference between total billed and total collected after adjustments.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any variance observed in the reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Association and reconciliation entity linking premium payments to invoice lines, tracking line-level allocation, and managing periodic premium reconciliation outcomes. For allocation: captures allocated amount, allocation date, allocation type (standard, partial, overpayment, suspense resolution), and line-level reconciliation status. Supports many-to-many payment-to-invoice matching and enables accurate accounts receivable aging. For periodic reconciliation: captures reconciliation period, total billed, total collected, total adjustments, variance amount, variance reason, reconciliation status (open, in review, approved, closed), approver reference, and reconciliation type (monthly, quarterly, annual). Supports MLR calculation inputs, financial close processes, CMS premium reconciliation, and end-to-end premium reconciliation from individual line-level allocation through period-level summary and variance analysis. SSOT for all payment allocation and premium reconciliation in the billing domain.'
}

SSOT for billing account identity, billing cycle configuration, and authorized payment methods.'
}

Business justification: Premium rates are priced and filed per provider network — narrow network plans carry lower rates than broad network plans.   tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: Tiered network products (Tier 1/Tier 2) carry distinct premium rates per tier. The rating engine must reference the network tier to compute correct member premiums and cost-sharing differentials. Heal']

  aptc_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of APTC applied to the premium for ACA‑eligible members.']

  Note: 'Defines the APR (Annual Premium Rate) and PMPM rate structures for a plan, coverage tier, rating area, and effective date range, including the parent rate schedule configuration that governs how rates are structured and applied. }

Table billing.grace_period {
  grace_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the grace period record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing account to which this grace period applies.']
  care_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_enrollment. Business justification: Grace period status directly affects care program enrollment continuity. Care coordinators must verify billing grace period status before authorizing care management services; grace period termination']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.cycle. Business justification: Grace periods are triggered within a specific billing cycle when payment is overdue. Linking grace_period to cycle establishes which billing cycle configuration governs the grace period duration and t']
  enrollment_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_eligibility_span. Business justification: Grace periods protect a specific active eligibility span from termination during non-payment. ACA mandates a 3-month grace period for APTC enrollees tied to the eligibility span. Grace period outcome ']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Grace period rules (90-day ACA APTC rule vs. 30-day non-ACA) are plan-specific and jurisdiction-dependent. This FK enables plan-level grace period compliance reporting, termination processing workflow']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the member associated with the account for this grace period.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.policy. Business justification: ACA mandates a 3-month grace period for APTC recipients tied to a specific policy. CMS regulatory reporting requires grace period tracking at the policy level. A compliance officer would expect grace_']
  premium_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice that triggered the grace period.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: ACA grace periods (45 CFR 156.270) are strictly regulated with mandatory 3-month windows for APTC recipients. This FK links each grace period record to the governing federal/state regulatory obligatio']
  resolving_premium_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_payment. Business justification: A grace period is resolved when a payment is received. Linking grace_period to the resolving premium_payment provides a direct audit trail from grace period to the payment that closed it. This FK is n']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the grace period record was first created in the system.']
  day_count varchar [note: 'Number of days elapsed since the start of the grace period.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the grace period is scheduled to end.']
  extension_end_date date [note: 'New end date if the grace period was extended.']
  extension_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the original grace period was extended.']
  grace_period_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the grace period.']
  grace_period_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the grace period, used in reporting and member communications.']
  grace_period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the grace period.']
  grace_period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the grace period based on product or regulatory rules.']
  is_eligible_for_aptc boolean [note: 'Indicates if the member is eligible for Advanced Premium Tax Credit during this grace period.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction applicable to the grace period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes captured by the billing team.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the grace period after it ends.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the premium payment was originally due before the grace period started.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date on which the overdue payment was received, if applicable.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why the grace period was initiated.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this grace period must be reported to CMS or state DOI.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the grace period began.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state abbreviation for jurisdictional reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AL|AK|AZ|AR|CA|CO|CT|DE|FL|GA|HI|ID|IL|IN|IA|KS|KY|LA|ME|MD|MA|MI|MN|MS|MO|MT|NE|NV|NH|NJ|NM|NY|NC|ND|OH|OK|OR|PA|RI|SC|SD|TN|TX|UT|VT|VA|WA|WV|WI|WY — promote to reference product]']
  subsidy_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of any subsidy applied during the grace period.']
  termination_warning_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a termination warning has been sent to the member.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the grace period record.']

  Note: 'Tracks the grace period status for a billing account or member when premium payment is overdue. Captures grace period start date, grace period end date (typically 30 days for individual/ACA, 31 days for group), grace period type (ACA APTC grace period is 90 days), triggering invoice reference, current grace period day count, termination warning issued flag, and resolution outcome (paid, terminated, reinstated). Required for ACA compliance and state DOI reporting.'
}

SSOT for all retroactive billing adjustments and billing dispute resolution in the billing domain. Critical for accurate premium reconciliation, dispute lifecycle management, and financial reporting.'

  billing_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months that define one billing period (e.g., 1 for monthly, 3 for quarterly).']

  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component added to the net premium.']

  Note: 'Defines the billing cycle configuration for a billing account or group, specifying billing frequency, invoice generation date, payment due date, grace period days, billing method, and cycle status. Captures cycle type (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual), next invoice date, last invoice date, auto-renewal flag, and billing calendar reference. Drives the automated invoice generation schedule in HealthEdge.'
}

Supports PCI-DSS compliant payment processing.'
}

This li']

  clinical_criteria_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific clinical rule applied.']

  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Employer cost reporting requires associating each prior‑auth request with the sponsoring employer group.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Prior Authorization Request Volume Report groups requests by health plan to monitor utilization and cost.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Utilization Management uses member\'s risk score to prioritize prior auth decisions; the \'PA Risk Score Review\' report requires linking each PA request to the member_risk_score record.']
  plan_election_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan under which the member is covered.']

  primary_pa_member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member requesting the service.']
  primary_pa_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Required for Prior Authorization request processing to link each request to the provider entity for reporting, compliance, and performance metrics.']

Business justification: PA requests must be evaluated against the specific provider contract to determine coverage, prior‑auth requirements, and payment methodology; required for PA decision and compliance reporting.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network relevant to the request.']

  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the reason for denial, if applicable.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'Diagnosis code supporting the medical necessity of the request.']

  estimated_net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final estimated net cost after adjustments.']
  is_appealable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member may appeal a denial.']

  last_air_received_date date [note: 'Date the last additional information request was received.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or notes related to the request.']
  pa_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the prior authorization request.']
  patient_age_at_request varchar [note: 'Age of the member at the time of request submission.']
  patient_gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the member at the time of request.']
  prior_auth_decision_date date [note: 'Date the prior authorization decision was made.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units or sessions requested.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the prior authorization request.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the prior authorization request was submitted.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Urgency classification of the prior authorization request.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Procedure, service, or drug code associated with the request.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of the service being requested for prior authorization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the request record.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the prior authorization request was submitted.']
  turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from submission to final decision.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the request record.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a prior authorization (PA) request submitted by a provider or member for a proposed service, procedure, drug, or admission. Captures requesting provider NPI, member ID, requested service codes (CPT/HCPCS/ICD), urgency type (standard, expedited, urgent), submission channel (portal, fax, phone, EDI 278), submission timestamp, current PA status, and full status transition history. Also tracks additional information requests (AIRs) issued during review — information type requested, requested-from party, due date, received date, and TAT clock extension impact. Authoritative SSOT for all PA requests, their status lifecycle, and associated documentation requests.'
}

Table utilization.pa_decision {
  pa_decision_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each prior authorization decision record.']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: PA decisions must reference the benefit package to confirm coverage scope, apply correct cost-share, and validate that the authorized service falls within package limits.   care_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_plan. Business justification: PA decisions (approvals/denials) directly affect care plan execution. Care managers must reconcile authorization outcomes against the active care plan to update goals and services. This link supports ']
  clinical_criteria_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.clinical_criteria. Business justification: A PA decision is made based on specific clinical criteria (InterQual/MCG). pa_decision has clinical_criteria_set and clinical_criteria_version as strings but lacks a FK to the clinical_criteria master']
  enrollment_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_eligibility_span. Business justification: PA decisions must be validated against the member\'s active benefit package, coverage limits, and network assignment stored on the eligibility span. This direct link supports benefit coordination, cost']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Employer‑level analytics on prior‑auth decisions need a link to the employer group for regulatory reporting.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: PA Decision Summary Dashboard needs the plan context to calculate approval rates per plan.']
  medical_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.medical_policy. Business justification: pa_decision references the medical policy that governs the decision; child (decision) gets FK to parent (medical_policy).']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member/patient associated with the request.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior‑authorization request to which this decision belongs.']
  provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_provider_contract.   provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Links PA decision to the originating provider for audit trails, regulatory reporting, and provider‑specific denial analysis.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Prior Authorization decisions require confirming the provider’s participation in a specific network; network_id enables compliance reporting and eligibility checks.']
  um_reviewer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.um_reviewer. Business justification: PA decisions are made by UM reviewers (medical directors, physician advisors). pa_decision currently stores reviewer_npi and reviewer_credentials as denormalized strings.   amendment_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates if this record is the original decision or an amendment/reversal.']
  appeal_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is eligible to appeal the decision.']
  authorization_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date of the approved authorization period.']
  authorization_period_type varchar [note: 'Specifies if the authorization period is a fixed date range or rolling.']
  authorization_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units (e.g., days, visits, doses) authorized.']
  authorization_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the approved authorization period.']
  authorization_units varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the authorized quantity.']
  clinical_criteria_set varchar [note: 'Name of the clinical criteria library applied (e.g., InterQual, MCG).']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the criteria set used for the decision.']
  clinical_rationale varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation of the clinical reasoning behind the decision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the decision record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  criteria_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the applied clinical criteria version became effective.']
  criteria_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request met all required clinical criteria (true) or not (false).']
  decision_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the prior authorization decision was rendered.']
  decision_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the decision, often used in communications and audit trails.']
  decision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the decision record.']
  decision_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the decision outcome.']
  denial_reason_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the denial reason.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for denial.']
  denial_regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory or policy citation supporting the denial.']
  diagnosis_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of ICD‑10 diagnosis codes supporting the medical necessity determination.']
  is_telehealth boolean [note: 'True if the service requested is delivered via telehealth.']
  is_urgent boolean [note: 'True if the request was marked urgent.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information.']
  prior_auth_number varchar [note: 'Business number assigned to the prior‑authorization request.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with NCQA UM accreditation or state PA regulations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the decision record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the authorization decision and underlying medical necessity determination rendered on a PA request. Stores decision date, decision type (approved, denied, partially approved, pended, withdrawn), clinical criteria applied (InterQual/MCG criteria set, version, effective date), criteria met/not met flag, clinical rationale narrative, ICD diagnosis codes supporting the determination, deciding reviewer NPI and credentials (MD, RN, PharmD), denial reason code and category (medical necessity, administrative, benefit exclusion, out-of-network, experimental/investigational), denial regulatory citation, appeal eligibility flag, and effective authorization period (start/end dates). Each PA request may have one or more decision records reflecting amendments, P2P reversals, or appeal overrides. Serves as the single evidentiary record for NCQA audits, state DOI market conduct exams, and denial defense.'
}

  social_work_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a social worker was consulted for discharge planning.']

  Note: 'Manages inpatient concurrent review records for admitted members — the ongoing UM review process evaluating continued medical necessity during a hospital stay. Captures admission date, current and approved length of stay (LOS), LOS benchmarks by DRG (geometric/arithmetic mean LOS, plan-specific target, outlier threshold, benchmark source — CMS, Milliman, plan actuarial), next review date, clinical reviewer NPI, InterQual/MCG criteria version applied, continued stay justification. Includes integrated discharge planning: planned discharge date, discharge destination (home, SNF, LTAC, home health, hospice), post-acute services authorized, barriers to discharge, social work involvement, care coordinator assigned, actual discharge date, and readmission risk score. Links to the originating PA request, UM case, and inpatient admission.'
}

  case_open_date date [note: 'Date the UM case was opened and became active.']
  case_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the case for workload management.']
  case_severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating reflecting clinical urgency and complexity.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the UM case.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Category of the case based on care setting or service type.']
  clinical_criteria_set varchar [note: 'Source set of clinical criteria applied (InterQual or MCG).']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the clinical criteria set used for the case.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case meets NCQA UM accreditation requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the UM case record was first created in the system.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why a prior authorization or claim was denied.']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Textual explanation of the denial reason.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the final outcome of the case.']
  disposition_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the case outcome.']
  length_of_stay_actual varchar [note: 'Observed length of stay for the episode, expressed in days.']
  length_of_stay_target varchar [note: 'Planned maximum length of stay for the episode, expressed in days.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for clinical or administrative comments.']
  primary_diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'ICD code representing the principal diagnosis driving the case.']
  primary_diagnosis_description varchar [note: 'Text description of the principal diagnosis.']
  primary_provider_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the primary provider.']
  prior_authorization_decision_date date [note: 'Date the prior authorization decision was rendered.']
  prior_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the prior authorization request linked to the case.']
  prior_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the prior authorization request.']
  turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from case open to final disposition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the UM case record.']
  urgency_flag boolean [note: 'True if the case requires expedited review per clinical criteria.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a utilization management (UM) case — the overarching workflow container grouping all UM activities (PA requests, concurrent reviews, retrospective reviews, P2P reviews, appeals) for a single member episode of care. Captures case type (inpatient, outpatient, behavioral health, DME, pharmacy), case open/close dates, assigned UM nurse or case manager, episode of care identifier, primary diagnosis, and case disposition. Enables end-to-end UM workflow tracking, workload management, and NCQA UM accreditation reporting.'
}

  um_reviewer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the utilization management reviewer.']

  country varchar [note: 'Country of reviewer’s residence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reviewer record was first created in the system.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Professional credential held by the reviewer.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Reviewer’s birth date.']
  delegation_authority_level varchar [note: 'Level of authority the reviewer has to delegate or approve utilization decisions.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email for reviewer communications.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Reviewer’s given name.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Concatenated full legal name of the reviewer.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the reviewer was hired by the health plan.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Reviewer’s family name.']
  last_training_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent required UM/PA training completed by the reviewer.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the reviewer’s professional license expires.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued license number for the reviewer.']
  license_state varchar [note: 'State that issued the professional license.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Reviewer’s middle name, if any.']
  ncqa_qualified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reviewer meets NCQA accreditation qualifications.']
  next_training_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the reviewer’s next required training.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or observations about the reviewer.']
  npi varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the reviewer by the National Provider Identifier system.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number.']
  reviewer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reviewer within UM operations.']
  specialty varchar [note: 'Medical specialty area of the reviewer (e.g., cardiology, oncology).']
  state varchar [note: 'State of reviewer’s address.']
  street_address varchar [note: 'Reviewer’s mailing street address.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the reviewer’s employment ended, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reviewer record.']
  urac_qualified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reviewer meets URAC accreditation qualifications.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of reviewer’s address.']

  Note: 'Master entity for UM clinical reviewers — nurses, medical directors, and physician advisors who conduct UM and PA reviews. Captures reviewer NPI (if applicable), reviewer name, credential type (RN, MD, PharmD, LCSW), specialty, active status, state licensure details, URAC/NCQA reviewer qualification status, assigned review queues, and delegation authority level. Distinct from the provider domain — this entity represents internal plan staff performing UM functions.'
}

Business justification: Inpatient admission authorization and cost-sharing determination require knowing which provider network the admitting facility belongs to. Network status drives coverage rules, authorization requireme']
  um_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.um_case. Business justification: An inpatient admission is the subject of a UM case. While concurrent_review bridges inpatient_admission and um_case, a direct FK from inpatient_admission to um_case enables efficient case management q']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final cost incurred for the admission after discharge.']

  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the utilization management review.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system for the admission data (e.g., InterQual, MCG).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the admission record.']

  Note: 'UM-managed inpatient admission record for admissions subject to concurrent review. Captures admitting facility NPI, admitting physician NPI, admission date, admission type (elective, urgent, emergent), primary admitting diagnosis (ICD), DRG assignment, expected LOS, actual discharge date, discharge disposition (home, SNF, rehab, expired), and payer authorization status. Serves as the anchor for concurrent review workflows and LOS benchmark comparisons. Distinct from the claim domain\'s institutional claim — this is the clinical/UM view of the admission, not the billing view.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference for the health plan\'s UM program configurations — the formal UM program definitions required by NCQA, URAC, and state DOI. Governs which services require PA and which clinical criteria apply.'
}

Drives PA intake routing, provider-facing PA requirement lookups, state PA reform compliance, and gold card mandate tracking.'
}

Table utilization.medical_policy {
  medical_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the medical policy record.']

Business justification: Medical policies may be scoped to specific benefit packages — a policy may apply to HMO but not PPO packages. This link enables package-level policy management, ensures correct policy application duri']
  clinical_criteria_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.clinical_criteria. Business justification: Medical policies are based on clinical criteria (InterQual/MCG guidelines). medical_policy has clinical_criteria_source and clinical_criteria_version as strings but no FK to the clinical_criteria mast']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Certain medical policies are employer‑specific; linking enables policy applicability checks per employer.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Medical Policy Applicability Matrix links each policy to the plans it governs for compliance reporting.']
  parent_medical_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on medical_policy (parent_medical_policy_id)']
  policy_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_document. Business justification: Medical policies are formally governed by compliance policy documents (coverage determination policies, clinical coverage bulletins). Linking medical_policy to its governing policy_document is require']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Medical policies must align with regulatory obligations; linking provides traceability for compliance audits.']
  applicable_cpt_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of CPT procedure codes covered under the policy.']
  applicable_hcpcs_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of HCPCS codes (including drugs) covered under the policy.']
  clinical_category varchar [note: 'High‑level clinical grouping (e.g., cardiology, orthopedics) to which the policy applies.']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the clinical criteria set applied.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the policy with respect to external regulations.']
  coverage_limit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cap applied to the total covered amount for the policy period.']
  coverage_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the coverage limit amount.']
  coverage_limit_units varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the coverage limit (e.g., visits, days).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the policy record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the policy becomes binding for members.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the policy ceases to be binding; null for open‑ended policies.']
  is_emergency_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether emergency services are covered without prior authorization.']
  is_exempt boolean [note: 'True if the policy includes exemptions for specific services or member groups.']
  is_experimental_covered boolean [note: 'True if experimental or investigational services are permitted under the policy.']
  is_prior_auth_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a prior authorization must be obtained for services covered by this policy.']
  is_telehealth_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether telehealth services are covered under the policy.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the most recent policy review.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the policy was last reviewed for clinical or regulatory updates.']
  medical_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the policy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments, special considerations, or audit remarks.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the policy for reporting and UI display.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the policy, used in member communications and claim adjudication.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Category of the policy indicating the primary clinical domain it governs.']
  prior_auth_approval_timeframe_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days allowed to approve a prior authorization request.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation to the regulatory rule or guideline that mandates this policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the policy record.']
  version_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the policy became effective.']
  version_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the policy was superseded or retired.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for policy revisions.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the policy record.']

  Note: 'Master reference for medical policies and clinical criteria used in utilization management decisions — including medical necessity criteria, clinical guidelines, evidence-based medicine references, and policy effective dates. Captures policy ID, policy name, clinical category, applicable CPT/HCPCS codes, review criteria source (InterQual, MCG, internal), and version history.'
}

Table utilization.clinical_criteria {
  clinical_criteria_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for clinical_criteria']
  parent_clinical_criteria_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on clinical_criteria (parent_clinical_criteria_id)']
  policy_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_document. Business justification: Clinical criteria (InterQual, MCG, proprietary) are formally documented in compliance policy documents. The criteria_reference on clinical_criteria is a denormalized reference to policy_document. NCQA']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  criteria_category varchar [note: 'Broad category indicating the clinical setting or context of the criteria.']
  criteria_code varchar [note: 'Unique code assigned to the clinical criteria, used for lookup and integration.']
  criteria_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the clinical criteria, including its purpose and application.']
  criteria_detail varchar [note: 'Textual or structured detail of the rule or logic that defines the criteria.']
  criteria_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the clinical criteria becomes effective and applicable.']
  criteria_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the clinical criteria is no longer valid or applicable.']
  criteria_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the limit value (e.g., days, dollars).']
  criteria_limit_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric limit value used in the criteria rule (e.g., maximum allowed).']
  criteria_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the clinical criteria.']
  criteria_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about the clinical criteria.']
  criteria_rule_expression varchar [note: 'Formal expression or logic that defines how the criteria is evaluated.']
  criteria_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or authority that defines the clinical criteria.']
  criteria_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the clinical criteria.']
  criteria_subcategory varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the category to refine the criteria context.']
  criteria_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the threshold value (e.g., days, dollars).']
  criteria_threshold_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold value used in the criteria rule (e.g., maximum days, minimum cost).']
  criteria_type varchar [note: 'Category of the clinical criteria indicating the type of clinical element it applies to.']
  criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the clinical criteria to track updates and revisions.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the record for data lifecycle management.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was last updated in the system.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for clinical_criteria. Referenced by clinical_criteria_id.'
}

Table utilization.reviewer_program_assignment {
  reviewer_program_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the reviewer_program_assignment association']
  um_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the UM program under which the reviewer is authorized.']
  um_reviewer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the UM reviewer who is authorized under this program assignment.']
  assigned_review_queue varchar [note: 'Primary review queue to which the reviewer is assigned. [Moved from um_reviewer: The review queue a reviewer works in is program-specific — a reviewer may work different queues under different UM programs. This attribute is more accurately a property of the reviewer-program assignment than a single static attribute on the reviewer master record. However, if the business treats assigned_review_queue as the reviewer\'s primary/default queue independent of program context, it may remain on um_reviewer. Flag for business stakeholder confirmation.]']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the reviewer\'s authorization under this UM program expires or is revoked. Null if the assignment is currently active. Enables historical compliance queries.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the reviewer\'s authorization to conduct reviews under this UM program becomes effective. Required for NCQA/URAC compliance auditing to confirm reviewer qualification was in place at time of review.']
  reviewer_role_in_program varchar [note: 'The specific functional role the reviewer performs within this UM program (e.g., primary clinical reviewer, peer-to-peer reviewer, medical director sign-off). A reviewer may hold different roles across different programs, so this attribute belongs to the assignment, not the reviewer.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal Assignment between um_reviewer and um_program. It captures the authorization of a qualified UM reviewer to conduct reviews under a specific UM program, as required by NCQA and URAC accreditation standards. Each record links one um_reviewer to one um_program and carries the effective dates and role of the assignment — data that exists only in the context of this reviewer-program authorization and is essential for compliance auditing and accreditation documentation.. Existence Justification: In health insurance UM operations, reviewers are formally assigned to specific UM programs based on their credentials, specialty, and licensure — a single reviewer (e.g., an RN with oncology specialty) may be authorized under multiple UM programs (e.g., inpatient UM and specialty drug UM), and each UM program requires multiple qualified reviewers to meet NCQA/URAC staffing standards. This assignment is an actively managed, compliance-critical business concept: health plans must document which reviewers are authorized under which programs, with effective dates, to satisfy accreditation audits. The relationship carries its own data (assignment start/end dates, reviewer role within the program) that belongs to neither the reviewer nor the program alone.'
}

Goals are modeled as embedded detail records within the care plan with independent status tracking, enabling multi-dimensional progress measurement across clinical, behavioral, and SDOH goal categories. Single source of truth for all care plan goal lifecycle management. Supports longitudinal tracking of member health outcomes. Each goal has its own lifecycle independent of the parent care plan, enabling granular tracking of member progress across multiple care dimensions.'
