  contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount contributed by employee per pay period for this division (if applicable).']
  contribution_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of premium contributed by employee for this division (if applicable).']
  contribution_strategy varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate employee contributions for this division.']
  covered_member_count varchar [note: 'Number of members (including dependents) covered under this division\'s benefits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the division record was first created in the system.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Unique code assigned to the division for internal reference and billing.']
  division_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the division or subsidiary within the employer group.']
  division_type varchar [note: 'Category of the division indicating its organizational role.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the division\'s benefit configuration ends or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the division\'s benefit configuration becomes effective.']
  eligibility_rule_set varchar [note: 'Reference to the eligibility rule set applied to members in this division.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Number of employees assigned to this division.']
  flex_spending_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer contribution amount for the division\'s Flexible Spending Account.']
  fsa_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer contribution amount for the division\'s Flexible Spending Account.']
  group_division_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the division.']
  hsa_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer contribution amount for the division\'s Health Savings Account.']
  is_eligible_for_flex_spending boolean [note: 'Indicates if the division participates in a Flexible Spending Account (FSA) program.']
  is_eligible_for_fsa boolean [note: 'Indicates if the division offers a Flexible Spending Account (FSA) to its members.']
  is_eligible_for_hsa boolean [note: 'Indicates if the division offers a Health Savings Account (HSA) to its members.']
  is_eligible_for_subsidy boolean [note: 'Indicates if the division qualifies for employer-sponsored subsidy programs.']
  is_eligible_for_waiver boolean [note: 'Indicates if the division is eligible for premium waivers under certain conditions.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date when the division\'s contract is up for renewal.']
  subsidy_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar subsidy amount applied to employee premiums for this division.']
  subsidy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage subsidy applied to employee premiums for this division.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the division\'s agreement was terminated, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the division record.']

  Note: 'Organizational sub-units within an employer group — divisions, subsidiaries, cost centers, or departments — that have distinct benefit configurations, billing aggregation, or eligibility rules. Tracks division name, division code, parent group reference, effective dates, and whether the division is a separate billing entity. Supports multi-site and multi-subsidiary group structures in Facets.'
}

Table employer.group_plan_offering {
  group_plan_offering_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the group plan offering record.']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: A group plan offering presents a specific benefit package to employees, not just a health plan. Benefits enrollment systems must resolve which benefit package (metal tier, cost-share structure) is act']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group that sponsors this offering.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying health plan catalog record.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Employer plan offerings are tied to a specific provider network for network adequacy reporting, open enrollment materials, and member steerage. Underwriters and account managers need direct access to ']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_tier. Business justification: Supports Plan Tier Assignment process: defines which network tier (e.g., Tier 1, Tier 2) an employer\'s plan offering uses for cost‑share calculations.']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: group_plan_offering.plan_year is a denormalized plain-text field. Normalizing to plan.year.year_id enables accurate plan-year-based contribution reporting, ACA affordability calculations, and renewal ']
  contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount the employer contributes per employee when contribution_type = \'flat\'.']
  contribution_effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the defined contribution strategy expires; null if ongoing.']
  contribution_effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the defined contribution strategy becomes effective.']
  contribution_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the premium the employer pays when contribution_type = \'percentage\'.']
  contribution_strategy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of how employer contributions are calculated.']
  contribution_tier varchar [note: 'Textual description of tiered contribution rules (e.g., "Tier 1: 70%", "Tier 2: 80%").']
  contribution_type varchar [note: 'Method the employer uses to calculate its contribution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the offering record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the offering becomes binding for the employer group.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the offering ends; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  employee_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer contribution amount for employee‑only coverage.']
  family_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer contribution amount for family coverage.']
  group_plan_offering_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the offering.']
  hra_seed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer‑funded seed contribution to employee HRA accounts.']
  hsa_seed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer‑funded seed contribution to employee HSA accounts.']
  is_affordable boolean [note: 'True if the offering meets ACA affordability testing for the employer group.']
  measurement_period_end date [note: 'End of the period used to evaluate participation compliance.']
  measurement_period_start date [note: 'Start of the period used to evaluate participation compliance.']
  minimum_enrolled_headcount varchar [note: 'Absolute minimum number of participants required for the offering.']
  minimum_participation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required percentage of eligible employees that must enroll for the offering to remain active.']
  offering_code varchar [note: 'External code used by the employer and carriers to reference this specific plan offering.']
  offering_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the plan offering, including benefits highlights.']
  offering_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the plan offering as displayed to employees.']
  offering_type varchar [note: 'Category of the health plan offering (e.g., QHP, HMO, PPO, EPO, HDHP, Dental, Vision). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qhp|hmo|ppo|epo|hdhp|dental|vision — promote to reference product]']
  open_enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Last day employees can enroll in the offering.']
  open_enrollment_start_date date [note: 'First day employees can enroll in the offering.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance result for the offering\'s participation thresholds.']
  plan_catalog_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the plan catalog used for this offering.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the offering record.']
  waiver_criteria_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the conditions under which a waiver may be granted.']
  waiver_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employer allows waiver of spousal or other coverage.']

  Note: 'The set of health plan products offered by an employer group to its eligible employees during a plan year — the single source of truth for what plans are available, how the employer contributes, and what participation rules apply. Captures which QHP, HMO, PPO, EPO, HDHP, dental, and vision plans are available; employer contribution strategy per plan including contribution type (flat dollar, percentage of premium, tiered by coverage tier), employee-only vs family contribution amounts, HSA/HRA employer seed amounts, and contribution effective date ranges; minimum participation percentage, minimum enrolled headcount, waiver eligibility criteria (e.g., spousal coverage waivers), measurement period, and participation compliance status; and the open enrollment window linkage. Consolidates contribution strategies and participation requirements as attributes of the plan offering. Links employer groups to the plan catalog and drives the enrollment eligibility matrix, premium billing split calculations, and ACA affordability compliance testing.'
}

  sic_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit industry classification code for the employer.']

  Note: 'Annual renewal lifecycle record and single source of truth for all group configuration changes — both at renewal and between renewal cycles. Captures renewal effective date, prior year vs renewal year premium rates, rate change percentage, benefit modifications, participation requirement outcomes, renewal status (pending, proposed, accepted, declined), and retention outcome. Also captures mid-year and off-cycle amendments including amendment type (benefit change, plan add/drop, contribution change, address update, contact change), amendment effective date, reason code, approval status, and before/after values of changed attributes. Consolidates group amendments as lifecycle events within the renewal record — amendments are tracked against the current plan year\'s renewal. Provides complete audit trail for group retention management, regulatory compliance, and configuration change history.'
}

  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the arrangement terminates or expires; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the arrangement becomes binding.']
  erisa_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the arrangement is subject to ERISA regulations.']
  fee_schedule_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount payable for the fee schedule within a billing period.']
  fee_schedule_rate_pmpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Per member per month rate charged for the specified service type.']
  fee_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Service category for which the fee schedule applies.']
  gfc_control_flag boolean [note: 'True if the arrangement is subject to Group Financial Control oversight.']
  group_size_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of covered lives allowed under the arrangement.']
  group_size_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of covered lives required for the arrangement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special conditions.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date when the arrangement is scheduled for renewal.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., Facets, QNXT) that supplied the record.']
  stop_loss_coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Benefit categories covered by the stop‑loss policy.']
  stop_loss_effective_date date [note: 'Date the stop‑loss coverage becomes active.']
  stop_loss_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the stop‑loss coverage ends.']
  tpa_arrangement_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the TPA arrangement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the arrangement record.']

  Note: 'Third Party Administrator (TPA) and Administrative Services Only (ASO) arrangement record for self-funded employer groups — the single source of truth for all ASO/self-funded configuration. Captures TPA entity name, TPA NPI/tax ID, ASO contract reference; administrative fee schedule components by service type (claims processing, network access, care management, PBM, utilization review) with PMPM rates, effective dates, and contractual fee caps; stop-loss (excess loss) insurance configuration including stop-loss carrier name, policy number, specific deductible (individual attachment point), aggregate deductible, aggregate corridor, lasering provisions with member-level laser amounts, stop-loss premium, covered benefits scope, and stop-loss policy effective/expiration dates; and overall arrangement effective dates. Consolidates ASO fee schedules and stop-loss policies as sub-configurations of the TPA arrangement, aligned with Facets/QNXT ASO module patterns. Drives ASO revenue recognition in the finance domain and self-funded group financial risk management.'
}

Table employer.open_enrollment_window {
  open_enrollment_window_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for open_enrollment_window']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employer group that sponsors the enrollment window.']
  group_renewal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_renewal. Business justification: An open enrollment window is triggered by and associated with a specific renewal cycle. The renewal record defines the plan year, effective dates, and benefit changes that the open enrollment window c']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: Open enrollment windows are defined per plan year; the plan year record governs enrollment start/end dates, grace periods, and special enrollment rules. HR systems and compliance reports require linki']
  change_deadline date [note: 'Last date an employee may modify an already‑submitted election within the window.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Category of benefits offered in this enrollment window.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment window record was first created in the system.']
  eligible_employee_count varchar [note: 'Number of employees who meet eligibility criteria for this enrollment window.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date the enrollment window closes; no further changes are accepted after this date.']
  enrollment_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which employees may submit their elections (online portal, paper forms, or EDI 834).']
  enrollment_window_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment window.']
  enrollment_window_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the window (annual open enrollment, new‑hire election, COBRA election, or special election).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks or special instructions related to the window.']
  participation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (to two decimal places) of eligible employees who completed an election.']
  plan_selection_method varchar [note: 'Whether employees may select a single plan or multiple plans during the window.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the enrollment window record (e.g., Facets, QNXT).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the enrollment window becomes effective for employee elections.']
  submission_deadline date [note: 'Final date by which an employee must submit an election to be processed.']
  total_employee_count varchar [note: 'Total number of employees in the employer group at the start of the window.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the enrollment window record.']
  waiver_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees may waive coverage during this window.']
  waiver_deadline date [note: 'Date by which a waiver must be submitted if waivers are permitted.']
  window_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the enrollment window (e.g., OE2024Q1).']

  Note: 'Scheduled open enrollment windows defined by an employer group during which employees may elect, change, or waive coverage. Captures enrollment period type (annual open enrollment, new hire window, COBRA election), start and end dates, eligible plan offerings, enrollment method (online portal, paper, EDI 834), and enrollment completion status. Drives eligibility event processing in the enrollment domain.'
}

Table employer.employer_underwriting_case {
  employer_underwriting_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the underwriting case.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the broker facilitating the employer’s enrollment.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employer group that is the subject of the underwriting case.']
  aca_adjustment_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Adjustment applied to ensure compliance with Affordable Care Act rating rules.']
  actuarial_basis_document varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the actuarial documentation supporting the rating.']
  age_gender_composite_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Composite factor derived from the group’s age and gender distribution.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the underwriting case, used for tracking and communication.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the underwriting case record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the premium estimate.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Last day the proposed premium rates are valid; after this date a new quote is required.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'First day the proposed premium rates become effective if the quote is accepted.']
  experience_rating_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Factor based on the employer’s prior loss experience and credibility weighting.']
  geographic_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Factor reflecting cost differentials across geographic rating areas.']
  group_average_age decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average age of covered members in the group.']
  group_census_size varchar [note: 'Number of covered lives in the employer group at the time of underwriting.']
  group_female_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of female members in the group census.']
  industry_risk_factor varchar [note: 'Code representing the industry classification risk factor applied to the group.']
  manual_rate_basis boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rate was manually set rather than algorithmically derived.']
  pmpm_estimate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated premium cost per covered member per month.']
  prior_carrier_loss_experience varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of loss experience with the previous carrier.']
  quote_expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quote expires if not accepted.']
  quote_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the premium quote was generated.']
  quote_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the premium quote within the underwriting workflow.']
  rating_area_code varchar [note: 'Geographic rating area identifier used for regional premium adjustments.']
  rating_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate rates: community rated, experience rated, or blended.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the employer group based on underwriting analysis.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the underwriting case was initially submitted.']
  total_premium_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate estimated premium for the entire employer group for the quoted period.']
  underwriting_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision outcome for the case after review.']
  underwriting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the underwriting case.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the underwriting case record.']

  Note: 'Underwriting evaluation record and single source of truth for the complete risk assessment process from submission through rating through quote generation through decision for a new or renewing employer group. Tracks case submission date, group census data, prior carrier loss experience, medical underwriting status, risk tier assignment, manual rate basis; rating factors including age/gender composite factors, geographic area rating factors, industry risk factors, experience rating credibility weights, ACA-compliant rating adjustments, and actuarial basis documentation; underwriting decision (approved, declined, modified); and premium rate quotes with proposed rates by plan, coverage tier, and rating area, quote effective and expiration dates, rating methodology (community rated, experience rated, blended), proposed PMPM rates, total group premium estimate, and quote status (draft, presented, accepted, expired). Consolidates rating factors and rate quotes as stages within the underwriting case lifecycle. Links to the risk domain for RAF and HCC-based risk scoring at the group level. Supports the sales and renewal pipeline.'
}

Table employer.rate_quote {
  rate_quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the premium rate quote.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the broker or sales agent handling the quote.']
  employer_underwriting_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_underwriting_case. Business justification: A rate quote is the commercial output of an underwriting evaluation. One employer_underwriting_case can produce multiple rate quotes (e.g., different plan configurations, quote versions, or revised qu']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.fee_schedule. Business justification: Actuarial rate quoting for employer groups uses contracted fee schedules as the pricing basis for pmpm_rate and total_premium_estimate calculations. Underwriters and actuaries must trace which fee sch']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group for which the quote is generated.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan to which the quote applies.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Rate quotes are generated for a specific provider network configuration — network type (HMO/PPO/EPO), tier structure, and geographic footprint directly drive premium calculations. Actuaries require di']
  contribution_strategy varchar [note: 'Employer contribution approach (e.g., fixed amount, percentage of premium).']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'Tier of coverage being quoted (e.g., employee only, family).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the quote (e.g., USD).']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of any discounts applied to the gross premium.']
  effective_date date [note: 'First date on which the quoted rates become effective if the quote is accepted.']
  erisa_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the employer group is subject to ERISA regulations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the quote is no longer valid.']
  gross_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total premium before any discounts or adjustments, expressed in the quote currency.']
  group_sic_code varchar [note: 'Standard Industrial Classification code describing the employer\'s industry.']
  group_size varchar [note: 'Total number of employees eligible for coverage under the employer group.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Funding arrangement for the group: Administrative Services Only (ASO) or Fully Insured.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the quote was formally issued to the employer.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Number of covered lives in the employer group for the quoted period.']
  net_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final premium after discounts, the amount the employer is expected to pay.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special conditions.']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year to which the quoted plan benefits apply.']
  pmpm_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Proposed premium rate expressed as a per‑member‑per‑month amount.']
  quote_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the quote for tracking and communication with the employer.']
  quote_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the quote, incremented on each revision.']
  rate_quote_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the quote (e.g., draft, presented, accepted, expired, rejected).']
  rating_area varchar [note: 'Geographic rating area used to calculate the premium.']
  rating_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate rates: community, experience, or blended.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date when the current policy term ends and renewal is expected.']
  total_group_premium_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected total premium for the entire employer group based on the quoted rates.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the quote record.']

  Note: 'Premium rate quote issued to a prospective or renewing employer group, capturing proposed rates by plan, coverage tier, and rating area. Includes quote effective date, expiration date, rating methodology (community rated, experience rated, blended), proposed PMPM rates, total group premium estimate, and quote status (draft, presented, accepted, expired). Supports the sales and renewal pipeline.'
}

  commission_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Commission amount paid to the broker.']
  commission_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the commission is paid.']

  start_date date [note: 'Date when the broker relationship began.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of broker\'s address.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Tax ID used for regulatory reporting.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for terminating the broker relationship.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for broker. Referenced by broker_id.'
}

Table contract.provider_contract {
  provider_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the provider contract record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for all contractual agreements between the health plan and providers.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.facility. Business justification: Facility contracts (hospital, outpatient center) are a distinct contracting process from professional provider contracts. Claims adjudication, network adequacy reporting, and facility billing all requ']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule (rate table) governing reimbursement amounts for specific CPT/HCPCS procedure codes under this contract. Applicable for fee-for-service and bundled payment contracts.']
  group_practice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.group_practice. Business justification: Group practices negotiate contracts as legal entities covering all member providers. Provider contracting operations require linking a contract to the group practice to determine which providers are c']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Provider contracts are explicitly plan-specific in health insurance — a provider may hold separate contracts for Medicare Advantage vs. commercial plans. Claims adjudication and contract management re']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider (physician, hospital, clinic, ancillary provider, or DME supplier) who is party to this contract. Sourced from Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource).']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider network that owns or administers this contract (e.g., HMO network, PPO network, EPO network). Determines which network directory listings are governed by this contract.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Effective date of the most recent amendment to this contract. Used to determine which contract terms apply for a given date of service. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the most recent amendment to this contract. Null for original, unamended contracts. Tracks the amendment sequence for audit and compliance purposes. Sourced from Cactus/ProviderSource.']
  aso_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract operates under an Administrative Services Only (ASO) arrangement where the employer self-funds claims and the health plan provides administrative services only. True = ASO arrangement.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract is configured to automatically renew upon reaching its termination date without requiring re-execution. True = auto-renews; False = requires manual renewal action.']
  capitation_rate_pmpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Fixed monthly payment amount per enrolled member (Per Member Per Month - PMPM) paid to the provider under capitation contracts. Applicable only when payment_methodology = capitation. Expressed in USD.']
  carve_out_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specific services or benefit categories are carved out of this contract and managed under a separate arrangement (e.g., behavioral health carve-out, pharmacy carve-out). True = carve-outs exist.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the contract as recorded in the Provider Management System. Used for display in contracting workflows, provider communications, and reporting dashboards.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable contract identifier assigned by the health plan contracting department. Used in correspondence, amendments, and provider communications. Sourced from Cactus/ProviderSource contract module.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the provider contract. Drives eligibility verification, claims adjudication routing, and provider directory inclusion. Values: draft, pending_execution, active, suspended, terminated, expired, under_amendment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_execution|active|suspended|terminated|expired|under_amendment — promote to reference product]']
  contract_tier varchar [note: 'Tiering classification assigned to this contract, reflecting the provider\'s preferred status and associated member cost-sharing levels. Tier 1/preferred providers typically have lower member cost-sharing. Used in benefit design and member-facing provider directory.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contractual arrangement by payment and relationship structure. Determines reimbursement methodology and applicable rate tables. Values: fee_for_service (FFS), capitation, bundled_payment, value_based_care (VBC/ACO), letter_of_agreement (LOA), single_case_agreement (SCA), out_of_network_gap_fill. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fee_for_service|capitation|bundled_payment|value_based_care|letter_of_agreement|single_case_agreement|out_of_network_gap_fill — promote to reference product]']
  cpt_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending CPT or HCPCS procedure code defining the upper bound of the service scope covered under this contract. Used with cpt_range_start to define the range of covered procedures.']
  cpt_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting CPT or HCPCS procedure code defining the lower bound of the service scope covered under this contract. Used with cpt_range_end to define the range of covered procedures. Sourced from AMA CPT code set.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this provider contract record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  credentialing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider must maintain active credentialing status as a condition of this contract. True = credentialing is a contractual requirement. Linked to NCQA credentialing standards.']
  delegation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether credentialing, utilization management, or other health plan functions have been delegated to the provider organization under this contract. True = delegation arrangement exists. Requires NCQA delegation oversight.']
  drg_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending Diagnosis Related Group (DRG) code defining the upper bound of inpatient service scope covered under this contract. Used with drg_range_start to define the range of covered DRGs.']
  drg_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting Diagnosis Related Group (DRG) code defining the lower bound of inpatient service scope covered under this contract. Applicable for hospital contracts with DRG-based payment methodology.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract becomes legally binding and reimbursement terms take effect. Used in claims adjudication to determine applicable rate schedule. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date on which all parties signed and the contract was fully executed. May differ from effective_date for retroactive or prospective contracts. Sourced from Cactus/ProviderSource contract module.']
  lob_applicability varchar [note: 'Line of Business (LOB) to which this contract applies. Determines which member populations and benefit plans are governed by this contract\'s reimbursement terms. Values: commercial, medicare_advantage, medicaid, marketplace, cobra, tricare, all. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|medicare_advantage|medicaid|marketplace|cobra|tricare|all — promote to reference product]']
  npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the contracting provider as assigned by CMS NPPES. 10-digit unique identifier used in EDI transactions (837/835) and claims adjudication to identify the contracted provider.']
  payment_methodology varchar [note: 'Specific reimbursement calculation methodology governing how the health plan pays the provider under this contract. Distinct from contract_type in that it specifies the payment calculation approach. Examples: fee_for_service (FFS), capitation (PMPM), per_diem, case_rate, DRG-based, bundled, Reference Based Pricing (RBP), global_budget, salary. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fee_for_service|capitation|per_diem|case_rate|drg_based|bundled|rbp|global_budget|salary — promote to reference product]']
  provider_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contracted provider entity type. Drives applicable reimbursement rules, credentialing requirements, and service scope definitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: physician|hospital|clinic|ancillary|dme_supplier|behavioral_health|skilled_nursing|home_health|laboratory|radiology — promote to reference product]']
  quality_incentive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract includes quality-based incentive payments tied to HEDIS, STAR ratings, CAHPS, or other quality performance measures. True = quality incentive provisions exist in the contract.']
  raf_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) / Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) coding obligations apply to the provider under this contract. True = provider has contractual HCC coding and encounter data submission requirements.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to the termination date by which either party must provide written notice of intent not to renew. Contractually mandated notice period for non-renewal. Typically 30, 60, or 90 days.']
  revenue_code_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending UB-04 revenue code defining the upper bound of facility service categories covered under this contract. Used with revenue_code_range_start to define the range of covered revenue codes.']
  revenue_code_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting UB-04 revenue code defining the lower bound of facility service categories covered under this contract. Used for hospital and facility contracts to define covered service lines.']
  risk_sharing_model varchar [note: 'Specifies the financial risk-sharing arrangement between the health plan and provider under VBC or ACO contracts. Determines how savings and losses are allocated. Values: none, shared_savings, shared_risk, full_risk, upside_only, downside_only.']
  service_exclusion_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of specific services, procedures, or conditions explicitly excluded from the scope of this contract. Supplements the CPT/DRG/revenue code ranges with narrative exclusion details.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this contract record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Provider Management System and Core Administration Platform.']
  source_system_contract_reference varchar [note: 'Native contract identifier from the originating operational system (Cactus, ProviderSource, Facets, or QNXT). Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Federal Tax Identification Number (TIN) or Employer Identification Number (EIN) of the contracting provider entity. Used for 1099 reporting, payment processing, and IRS compliance. 9-digit numeric value.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the standard contract template used as the basis for this agreement. Enables tracking of which template version governs the contract terms and supports template-level compliance audits.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract ceases to be binding. Null for open-ended contracts. Used to determine provider in-network status for claims with dates of service at or before this date. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance written notice required by either party to terminate the contract for convenience (without cause). Distinct from renewal_notice_days which applies at contract expiry.']
  tpa_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract is administered by a Third Party Administrator (TPA) on behalf of the health plan. True = TPA-administered; False = directly administered by the health plan. Relevant for ASO arrangements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this provider contract record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for incremental load detection and change data capture. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  vbc_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract includes Value-Based Care (VBC) components such as quality bonuses, shared savings, or risk-sharing arrangements beyond standard fee-for-service reimbursement.']

  Note: 'Master record for all contractual agreements between the health plan and providers (physicians, hospitals, clinics, ancillary providers, DME suppliers), including formal contracts, Letters of Agreement (LOAs), Letters of Intent (LOIs), single-case agreements (SCAs), and out-of-network gap-fill arrangements. Captures contract type (fee-for-service, capitation, bundled payment, VBC/ACO, LOA, SCA), contract status, effective and termination dates, line of business (LOB) applicability, TPA/ASO designations, auto-renewal flags, contract tier, owning network, template reference, payment methodology classification, and service scope definitions (covered/excluded service categories, CPT/HCPCS ranges, revenue code ranges, DRG ranges, carve-out indicators, inclusion/exclusion flags). SSOT for all provider reimbursement agreements, interim arrangements, and contracted service scope. Sourced from Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource) and Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT).'
}

Table contract.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each party record in the provider contract domain. Serves as the primary key for all contractual party references across the health plan enterprise.']
  provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider contract this party is bound by. Links the party record to its governing contractual agreement within the contract domain.']
  aco_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this party is an Accountable Care Organization (ACO) participating in a value-based care (VBC) arrangement. Triggers ACO-specific contract terms, shared savings calculations, and CMS reporting requirements.']
  capitation_eligible_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this party is eligible to receive capitation payments (Per Member Per Month - PMPM) under the contract. Drives capitation payment processing and actuarial PMPM rate application.']
  contract_party_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to this party within the contract management system (e.g., Cactus or ProviderSource provider contract number). Used for cross-system reconciliation, EDI transactions, and provider directory publication.']
  credentialing_date date [note: 'Date on which the party was most recently credentialed or re-credentialed by the health plan. NCQA requires re-credentialing every 36 months. Used to trigger re-credentialing workflows and network compliance reporting.']
  credentialing_status varchar [note: 'Current credentialing status of the party as maintained in the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource). Determines whether the party is eligible to provide services to plan members. Credentialing must be current for claims to be adjudicated as in-network.']
  delegation_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this party is a delegated entity authorized to perform functions on behalf of the health plan (e.g., credentialing, utilization management, claims processing). Triggers delegation oversight and audit requirements per NCQA and state DOI standards.']
  delegation_scope varchar [note: 'Specifies the functional scope of delegation authority granted to this party. Applicable only when delegation_indicator is true. Defines which health plan functions the delegated entity is authorized to perform on behalf of the payer.']
  doing_business_as_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or DBA name under which the party operates, if different from the legal entity name. Commonly used for group practices and hospital systems that operate under a brand name distinct from their legal registration.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this party\'s participation in the contract becomes effective. Drives claims adjudication eligibility windows, network directory publication, and provider credentialing timelines.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the contracting party as registered with state or federal authorities. Used for contract execution, credentialing, and regulatory reporting. May be an individual provider name, group practice name, IPA, ACO, or hospital system name.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the party\'s primary state professional or business license expires. Triggers credentialing re-verification workflows and may result in contract suspension if not renewed. Critical for NCQA and URAC credentialing compliance.']
  license_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation where the party holds its primary professional or business license. Used for network adequacy reporting, credentialing, and state-specific regulatory compliance.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Health plan line of business under which this party is contracted. Determines applicable regulatory requirements, reimbursement methodologies, and network adequacy standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|medicare_advantage|medicaid|marketplace|cobra|fehb — promote to reference product]']
  malpractice_coverage_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party maintains active professional liability (malpractice) insurance coverage as required by the contract. Failure to maintain coverage may trigger contract suspension or termination per NCQA credentialing standards.']
  malpractice_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the party\'s professional liability (malpractice) insurance policy expires. Triggers credentialing re-verification and contract compliance monitoring workflows.']
  network_participation_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the party is a participating (PAR) in-network provider, a non-participating (non-PAR) provider, or an out-of-network provider. Drives member cost-sharing calculations, claims adjudication rules, and EOB generation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing supplemental information about the party that does not fit structured attributes. May include contract negotiation notes, special arrangement details, or credentialing exceptions. Classified as confidential due to potential inclusion of sensitive business information.']
  npi varchar [note: 'The 10-digit National Provider Identifier assigned by CMS to uniquely identify the provider party. Type 1 NPI for individual providers; Type 2 NPI for organizational providers. Required for all HIPAA-covered transactions including 837 claims and 270/271 eligibility inquiries.']
  npi_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the NPI is Type 1 (individual/solo practitioner) or Type 2 (organizational entity such as group practice, hospital, or IPA). Determines credentialing requirements and contract structure.']
  oig_exclusion_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party appears on the OIG List of Excluded Individuals/Entities (LEIE). Excluded parties are prohibited from participating in Medicare, Medicaid, and other federal healthcare programs. Checked monthly per CMS compliance requirements.']
  party_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of this party within the contract relationship. Identifies whether the party is the health plan (payer), a contracting provider, a delegated entity, an Independent Practice Association (IPA), an Accountable Care Organization (ACO), a hospital system, or a group practice. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: payer|provider|delegated_entity|ipa|aco|hospital_system|group_practice — promote to reference product]']
  party_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the party within the contract. Indicates whether the party is actively bound by the contract, pending execution, suspended, terminated, or inactive. Drives eligibility verification and claims adjudication routing.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the party as an individual (natural person, e.g., solo practitioner with individual NPI) or an organization (legal entity, e.g., group practice, hospital, IPA, ACO). Drives NPI type assignment and credentialing workflow.']
  primary_specialty_code varchar [note: 'NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code representing the party\'s primary clinical specialty. Used for network adequacy analysis, claims routing, prior authorization rules, and provider directory classification.']
  raf_applicable_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) scoring applies to this party\'s contract arrangement. Relevant for Medicare Advantage and ACA marketplace contracts where Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) risk scores drive capitation payments.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this party record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this party record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change data capture, audit trail, and SCD Type 2 processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  recredentialing_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the party must complete re-credentialing to maintain active network participation. Calculated as 36 months from the credentialing_date per NCQA standards. Drives automated re-credentialing workflow initiation.']
  sanction_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has an active sanction, exclusion, or adverse action recorded by OIG, SAM, or state licensing boards. A true value triggers contract suspension review and claims payment hold per CMS and HIPAA compliance requirements.']
  service_area_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation representing the primary state in which this party provides services under the contract. Used for network adequacy reporting, state regulatory compliance, and geographic claims routing.']
  service_area_zip_code varchar [note: 'Primary ZIP code of the party\'s principal service location. Used for network adequacy geo-mapping, member-to-provider distance calculations, and CMS network adequacy submissions.']
  signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized individual who signed or has authority to sign the contract on behalf of this party. Required for contract execution validation and legal enforceability. Typically the CEO, CMO, or designated contracting officer.']
  signatory_title varchar [note: 'Official title or position of the authorized signatory (e.g., Chief Executive Officer, Medical Director, Contracting Officer). Validates the authority level of the individual executing the contract on behalf of the party.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this party record originated (e.g., Cactus, ProviderSource, Facets, QNXT). Supports data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and ETL audit in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_party_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this party record in the originating operational system (e.g., Cactus provider ID, ProviderSource contract party key). Enables cross-system reconciliation and traceability from the Silver Layer back to the system of record.']
  state_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued professional or business license number for the contracting party. Required for credentialing verification and network participation. For individual providers, this is the state medical license; for organizations, the state business license or certificate of authority.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which this party\'s participation in the contract ends. Null for open-ended or currently active parties. Used to determine network participation windows, claims payment eligibility, and member notification requirements.']
  tin varchar [note: 'Federal Tax Identification Number (EIN or SSN) used for payment processing, 1099 reporting, and IRS compliance. Critical for remittance and premium payment routing. Stored as 9-digit numeric string without hyphens.']
  vbc_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the payment methodology or value-based care arrangement type applicable to this party under the contract. Drives reimbursement calculation logic, risk-sharing model application, and financial reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fee_for_service|capitation|bundled_payment|shared_savings|global_risk — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Identifies all parties bound by a provider contract — the health plan entity, the contracting provider (individual NPI or organizational NPI), group practice, IPA, ACO, or hospital system. Tracks party role (payer, provider, delegated entity), NPI, TIN, legal entity name, signatory authority, and party effective dates. Supports multi-party and delegated contracting arrangements.'
}

Table contract.term {
  term_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a contract term record in the provider contract management system (Facets/QNXT or Provider Management System). Serves as the primary key for the term entity.']
  amendment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract amendment that introduced or modified this term, if applicable. Null if the term originates from the base contract rather than an amendment.']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule associated with this term when payment_methodology is fee_for_service. Links to the rate table governing allowed amounts for procedure codes under this term.']
  provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent provider contract to which this term belongs. Links the term to its governing contract in the Provider Network Management system.']
  audit_rights_period_years varchar [note: 'The number of years the health plan retains the right to audit provider records under this audit rights term. Typically 7 years per HIPAA and CMS requirements. Applicable when term_type is \'audit_rights\'.']
  auto_renew boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term automatically renews upon expiration without requiring renegotiation (True). Supports contract lifecycle management and renewal notification workflows.']
  capitation_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'The Per Member Per Month (PMPM) capitation rate defined by this term when payment_methodology is capitation. Represents the fixed monthly payment per enrolled member to the provider regardless of services rendered. Classified confidential as proprietary pricing.']
  compliance_checkpoint_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next compliance review or audit checkpoint associated with this term. Used by the compliance team to track regulatory obligations, CMS audit readiness, and DOI examination preparation.']
  cpt_range_end varchar [note: 'The ending CPT or HCPCS procedure code of the range to which this reimbursement rule term applies. Used in conjunction with cpt_range_start to define the applicable procedure code range for adjudication.']
  cpt_range_start varchar [note: 'The starting CPT or HCPCS procedure code of the range to which this reimbursement rule term applies. Used in conjunction with cpt_range_end to define the applicable procedure code range for adjudication.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contract term record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record retention requirements.']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'The contractually agreed method for resolving disputes arising under this term: Arbitration, Mediation, Litigation, or Internal Review. Applicable for hold-harmless, indemnification, and audit rights terms.']
  drg_grouper_version varchar [note: 'The DRG grouper version (e.g., \'MS-DRG v41.0\', \'APR-DRG v39\') applicable to this term when payment_methodology is \'drg_based\'. Ensures adjudication uses the correct DRG weight tables for inpatient reimbursement.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this contract term becomes legally binding and operationally enforceable. Used to determine applicability during claims adjudication in Facets/QNXT.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this contract term ceases to be binding. Null for open-ended terms with no defined end date. Used in adjudication date-range checks.']
  governing_state varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code whose laws govern this contract term. Determines applicable state DOI regulations, insurance code requirements, and state-specific provider contract mandates.']
  hipaa_baa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term constitutes or requires a HIPAA Business Associate Agreement (BAA) (True). Triggers compliance tracking and ensures Protected Health Information (PHI) handling obligations are documented per HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.']
  icd_version varchar [note: 'Specifies the ICD diagnosis coding version applicable to this term when the term references diagnosis-based reimbursement rules or Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) risk adjustment provisions.']
  incentive_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum dollar amount of quality incentive payment available to the provider under this term upon meeting quality_metric_threshold. Applicable for VBC, ACO, and pay-for-performance contract terms. Classified confidential as proprietary financial terms.']
  is_adjudication_rule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term directly drives claims adjudication logic in Facets/QNXT (True). When True, the term\'s reimbursement_policy_subtype, cpt_range_start/end, and reduction_percentage are loaded into the adjudication engine.']
  is_negotiable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract term is open to negotiation (True) or is standard non-negotiable boilerplate language (False). Supports contract negotiation workflow and template management.']
  is_standard_boilerplate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term is sourced from the organization\'s standard contract template library (True) or is a custom-negotiated term specific to this contract (False). Supports template governance and deviation tracking.']
  lob_applicability varchar [note: 'Specifies which Line of Business (LOB) this contract term applies to. Enables selective application of reimbursement rules and contractual obligations across Commercial, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, Marketplace (ACA/QHP), or all lines of business.']
  mac_pricing_basis varchar [note: 'The drug pricing benchmark used for pharmacy reimbursement terms: Average Wholesale Price (AWP), Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC), Maximum Allowable Cost (MAC), National Average Drug Acquisition Cost (NADAC), or a custom contracted rate. Applicable for terms covering pharmacy benefits.']
  payment_methodology varchar [note: 'The reimbursement payment methodology defined by this term when term_type is \'payment_methodology\' or \'reimbursement_rule\'. Drives adjudication logic in Facets/QNXT. Includes Fee-for-Service (FFS), Capitation, Bundled Payment, Case Rate, Per Diem, Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)-based, Reference Based Pricing (RBP), and Value-Based Care (VBC). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fee_for_service|capitation|bundled_payment|case_rate|per_diem|drg_based|rbp|vbc — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pos_code varchar [note: 'The CMS Place of Service (POS) code to which this term\'s reimbursement differential applies, when reimbursement_policy_subtype is \'pos_differential\'. Two-digit code per CMS POS code set (e.g., \'11\' for Office, \'21\' for Inpatient Hospital).']
  quality_metric_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum quality performance threshold (expressed as a percentage or score) that the provider must achieve to qualify for incentive payments or avoid penalties under quality incentive terms. Linked to HEDIS or STAR measures.']
  reduction_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage reduction applied to the allowed amount under this reimbursement rule term (e.g., 50.00 for a 50% reduction on secondary procedures under multiple procedure reduction policy). Applicable when reimbursement_policy_subtype is set. Classified confidential as it represents proprietary pricing.']
  reimbursement_policy_subtype varchar [note: 'Granular sub-classification of the reimbursement rule when term_type is \'reimbursement_rule\'. Specifies the specific adjudication policy: Multiple Procedure Reduction, Bilateral Procedure, Assistant Surgeon, Global Surgery, Unbundling Rules, Place-of-Service (POS) Differential, or Modifier Impact. Directly maps to adjudication logic in Facets/QNXT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: multiple_procedure_reduction|bilateral_procedure|assistant_surgeon|global_surgery|unbundling|pos_differential|modifier_impact — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days prior to expiration_date by which either party must provide notice of intent not to renew this term. Applicable when auto_renew is True.']
  risk_sharing_model varchar [note: 'Defines the risk-sharing arrangement under Value-Based Care (VBC) or Accountable Care Organization (ACO) contract terms. Specifies whether the provider participates in shared savings only (upside), shared risk (two-sided), or full risk (global capitation).']
  source_system_term_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this term record in the originating operational system (e.g., Cactus contract clause ID, QNXT reimbursement rule ID, or ProviderSource term reference). Supports data lineage and reconciliation with source systems.']
  summary varchar [note: 'Plain-language operational summary of the term\'s intent and impact, authored by the contracting team. Used for training, audits, and quick reference without reading full legal text.']
  term_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable external identifier for the term, typically formatted as a section or clause reference (e.g., \'TERM-2024-0042\' or \'Section 4.2.1\'). Used in contract documents and correspondence.']
  term_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract term. \'active\' indicates the term is currently in force; \'superseded\' indicates it has been replaced by an amendment; \'expired\' indicates the effective period has lapsed.']
  term_text varchar [note: 'Full verbatim legal text of the contract term as negotiated and executed. Contains the binding contractual language. Classified confidential as it represents proprietary reimbursement and legal obligations.']
  term_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the contract term defining its legal or operational purpose. Drives downstream adjudication logic and compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reimbursement_rule|payment_methodology|exclusivity|termination_clause|audit_rights|hold_harmless|indemnification|confidentiality|hipaa_baa|quality_incentive — promote to reference product]']
  termination_cause_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the basis under which termination is permitted by this term: For Cause (material breach, fraud, license revocation), Without Cause (convenience), Mutual Agreement, or Regulatory (required by law or CMS/DOI directive).']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days of advance written notice required to terminate the contract under this termination clause term. Applicable when term_type is \'termination_clause\'. Typically 30, 60, or 90 days per regulatory requirements.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the contract term as it appears in the contract document (e.g., \'Multiple Procedure Reduction Policy\', \'Termination for Cause Clause\'). Supports search and display in contract management UIs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contract term record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  withhold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of reimbursement withheld from provider payments pending quality performance or risk settlement under VBC or capitation arrangements. Classified confidential as proprietary financial terms.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific terms, conditions, obligations, and reimbursement policies within a provider contract. Captures term type (payment methodology, exclusivity, termination clause, audit rights, hold-harmless, indemnification, confidentiality, HIPAA BAA, reimbursement rule), term text, effective date, expiration date, and whether the term is negotiable or standard boilerplate. For reimbursement rule terms: captures policy sub-type (multiple procedure reduction, bilateral procedure, assistant surgeon, global surgery, unbundling rules, place-of-service differential), applicable CPT/HCPCS ranges, reduction percentage, and LOB applicability. Enables granular tracking of contractual obligations, compliance checkpoints, and reimbursement adjudication rules. Bridges contract terms to claims adjudication logic in Facets/QNXT.'
}

Table contract.amendment {
  amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each provider contract amendment record. Primary key for the amendment entity.']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule record activated or superseded by this amendment. Links the amendment to the specific rate table used for claims adjudication under the amended terms.']
  provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent provider contract that this amendment modifies. Links the amendment to its originating contract record.']
  superseded_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a prior amendment record that this amendment replaces or supersedes. Enables traversal of the full amendment history chain for a given contract. Null if this is the first amendment or does not supersede a prior amendment.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: Contract amendments frequently change tier assignments or tier-specific rates. amendment.network_tier is a denormalized string. Linking to network.tier normalizes this and enables precise tracking of ']
  vbc_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated Accountable Care Organization (ACO) agreement when this amendment relates to an ACO or Value-Based Care (VBC) arrangement. Nullable for non-ACO amendments.']
  vbc_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vbc_contract. Business justification: amendment has vbc_target_metric (STRING) and amendment_effective_date-style attributes that indicate amendments can specifically modify VBC contract terms. The existing amendment.vbc_arrangement_id po']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable sequential identifier for the amendment (e.g., AMD-2024-001234-001). Used in correspondence, audit trails, and regulatory submissions. Unique within the parent contract.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the amendment indicating the nature of the modification. Drives downstream processing workflows and audit categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rate_change|term_modification|lob_addition|network_tier_change|scope_change|termination|capitation_change|bundled_payment_change|vbc_modification|addendum — promote to reference product]']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the amendment through the negotiation and approval workflow. Tracks progression from draft through execution or withdrawal.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the amendment received final internal approval from all required approvers. Distinct from execution date which reflects provider countersignature.']
  capitation_rate_pmpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'The Per Member Per Month (PMPM) capitation rate established or modified by this amendment for capitation-based payment arrangements. Applicable when payment_methodology is capitation. Confidential commercial negotiation data.']
  claims_reprocess_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether claims adjudicated under prior contract terms must be reprocessed following execution of this amendment. Triggers downstream claims reprocessing workflows in Facets/QNXT.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the amendment record was first created in the contract management system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the executed amendment document stored in the contract management document repository (e.g., SharePoint, Cactus/ProviderSource document store). Enables direct access to the legal document.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the terms of this amendment become contractually binding and operationally effective. Used for rate table activation, benefit configuration updates, and claims adjudication rule changes.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which all required parties formally signed and executed the amendment document. May differ from the effective date for retroactive or prospective amendments.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the amendment terms cease to be effective. Nullable for permanent amendments with no defined end date. Used to manage time-limited rate changes or temporary term modifications.']
  formulary_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment affects pharmacy formulary terms, drug reimbursement rates, or Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) arrangements. Triggers updates to the PBM system (RxClaim/Argus).']
  initiating_party varchar [note: 'Identifies which party initiated the amendment request — the health plan, the provider, a regulatory mandate, or a mutual agreement. Used for negotiation analytics and contract management reporting.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal health plan staff notes regarding the amendment, including strategic context, escalation history, or implementation considerations. Confidential internal business information not shared with providers.']
  lob_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the Line of Business (LOB) affected by this amendment (e.g., Commercial, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, CHIP, Exchange/QHP). Critical for segregating contractual terms by regulatory program.']
  negotiation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative capturing key negotiation points, concessions, and outcomes during the amendment negotiation process. Confidential commercial information used for internal reference and future renegotiation strategy.']
  negotiation_outcome varchar [note: 'The result of the contract negotiation process leading to this amendment. Indicates whether terms were mutually agreed, required counter-proposals, or were resolved through arbitration or mediation. Confidential commercial negotiation data.']
  payment_methodology varchar [note: 'The reimbursement methodology established or modified by this amendment. Determines how providers are compensated under the amended contract terms. Aligns with Value-Based Care (VBC), Reference Based Pricing (RBP), capitation, or Fee-For-Service (FFS) models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fee_for_service|capitation|bundled_payment|per_diem|case_rate|vbc|rbp — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  plan_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the authorized representative of the health plan who executed the amendment on behalf of the health plan. Required for legal enforceability of the amendment.']
  prior_auth_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment modifies Prior Authorization (PA) requirements for services rendered by the provider. Triggers updates to Utilization Management (UM) system configurations in InterQual/MCG.']
  provider_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the authorized representative of the provider organization who executed the amendment on behalf of the provider. Required for legal enforceability of the amendment.']
  rate_change_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage increase or decrease in reimbursement rates resulting from this amendment. Expressed as a signed decimal (positive for increases, negative for decreases). Applicable when amendment_type is rate_change. Confidential commercial negotiation data.']
  rate_effective_date date [note: 'The specific date on which revised reimbursement rates established by this amendment take effect in the claims adjudication system. May differ from the overall amendment effective date for phased implementations.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the business rationale or triggering event for the amendment (e.g., annual rate renegotiation, regulatory mandate, network expansion, provider request). Supports audit and compliance documentation.']
  regulatory_mandate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment was required by a regulatory mandate (e.g., CMS rate update, state DOI directive, ACA compliance requirement). Drives prioritization and compliance reporting workflows.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific regulation, CMS bulletin, state DOI directive, or legislative act that mandated or triggered this amendment (e.g., \'CMS-2024-MA-Rate-Update\', \'ACA Section 1302\'). Populated when regulatory_mandate_flag is true.']
  retroactive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the amendment has a retroactive effective date that precedes the execution date. Retroactive amendments require claims reprocessing and financial reconciliation activities.']
  retroactive_start_date date [note: 'The earliest date from which retroactive amendment terms apply, when retroactive_flag is true. Defines the claims reprocessing window for financial reconciliation.']
  risk_share_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of financial risk shared between the health plan and the provider under risk-sharing or Value-Based Care (VBC) arrangements established by this amendment. Confidential commercial negotiation data.']
  sections_modified varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list or narrative description of the specific contract sections, exhibits, or schedules modified by this amendment (e.g., \'Exhibit A - Fee Schedule, Section 4.2 - Term\'). Supports targeted audit and compliance review.']
  submitted_date date [note: 'The date on which the amendment was formally submitted for internal review and approval. Marks the start of the amendment approval workflow.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title summarizing the purpose of the amendment (e.g., \'2024 Annual Rate Increase — Hospital Services\'). Used in contract management dashboards and correspondence.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the amendment record was most recently modified in the contract management system. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  vbc_target_metric varchar [note: 'The primary quality or cost performance metric targeted under a Value-Based Care (VBC) or Accountable Care Organization (ACO) arrangement established by this amendment (e.g., HEDIS measure, readmission rate, cost per episode). Applicable when payment_methodology is vbc.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version counter tracking revisions to the amendment record during the drafting and negotiation process. Supports amendment history and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Tracks all formal amendments, addenda, and modifications to an existing provider contract. Captures amendment number, amendment type (rate change, term modification, LOB addition, network tier change), effective date, reason for amendment, negotiation outcome, approval status, and the specific contract sections modified. Maintains full amendment history for audit and compliance purposes.'
}

Table contract.fee_schedule {
  fee_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the fee schedule record in the contract domain. Primary key for the fee_schedule data product.']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: Fee schedules in health insurance are scoped to specific benefit packages — a Gold PPO benefit package carries different allowed amounts than a Bronze HMO. Claims adjudication requires selecting the c']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.facility. Business justification: Facility-specific fee schedules (DRG base rates, per diem rates, facility fees) are standard in health insurance. Claims adjudication requires resolving the applicable fee schedule for a specific faci']
  group_practice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.group_practice. Business justification: Group practices negotiate fee schedules that apply to all member providers. Rate configuration and claims adjudication require resolving the group-level fee schedule; fee_schedule has provider_id for ']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific health plan or benefit plan to which this fee schedule is scoped. Enables plan-level fee schedule assignment in Facets/QNXT Benefits Configuration.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider or provider group for whom this fee schedule defines reimbursement rates. Sourced from the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource).']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider network (e.g., HMO, PPO, EPO, POS) to which this fee schedule applies. Determines in-network vs. out-of-network reimbursement applicability.']
  superseded_by_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee_schedule_id of the newer fee schedule that replaced this one when schedule_status is \'superseded\'. Enables fee schedule version chain navigation and historical rate lookups.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: Fee schedules are tier-differentiated in health insurance — Tier 1 providers receive higher reimbursement rates than Tier 2. Claims adjudication and rate lookup require knowing which tier a fee schedu']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: Fee schedules are versioned annually in health insurance — annual rate negotiations, CMS fee schedule submissions, and regulatory rate filings all require explicit plan year association. Actuarial and']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the contract amendment that introduced or modified this fee schedule version. Null for the original fee schedule. Enables traceability between fee schedule versions and contract amendments.']
  anesthesia_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fee schedule includes anesthesia service reimbursement, which uses base units plus time units multiplied by a conversion factor rather than standard CPT-based rates.']
  anesthesia_conversion_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Dollar amount per anesthesia unit (base units + time units) used to calculate anesthesia reimbursement when anesthesia_applicable is True. Negotiated rate specific to this fee schedule.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the fee schedule was formally approved by the authorized contracting officer or governance body. Required for audit and regulatory compliance.']
  cms_fee_schedule_year varchar [note: 'The CMS Medicare fee schedule calendar year used as the basis or benchmark for this fee schedule when fee_schedule_source is \'cms_medicare\'. Enables tracking of which CMS annual update cycle the rates are derived from.']
  contract_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider contract under which this fee schedule is established. Links the fee schedule to its governing contractual agreement.']
  conversion_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Dollar amount multiplied by the total Relative Value Units (RVUs) to calculate the reimbursement amount for RVU-based fee schedules. Applicable when payment_basis is \'rvu_based\'. Sourced from CMS RBRVS conversion factor or negotiated contract rate.']
  coordination_of_benefits_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Coordination of Benefits (COB) rules apply to claims adjudicated under this fee schedule. When True, COB logic determines primary vs. secondary payer responsibilities.']
  cpt_code_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending CPT procedure code in the range of services covered by this fee schedule. Used in conjunction with cpt_code_range_start to define the scope of applicable procedure codes.']
  cpt_code_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting CPT procedure code in the range of services covered by this fee schedule. Used in conjunction with cpt_code_range_end to define the scope of applicable procedure codes. Null if the fee schedule applies to all CPT codes or uses ICD/DRG-based scoping.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fee schedule record was first created in the system. Audit field capturing the initial record creation event in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  drg_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fee schedule uses Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)-based reimbursement for inpatient facility claims. When True, DRG weights and base rates govern payment rather than individual procedure codes.']
  drg_base_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'The negotiated base dollar rate per DRG weight unit used for inpatient facility reimbursement when drg_applicable is True. Multiplied by the DRG relative weight to calculate the total inpatient payment.']
