The procedure with rank 1 is reimbursed at 100%; subsequent ranks receive the reduction percentage. Applies to multiple procedure reduction policies.']
  provider_specialty_code varchar [note: 'CMS or NUCC provider specialty code restricting this reimbursement policy to a specific provider specialty (e.g., 02=General Surgery, 93=Radiation Oncology). Null indicates the policy applies to all specialties. Used for specialty-specific reimbursement rules.']
  reduction_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by which the allowed amount is reduced when this policy is triggered during claims adjudication. For example, a value of 50.00 indicates the secondary procedure is reimbursed at 50% of the contracted rate. Applies to policies such as multiple procedure reduction and assistant surgeon.']
  regulatory_mandate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reimbursement policy is mandated by a federal or state regulatory authority (e.g., CMS NCCI edits, state prompt payment laws). Regulatory mandate policies cannot be waived or overridden without regulatory approval.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation to the specific regulatory rule, CMS manual chapter, state statute, or contractual clause that mandates or governs this reimbursement policy (e.g., \'CMS NCCI Chapter 4, Section 4.2\', \'42 CFR 422.520\'). Populated when regulatory_mandate_flag is True.']
  retroactive_adjustment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reimbursement policy can be applied retroactively to previously adjudicated claims when the policy is newly activated or amended. When True, claims reprocessing may be triggered for the applicable service date range.']
  revenue_code_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending UB-04 revenue code in the range to which this reimbursement policy applies for facility claims. Used in conjunction with revenue_code_range_start to define applicable revenue code ranges for institutional claim adjudication.']
  revenue_code_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting UB-04 revenue code in the range to which this reimbursement policy applies for facility claims. Used in conjunction with revenue_code_range_end to define applicable revenue code ranges for institutional claim adjudication.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the reimbursement policy expires and is no longer applied to new claims. Null indicates an open-ended policy with no defined end date. Claims with service dates after this date are not subject to this policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reimbursement policy record was last modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Defines payer reimbursement policies that govern how claims are adjudicated against contracted rates. Captures policy type (multiple procedure reduction, bilateral procedure, assistant surgeon, global surgery, unbundling rules, place-of-service differential), applicable CPT/HCPCS ranges, reduction percentage, effective date, and LOB applicability. Bridges contract terms to claims adjudication logic in Facets/QNXT.'
}

Table contract.service_scope {
  service_scope_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service scope record. Primary key.']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: Service scopes define covered services per contract. In health insurance, service scope applicability is determined at the benefit package level — a carve-out for behavioral health may apply to one be']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.facility. Business justification: Facility service scope defines which inpatient/outpatient services are covered under a facility contract (e.g., trauma services, NICU). Claims adjudication and prior authorization require linking serv']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific fee schedule that governs reimbursement rates for services within this scope.']
  practice_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.practice_location. Business justification: Service scope can be site-specific — a provider may be authorized for certain services only at specific locations (e.g., telehealth at one site, surgery at another). Prior authorization and claims adj']
  provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent provider contract under which this service scope is defined.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider to whom this service scope applies.']
  superseded_by_service_scope_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service scope record that supersedes this one, used to maintain historical lineage when scope definitions are updated or replaced.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: Service scope (included/excluded services, prior auth requirements) is tier-differentiated in tiered network plans. service_scope.network_tier is a denormalized string. Linking to network.tier normali']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this service scope definition was formally approved and authorized for use in claims adjudication.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or role who approved this service scope definition for inclusion in the contract.']
  carve_out_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this service scope represents a carve-out arrangement where specific services are managed or reimbursed separately from the main contract (e.g., behavioral health carve-out, pharmacy carve-out).']
  coordination_of_benefits_applicable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether coordination of benefits rules apply when a member has multiple coverage sources for services within this scope.']
  cpt_code_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending CPT or HCPCS code in the range of procedure codes covered or excluded by this service scope.']
  cpt_code_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting CPT or HCPCS code in the range of procedure codes covered or excluded by this service scope.']
  drg_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending DRG code in the range of inpatient diagnosis-related groups covered or excluded by this service scope.']
  drg_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting DRG code in the range of inpatient diagnosis-related groups covered or excluded by this service scope.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this service scope becomes active and applicable for claims adjudication and reimbursement.']
  exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Business rationale or regulatory reason for excluding specific services from the contract scope (e.g., experimental treatment, non-covered benefit, carved out to specialty vendor).']
  global_surgery_applicable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether global surgery payment rules apply to surgical procedures within this scope, bundling pre-operative, intra-operative, and post-operative services.']
  icd_code_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending ICD-10 diagnosis code in the range of diagnoses for which services are covered or excluded under this scope.']
  icd_code_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting ICD-10 diagnosis code in the range of diagnoses for which services are covered or excluded under this scope.']
  inclusion_exclusion_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this scope definition specifies services that are included (covered) or excluded (not covered) under the contract.']
  lob varchar [note: 'Line of business to which this service scope applies. Values include commercial, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, ACA Exchange, Administrative Services Only (ASO), Third Party Administrator (TPA), and others.']
  medicare_crossover_applicable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether Medicare crossover claims processing applies for dual-eligible members receiving services within this scope.']
  modifier_applicable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether CPT or HCPCS modifiers are applicable and affect reimbursement for services within this scope.']
  payment_methodology varchar [note: 'Reimbursement method applicable to services within this scope. Values include fee-for-service, capitation, bundled payments, case rate, per diem, value-based care (VBC), and others.']
  place_of_service_code varchar [note: 'Two-digit CMS place of service code indicating where the covered or excluded services may be rendered (e.g., 11 for office, 21 for inpatient hospital, 22 for outpatient hospital).']
  prior_authorization_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether services within this scope require prior authorization before rendering to be eligible for reimbursement.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service scope record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service scope record was last updated in the data warehouse.']
  referral_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a referral from a primary care provider or other designated provider is required for services within this scope.']
  reimbursement_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of a reference fee schedule (e.g., Medicare, billed charges) used to calculate reimbursement for services within this scope. Applicable when payment methodology is percentage-based.']
  revenue_code_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending revenue code in the range of institutional billing codes covered or excluded by this service scope.']
  revenue_code_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting revenue code in the range of institutional billing codes covered or excluded by this service scope.']
  scope_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying this service scope within the contract management system.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the services included or excluded under this scope, including any special conditions, limitations, or clarifications.']
  scope_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or title for this service scope definition.']
  scope_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service scope definition.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the type of healthcare service covered under this scope. Categories include inpatient, outpatient, professional services, ancillary services, behavioral health, Durable Medical Equipment (DME), Skilled Nursing Facility (SNF), home health, hospice, and emergency services.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this service scope definition originated (e.g., Facets, QNXT, ProviderSource).']
  specialty_code varchar [note: 'Provider specialty or taxonomy code to which this service scope applies. Used to limit scope to specific provider types (e.g., cardiology, orthopedics).']
  stop_loss_applicable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether stop-loss provisions apply to high-cost services within this scope, limiting provider or plan financial exposure.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when this service scope ceases to be active. Null indicates open-ended scope.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this service scope definition, incremented with each amendment or update to track changes over time.']

  Note: 'Defines the scope of covered services included or excluded under a specific provider contract. Captures service category (inpatient, outpatient, professional, ancillary, behavioral health, DME, SNF), CPT/HCPCS code ranges, revenue code ranges, DRG ranges, inclusion/exclusion flag, carve-out indicator, and applicable LOB. Determines which services are reimbursable under the contract.'
}

Table contract.delegation_agreement {
  delegation_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delegation agreement record. Primary key.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific health plan product under which delegation is authorized.']
  primary_delegation_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan or entity delegating specific functions to another organization.']
  provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent provider contract under which this delegation agreement is established.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider network associated with this delegation agreement.']
  superseded_by_delegation_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the new delegation agreement that replaces this one when a new version is executed.']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the delegation agreement for business reference and reporting.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the delegation agreement used in contracts and communications.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delegation agreement indicating its operational state.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delegation arrangement based on scope and functions delegated.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the delegation agreement was formally approved by the delegating entity\'s governance or compliance committee.']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the delegating entity conducts audits of the delegate\'s performance and compliance with delegation standards.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delegation agreement automatically renews at the end of its term unless terminated by either party.']
  cms_oversight_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delegation agreement requires Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) oversight and reporting for Medicare or Medicaid programs.']
  delegate_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the authorized representative who signed the delegation agreement on behalf of the delegate entity.']
  delegate_signatory_title varchar [note: 'Title or position of the authorized representative who signed on behalf of the delegate entity.']
  delegated_function_type varchar [note: 'Specific function or set of functions delegated under this agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credentialing|recredentialing|utilization_management|prior_authorization|claims_adjudication|claims_payment|member_services|care_management|disease_management|case_management|pharmacy_benefits|provider_network_management|quality_improvement|appeals_grievances — promote to reference product]']
  delegating_signatory_title varchar [note: 'Title or position of the authorized representative who signed on behalf of the delegating entity.']
  delegation_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope of delegated activities, including specific services, populations, and geographic areas covered.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the executed delegation agreement document stored in the document management system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the delegation agreement becomes binding and the delegate entity assumes delegated responsibilities.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date when the delegation agreement was signed by all parties and became legally binding.']
  financial_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Type of financial arrangement governing payment to the delegate entity for delegated functions.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and comments regarding the delegation agreement for operational reference and compliance tracking.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent delegation audit conducted by the delegating entity.']
  lob_code varchar [note: 'Line of business to which this delegation agreement applies, indicating the member population covered.']
  ncqa_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delegation agreement meets National Committee for Quality Assurance (NCQA) delegation standards required for health plan accreditation.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next delegation audit to ensure ongoing compliance and performance monitoring.']
  oversight_requirement varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the delegating entity\'s oversight responsibilities, monitoring activities, and reporting requirements for the delegate.']
  performance_standard varchar [note: 'Documented performance standards and quality metrics that the delegate entity must meet, including Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set (HEDIS) measures, turnaround times, and accuracy thresholds.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delegation agreement record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delegation agreement record was last modified in the system.']
  regulatory_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the delegation agreement was filed with the applicable regulatory authority.']
  regulatory_filing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delegation agreement must be filed with state Department of Insurance (DOI) or other regulatory bodies.']
  risk_share_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delegation agreement includes risk-sharing arrangements between the delegating entity and delegate.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the delegation agreement ends and delegated functions revert to the delegating entity. Nullable for open-ended agreements.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the delegation agreement.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason for termination of the delegation agreement, including performance issues, regulatory non-compliance, or mutual agreement.']
  urac_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delegation agreement meets URAC delegation standards for health care accreditation.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the delegation agreement to track amendments and revisions over time.']

  Note: 'Manages delegation agreements where the health plan delegates specific functions (credentialing, UM, claims payment, member services) to an IPA, ACO, TPA, or medical group. Captures delegated function type, delegating entity, delegate entity, oversight requirements, audit frequency, performance standards, NCQA/URAC delegation standards compliance, and delegation effective dates. Required for NCQA accreditation and CMS oversight.'
}

Table contract.incentive_arrangement {
  incentive_arrangement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the performance-linked financial arrangement within a provider contract. Primary key.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.facility. Business justification: Hospital quality incentive programs (readmission reduction, HAC bonuses) are facility-level arrangements. Incentive settlement and quality reporting require linking the arrangement to the specific fac']
  group_practice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.group_practice. Business justification: Quality incentive and shared savings arrangements are commonly structured at the group practice level in value-based care. Performance measurement, withhold release, and settlement reporting require l']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Quality incentive arrangements (P4P, shared savings) are plan-specific — HEDIS measure targets and quality thresholds differ by health plan line of business. Quality reporting, incentive payment calcu']
  provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent provider contract under which this incentive arrangement is established.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider or provider group participating in this incentive arrangement.']
  vbc_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vbc_contract. Business justification: incentive_arrangement has vbc_program_name (STRING) which is a denormalized reference to the VBC contract. Adding vbc_contract_id as a proper FK to vbc_contract normalizes this relationship. The VBC p']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: Quality incentive arrangements are measured and settled per plan year — HEDIS measurement periods, quality withhold release, and shared savings calculations are all plan-year-driven. Finance and quali']
  aco_arrangement_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this incentive arrangement is part of an ACO agreement with shared savings and shared loss provisions.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract amendment that introduced or modified this incentive arrangement, if applicable.']
  amount_forfeited decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of withheld funds forfeited by the provider due to failure to meet performance criteria. Expressed in USD.']
  amount_released decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of withheld funds released back to the provider upon meeting performance criteria. Expressed in USD.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the incentive arrangement was formally approved by authorized parties within the health plan.']
  arrangement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the incentive arrangement for business identification and reporting purposes.']
  arrangement_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique identifier for the incentive arrangement, used for external reference and reporting.']
  arrangement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the incentive arrangement indicating whether it is active, pending approval, suspended, terminated, or fully settled.']
  arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the incentive arrangement structure. Defines whether the arrangement is a bonus, withhold, shared savings distribution, performance guarantee, or penalty mechanism.']
  earned_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the provider has earned the incentive or withhold release based on performance evaluation results.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the incentive arrangement becomes active and performance measurement begins.']
  lob_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the line of business (Commercial, Medicare, Medicaid, Exchange) to which this incentive arrangement applies.']
  maximum_incentive_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum financial incentive or bonus amount that can be earned by the provider under this arrangement. Expressed in USD.']
  maximum_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum financial penalty that can be assessed against the provider for non-performance under this arrangement. Expressed in USD.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Detailed description of how the target metric is calculated, including data sources, calculation formulas, exclusions, and measurement period definitions.']
  measurement_period_end date [note: 'End date of the performance measurement period during which provider performance is evaluated against target metrics.']
  measurement_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the performance measurement period during which provider performance is evaluated against target metrics.']
  payout_schedule varchar [note: 'Frequency at which incentive payments or withhold releases are distributed to the provider.']
  performance_threshold_tier varchar [note: 'Tiered performance level definition (e.g., bronze, silver, gold, platinum) that determines incentive payout or withhold release amounts.']
  performance_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year or contract year during which performance is measured for this incentive arrangement.']
  quality_gate_met boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the provider met the minimum quality threshold required for incentive payout or withhold release.']
  quality_measure_set varchar [note: 'Identifies the quality measure framework used for performance evaluation (HEDIS, STAR, CAHPS, custom measures).']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite quality performance score achieved by the provider during the measurement period, expressed as a percentage or index value.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incentive arrangement record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incentive arrangement record was last modified in the system.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which performance reports are generated and shared with the provider for transparency and tracking purposes.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the final financial settlement of the incentive arrangement is completed and all payments or forfeitures are processed.']
  shared_loss_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of cost overruns that will be shared by the provider under a shared risk arrangement. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).']
  shared_savings_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of cost savings that will be shared with the provider under a shared savings arrangement. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).']
  sla_commitment varchar [note: 'Specific service level agreement commitments that the provider must meet as part of this incentive arrangement (e.g., appointment availability, response time, member satisfaction).']
  target_metric varchar [note: 'Specific performance metric or key performance indicator (KPI) that the provider must achieve (e.g., readmission rate, preventive care completion, cost per member per month).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the incentive arrangement ends and performance measurement ceases. Nullable for open-ended arrangements.']
  total_withheld_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount withheld from provider reimbursement during the measurement period. Expressed in USD.']
  withhold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of provider reimbursement withheld for quality, utilization, or risk-sharing purposes. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).']
  withhold_pool_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the withhold pool indicating whether it is for quality performance, utilization management, risk-sharing, or a combined pool.']
  withhold_release_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules and conditions that must be met for withheld amounts to be released to the provider (quality gates, utilization targets, shared risk thresholds).']

  Note: 'Unified record for all performance-linked financial arrangements within provider contracts — including pay-for-performance (P4P) bonuses, quality withholds, risk withholds, shared savings distributions, performance guarantees, SLA commitments, and penalty/incentive structures. Captures arrangement type (bonus, withhold, shared savings, performance guarantee, penalty, SLA), quality measure set (HEDIS, STAR, CAHPS, custom), performance threshold tiers, withhold percentage and release criteria (quality, utilization, shared risk), withhold pool type, target metric and measurement methodology, maximum incentive or penalty amount, total withheld amount, amount released, amount forfeited, measurement period, payout schedule, earned/forfeited status, settlement date, and reporting frequency. Supports VBC quality gate enforcement, HMO/IPA withhold models, pay-for-performance programs, and provider engagement programs. SSOT for all performance-linked financial commitments in provider contracts.'
}

  vbc_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Type of value-based care or alternative payment model applied to this network participation, defining financial incentive structure.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking a provider contract to specific network configurations, defining which provider networks the contract covers. Captures network ID reference, participation tier (tier 1, tier 2, preferred, standard, narrow), in-network effective and termination dates, out-of-network flag, geographic service area, product line applicability (commercial, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, exchange/QHP), and participation status (active, pending, terminated). Bridges the contract domain to the network domain for directory accuracy and claims adjudication network matching.'
}

SSOT for grievance identity and lifecycle in the appeal domain. Supports ACA, Medicare Part C/D CTM, and state DOI grievance reporting requirements.'

  appeal_assigned_to varchar [note: 'Internal team or individual responsible for handling the appeal.']

  completeness_determination varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the appeal package was deemed complete at intake.']

  decision_type varchar [note: 'Outcome of the appeal decision.']
  effective_benefit_change_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit change resulting from the decision becomes effective.']
  expedited_clinical_urgency_basis varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for expedited status (e.g., life‑threatening condition).']
  expedited_trigger boolean [note: 'True if the appeal qualified for expedited processing based on clinical urgency.']
  filing_channel varchar [note: 'Method used to submit the appeal.']
  filing_method varchar [note: 'Technical method of filing (e.g., electronic upload, paper mail).']
  filing_party_type varchar [note: 'Entity that filed the appeal.']
  filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the appeal was received by the plan.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line to which the appeal belongs.']
  member_notification_date date [note: 'Date the member was notified of the appeal outcome.']
  original_claim_number varchar [note: 'External claim number associated with the appealed claim.']
  overturn_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the original benefit determination was overturned, if applicable.']
  provider_notification_date date [note: 'Date the provider was notified of the appeal outcome.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the appeal case record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the appeal case record.']
  regulatory_tier varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the appeal rights.']
  supporting_documentation_checklist varchar [note: 'List of required documents attached to the appeal (e.g., medical records, provider notes).']

  Note: 'Core master record for every formal appeal filed by a member, provider, or authorized representative contesting an adverse benefit determination (ABD). Captures appeal type (first-level internal, second-level internal, expedited, external independent review, state fair hearing), regulatory tier (ACA, ERISA, Medicare Part C/D, Medicaid/CHIP), line-of-business classification, and appeal level tracking for multi-level appeal processes. Includes filing details (channel, filing party type, receipt timestamp, filing method, completeness determination, expedited trigger, supporting documentation checklist), resolution details (decision type — upheld, overturned, partially overturned, withdrawn, dismissed, remanded — decision rationale, clinical criteria applied, decision author NPI or committee ID, decision date, effective date of benefit change, member/provider notification date), and overturn reason. Also tracks expedited appeal attributes (clinical urgency basis, 72-hour clock, treating provider attestation) when applicable. Primary SSOT for appeal identity, filing, resolution, and outcome in the appeal domain. Serves as the binding record of plan decision for regulatory reporting, HEDIS measurement, downstream claim reprocessing triggers, and benefit restoration workflows.'
}

  determination_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the determination.']
  determination_type varchar [note: 'Category of the adverse action issued by the health plan.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'Diagnosis code associated with the service.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the determination record.']

  Note: 'Records every adverse benefit determination (ABD) issued by the health plan — the triggering event for member and provider appeal rights. Links to the originating claim or prior authorization in the claim and utilization domains. Owned by the appeal domain as the formal adverse action record.'
}

  Note: 'Tracks each discrete review action performed on an appeal case — first-level clinical review, second-level peer review, medical director review, and committee review. Supports NCQA and URAC reviewer independence requirements.'
}

Distinct from internal appeal reviews. Supports CMS external review reporting and state DOI compliance.'

  clock_type varchar [note: 'The regulatory framework that defines the appeal\'s timing requirements.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the appeal met its regulatory deadlines.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of remediation steps performed after a breach.']

  sla_breach boolean [note: 'True when actual days exceed target days.']
  sla_target_days varchar [note: 'Number of days defined by the regulatory clock for the required action.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that supplied the timeline data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Facets|QNXT|Cactus|ProviderSource|RxClaim|Argus|InterQual|MCG|Casenet|TruCare|Altruista|Salesforce|Pega|HealthEdge|CustomBilling|OracleFinancials|Milliman — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the record in the originating source system.']

  Note: 'Tracks all regulatory and contractual deadline milestones for each appeal case — acknowledgment due date, decision due date, expedited decision due date, extension notification date, and actual completion dates. Captures regulatory clock type (ACA, ERISA, Medicare Part C/D, Medicaid, state DOI), clock start event, clock pause events, compliance status (on-time, late, extended), and SLA breach details (days overdue, root cause category, corrective action, self-report flag) when milestones are missed. Drives proactive SLA management, regulatory timeliness reporting to CMS and state DOIs, and breach tracking for quality improvement programs.'
}

  storage_path varchar [note: 'Secure storage location reference (e.g., S3 URI).']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the document if multiple revisions exist.']

  Note: 'Manages all documents associated with an appeal or grievance case — member-submitted evidence, medical records, EOB copies, denial letters, acknowledgment letters, decision letters, IRO submissions, hearing transcripts, and peer-to-peer review notes. Tracks document type, document source, receipt date, document format, storage reference (BLOB/S3 path), PHI classification, redaction status, retention period (7-10 years per CMS and state DOI requirements), and whether the document was considered in the review decision. Supports HIPAA PHI handling requirements, regulatory record retention obligations, and litigation hold compliance.'
}

The product has regulatory_basis and regulatory_notice_flag a']
  accessibility_accommodation varchar [note: 'Any accommodation provided for accessibility (e.g., large print, braille, interpreter).']

  attachment_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the communication includes one or more attachments.']

  created_by_user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the user who created the record.']
  delivery_confirmation_date date [note: 'Date on which delivery confirmation was received.']
  delivery_confirmation_flag boolean [note: 'True if delivery of the communication was confirmed by the recipient.']

  escalation_date date [note: 'Date the communication was escalated.']

  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the communication contains confidential or PHI content.']

  language varchar [note: 'Language code of the communication content.']
  last_modified_by_user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the user who last modified the record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form internal notes related to the communication.']

  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the communication record.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework that mandates the communication (e.g., ACA, ERISA, state DOI, CMS, HIPAA).']
  regulatory_notice_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the communication satisfies a specific regulatory notice requirement.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Date by which a response is required from the party.']
  response_received_date date [note: 'Date the response was received.']
  response_received_flag boolean [note: 'True if a response to the communication has been received.']
  returned_mail_date date [note: 'Date the mail was returned to sender.']
  returned_mail_flag boolean [note: 'True if the mailed communication was returned as undeliverable.']
  sent_date date [note: 'Date the communication was sent (applicable for outbound messages).']
  subject varchar [note: 'Subject or title of the communication.']

  Note: 'Records all outbound and inbound communications related to appeal and grievance cases — acknowledgment letters, extension notices, decision letters (NOA/NOAD), Notice of Appeal Rights, member rights notices, requests for additional information, and member/provider responses. Tracks communication type, communication channel (mail, phone, secure portal, fax), sent/received date, recipient type, language, accessibility accommodation (large print, Braille, interpreter), regulatory notice compliance flag, regulatory basis, delivery confirmation, and returned mail tracking. Supports ACA §2719, ERISA §503, and state DOI member notice requirements.'
}

Table appeal.coverage_dispute {
  coverage_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the coverage dispute record.']
  benefit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit. Business justification: Coverage disputes reference a specific benefit being disputed (disputed_benefit_code is a denormalized plain attribute). Replacing with a proper FK to plan.benefit normalizes the model and enables dir']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: Coverage disputes center on which benefit package applies to a member\'s claim or enrollment period. Linking coverage_dispute to benefit_package is essential for benefit-package-level dispute resolutio']
  care_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_enrollment. Business justification: Care program coverage disputes: members dispute whether care management program services are covered under their benefit plan. The coverage dispute must reference the care enrollment to determine prog']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: Coverage disputes arise within the context of a specific appeal case; linking provides direct access to case details.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim that triggered the dispute.']
  cob_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.cob_record. Business justification: coverage_dispute has cob_order and cob_rule_applied attributes indicating COB disputes. Linking to the specific cob_record normalizes the relationship and enables COB dispute resolution teams to acces']
  cobra_election_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.cobra_election. Business justification: COBRA coverage disputes are a distinct, common business process — members dispute election status, premium amounts, or coverage periods. coverage_dispute has cob_order and cob_rule_applied fields indi']
  cost_share_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.cost_share_rule. Business justification: Coverage disputes about member cost-sharing (copay disputes, deductible application, OOP max disputes) require reference to the specific cost_share_rule applied. This is a named dispute type in health']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.facility. Business justification: Coverage disputes frequently involve facility-rendered services — out-of-network facility billing disputes, COB disputes for hospital stays. coverage_dispute has provider_id but no facility_id; facili']
  formulary_drug_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.formulary_drug_tier. Business justification: Formulary tier disputes (member disputes tier 3 vs tier 2 placement) are a primary category of pharmacy coverage disputes. The coverage_dispute record must reference the specific formulary_drug_tier a']
  formulary_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.formulary. Business justification: Formulary exception disputes require knowing which formulary version was in effect when the dispute was filed. coverage_dispute has formulary_exception_flag confirming this scenario. Not all formulary']
  gap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.gap. Business justification: Coverage dispute resolution: members dispute coverage decisions that created care gaps (e.g., denial of a preventive service that is a HEDIS gap). Linking the coverage dispute to the gap enables gap c']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group (if dispute is on behalf of an employer).']
  group_plan_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_plan_offering. Business justification: Coverage disputes are fundamentally adjudicated against the specific terms of a group plan offering — benefit codes, contribution tiers, waiver eligibility, and affordability rules. Resolving disputed']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan under which the dispute is being processed.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Coverage disputes in health insurance frequently contest a specific billed premium line item (e.g., incorrect coverage tier, wrong contribution split). Linking coverage_dispute to the exact invoice_li']
  member_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.member_eligibility_span. Business justification: Coverage disputes frequently challenge eligibility determinations for a specific coverage period. Linking directly to the member eligibility span enables dispute resolution teams to verify coverage da']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member involved in the dispute.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.pa_request. Business justification: Coverage disputes frequently originate from PA denials. Dispute resolution staff must reference the original PA request to assess whether the service was properly evaluated. This link supports the cov']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.policy. Business justification: Coverage disputes are inherently about specific policy terms — deductibles, out-of-pocket limits, coverage type. Direct policy_id on coverage_dispute is essential for dispute resolution, benefit adjud']
  premium_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_payment. Business justification: Coverage disputes frequently contest whether a specific premium payment was correctly applied (e.g., payment misapplied to wrong period, NSF dispute). Linking coverage_dispute to the specific premium_']
  prior_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior authorization associated with the disputed service.']
  provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_provider_contract. Business justification: Coverage disputes frequently arise from specific provider contract terms (in-network status, service scope, payment methodology). Direct FK enables contract performance reporting and dispute resolutio']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider involved in the dispute.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Coverage disputes may trigger regulatory reporting obligations; linking captures the governing obligation for each dispute.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: Coverage disputes about tier-based cost-sharing differentials (member billed Tier 2 rates, claims Tier 1 applies) are a standard health insurance dispute type. coverage_dispute.tier_id directly identi']
  appeal_deadline date [note: 'Last date by which an appeal must be filed.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date the appeal was submitted.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the appeal process.']
  cob_order varchar [note: 'Ordered list of payer responsibilities for the claim (e.g., primary|secondary|tertiary).']
  cob_rule_applied varchar [note: 'COB rule used to determine payer order for the dispute.']
  coordination_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount determined by the coordination of benefits calculation.']
  coverage_dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispute.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary fields.']
  dispute_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the issue being disputed.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the dispute for tracking and communication.']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Category of the coverage dispute indicating the underlying issue.']
  disputing_party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute was raised by a member, provider, employer group, or other entity.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the dispute became effective (typically the date of filing).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the dispute was closed or otherwise terminated; null if still open.']
  formulary_exception_flag boolean [note: 'True if the dispute relates to a formulary exception request.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute is flagged as high priority due to potential impact.']
  network_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the network classification of the service at issue.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined priority for handling the dispute.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the dispute handling.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the dispute was resolved.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the dispute after final adjudication.']
  subrogation_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount involved in a subrogation claim.']
  subrogation_flag boolean [note: 'True if the dispute involves a subrogation scenario.']
  supporting_document_count varchar [note: 'Count of documents attached to the dispute for evidence.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dispute record.']

  Note: 'Master record for coverage disputes — formal disagreements between members, providers, or employer groups and the health plan regarding benefit interpretation, plan exclusions, coordination of benefits (COB) determinations, or subrogation decisions. Captures dispute type (benefit interpretation, COB, subrogation, network status, formulary exception), disputing party, disputed benefit or service, plan position, and resolution outcome. For COB disputes, additionally captures other carrier information, COB rule applied (birthday rule, NAIC COB model), disputed payer order, coordination amount, and inter-plan communication records. Supports ERISA plan document interpretation, NAIC COB model law compliance, and state DOI market conduct requirements.'
}

Table appeal.iro_organization {
  iro_organization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the Independent Review Organization.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Accrediting organization for the IRO.']
  accreditation_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the IRO\'s accreditation expires.']
  accreditation_review_notes varchar [note: 'Notes from the latest accreditation review.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the IRO.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the IRO\'s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the IRO\'s mailing address, if applicable.']
  approved_states varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of state codes where the IRO is approved.']
  assignment_rotation_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to rotate case assignments among IROs (e.g., round-robin, random).']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the IRO\'s mailing address.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance requirements applicable to the IRO (e.g., ACA §2719).']
  conflict_of_interest_attestation_date date [note: 'Date when the IRO provided a conflict-of-interest attestation.']
  contract_effective_end_date date [note: 'End date of the contract with the IRO.']
  contract_effective_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the contract with the IRO.']
  country varchar [note: 'Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3) of the IRO\'s mailing address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the IRO record was first created in the system.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contacting the IRO.']
  external_review_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of reviews performed by the IRO (e.g., medical, pharmacy, dental).']
  iro_organization_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the Independent Review Organization.']
  iro_organization_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the IRO.']
  is_conflict_of_interest boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a conflict of interest was identified (True/False).']
  is_state_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates if the IRO is currently approved in all required states (True/False).']
  last_accreditation_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent accreditation review.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the IRO record.']
  organization_type varchar [note: 'Category of the IRO based on its role and accreditation.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the IRO.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the IRO\'s mailing address.']
  rotation_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months in each rotation cycle.']
  specialty_capabilities varchar [note: 'List of medical specialties the IRO is qualified to review.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the IRO\'s mailing address.']
  state_approval_status varchar [note: 'Overall state-level approval status for the IRO.']

  Note: 'Master reference for Independent Review Organizations (IROs) and external review entities contracted or approved to conduct external independent reviews. Captures IRO name, URAC/NCQA accreditation status, accreditation expiration date, state approval status (by state), specialty capabilities, conflict-of-interest attestation date, contract effective dates, and assignment rotation methodology. Required for ACA §2719 external review compliance and state external review law adherence. Distinct from the provider and contract domains — IROs are specialized regulatory entities, not clinical providers.'
}

Table appeal.expedited_review {
  expedited_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each expedited review request.']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: Expedited reviews are evaluated against the benefit package\'s coverage terms to determine clinical urgency and coverage applicability. The benefit package determines authorization requirements and cov']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: Expedited reviews are a specialized review type for an appeal case; the existing appeal_reference_number duplicates the case identifier and can be removed.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health claim associated with the expedited review request.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.facility. Business justification: Expedited reviews are most commonly triggered by urgent inpatient or facility-based care denials (ICU, ER, surgical). Clinical urgency classification and SLA compliance depend on facility type. expedi']
  member_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.member_eligibility_span. Business justification: Expedited clinical reviews require real-time eligibility verification to confirm active coverage before urgent treatment decisions. Linking to the eligibility span enables reviewers to confirm coverag']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Expedited review clinical_urgency_classification is directly informed by the member\'s HCC risk profile — high-risk members with complex chronic conditions receive priority triage. Linking expedited re']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member requesting the expedited review.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.pa_request. Business justification: Expedited reviews are triggered when a PA denial creates urgent clinical risk. The expedited review must reference the original PA request to assess clinical urgency basis and service type. URAC and N']
  parent_expedited_review_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on expedited_review (parent_expedited_review_id)']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.policy. Business justification: Expedited reviews for urgent clinical situations require immediate access to policy benefit limits and coverage terms. IRO and internal reviewers must reference the specific policy to make binding dec']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Expedited Review tracking requires linking the reviewed provider to the review record for SLA monitoring.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Expedited reviews are governed by specific regulatory obligations (e.g., ERISA 72-hour rule, state urgent care mandates). Compliance tracking of expedited review SLA adherence and regulatory reporting']
  actual_decision_date date [note: 'Date the final decision for the expedited review was entered into the system.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustments (e.g., discounts, penalties) applied as a result of the expedited review.']
  claim_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount billed on the original claim.']
  clinical_urgency_classification varchar [note: 'Categorization of the clinical urgency that drives the expedited decision deadline.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the review triggered any compliance or regulatory follow‑up.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the expedited review record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency identifier (e.g., USD).']
  decision_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory or contractual deadline for completing the expedited review.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the expedited review (e.g., claim upheld, denied, partially approved).']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Clinical and policy rationale supporting the final decision.']
  expedited_notification_required boolean [note: 'True if the member must receive a notification within the expedited timeline.']
  expedited_reason varchar [note: 'Business‑defined reason that qualifies the request for an expedited timeline.']
  expedited_review_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the processing stage of the expedited review request.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'True if the expedited review record includes protected health information.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final payable amount after applying adjustments from the expedited review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations related to the expedited review.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the member was notified of the decision.']
  notified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time the notification was sent to the member.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing agency (e.g., CMS or state authority) that mandates the expedited review.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time the member or provider submitted the expedited review request.']
  review_request_number varchar [note: 'External reference number used in communications and reporting for the expedited review.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system (e.g., Utilization Management System) that created the record.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'US state code relevant to the expedited review jurisdiction.']
  supporting_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents (e.g., medical records) submitted with the expedited request.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the expedited review record was most recently modified.']

  Note: 'Tracks expedited appeal review requests where standard timeframes could jeopardize the members life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. Captures expedited request reason, clinical urgency classification, shortened decision timeline, and expedited notification requirements per CMS and state regulations.'
}

Table appeal.outcome {
  outcome_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the appeal outcome record.']
  adverse_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.adverse_determination. Business justification: An outcome records the final disposition of an appeal — specifically whether the adverse determination was upheld or overturned. Linking outcome directly to adverse_determination enables critical regu']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: Appeal outcomes (overturn, uphold, partial) are reported at the benefit-package level for CMS and state DOI regulatory filings. Linking outcome to benefit_package enables package-level outcome analyti']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original appeal case to which this outcome belongs.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim that was the subject of the appeal.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Outcome reporting per employer is required for financial impact analysis and employer‑specific compliance dashboards.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member associated with the appeal.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Overturned appeal outcomes confirming previously denied diagnoses trigger risk score recalculation under CMS risk adjustment rules. The outcome product\'s downstream_action field tracks these risk adju']
  pa_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.pa_decision. Business justification: When an appeal outcome overturns or upholds a PA denial, the outcome record must reference the original PA decision to trigger downstream actions such as authorization reinstatement or claim reprocess']
  parent_outcome_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on outcome (parent_outcome_id)']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.policy. Business justification: Appeal outcomes trigger downstream policy actions — benefit reinstatement, premium adjustments, coverage modifications. Linking outcome directly to the affected policy enables automated downstream pro']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider associated with the appeal.']
  raps_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.raps_submission. Business justification: When an appeal outcome overturns a denial and confirms a diagnosis, the plan must submit corrected RAPS data to CMS for risk adjustment. This operational link between appeal outcomes and RAPS submissi']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Appeal outcomes must be reported under specific regulatory obligations (CMS reporting, state mandates). The outcome product has regulatory_body and jurisdiction_state as text but no FK to the governin']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the appeal outcome complies with applicable regulations and internal policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the appeal outcome record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|CAD|GBP|JPY|AUD — promote to reference product]']
  downstream_action varchar [note: 'Action triggered downstream as a result of the appeal outcome.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount restored to the member as a result of the appeal outcome.']
  jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state code where the appeal was adjudicated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CA|NY|TX|FL|IL|PA — promote to reference product]']
  member_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the member was notified of the appeal outcome.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the reviewer or system regarding the outcome.']
  outcome_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the appeal outcome for tracking and reporting.']
  outcome_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the appeal outcome record.']
  outcome_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the appeal outcome was rendered.']
  outcome_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the final disposition of the appeal.']
  provider_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the provider was notified of the appeal outcome.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the reason for the appeal outcome.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason behind the appeal outcome.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority overseeing the appeal outcome (e.g., CMS, state insurance department).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the appeal outcome record.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the record in the source system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the appeal outcome record.']

  Note: 'Records the final disposition and outcome of an appeal case after all levels of review are exhausted — including overturn, uphold, partial overturn, and withdrawn outcomes. Captures outcome date, outcome type, financial impact (claim amount restored), member notification date, and downstream action triggers (claim reprocessing, authorization issuance).'
}

Table risk.member_risk_score {
  member_risk_score_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the member risk score record.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: CMS Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) audits directly examine member risk scores. Linking member_risk_score to the audit_engagement driving the RADV review is essential for tracking which scores ']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: CMS risk adjustment operations require member risk scores to be attributed to a specific health plan for plan-level RAF calculation, CMS payment reconciliation, and HCC-based risk adjustment reporting']
  member_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.member_eligibility_span. Business justification: CMS risk adjustment (RAPS/EDPS) requires anchoring each risk score to the specific eligibility span it covers for payment year reconciliation. Domain experts in risk adjustment operations consider thi']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member to whom the risk score applies.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.policy. Business justification: Risk scores are calculated per policy for CMS risk adjustment submissions and premium rating. Domain experts expect member_risk_score to reference the specific policy it covers, enabling policy-level ']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: Member risk scores are calculated per plan year for CMS payment year submissions. Linking to plan.year enables year-based risk score aggregation, open enrollment period alignment, and annual CMS risk ']
  audit_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who performed the most recent audit action.']
  cms_published_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'RAF score value as published by CMS after reconciliation.']
  cms_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the score\'s submission to CMS.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of actions taken to resolve score variance (e.g., code addendum, data correction).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk score record was first created in the data lake.']
  demographic_factor_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Score component derived from member demographics (age, gender, Medicaid status).']
  diagnosis_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct diagnoses captured for the member in the scoring period.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the risk score became effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the risk score is no longer valid for payment.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the score was manually overridden.']
  manual_override_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for a manual override of the risk score.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Name of the risk adjustment model (e.g., "CMS‑HCC", "RxHCC", "HHS‑HCC").']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the actuarial model used to generate the score (e.g., "CMS‑HCC v2023").']
  payment_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the risk score is used in CMS payment calculations.']
  plan_calculated_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'RAF score calculated internally by the health plan prior to CMS submission.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the risk score record.']
  resubmission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for any resubmitted score file to CMS.']
  risk_adjustment_factor_category varchar [note: 'Category of the risk adjustment factor (e.g., "RAF", "HCC", "RxHCC").']
  risk_score_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the risk score record, often composed of member ID, year, and model version.']
  risk_score_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence level (0‑100) assigned by the model to the score.']
  risk_score_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label describing the risk score record (e.g., "2023 Medicare Advantage RAF").']
  risk_score_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for any additional commentary or observations.']
  risk_score_source varchar [note: 'Data source used to generate the score.']
  risk_score_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the risk score.']
  risk_score_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the score is projected (prospective), derived from current data (concurrent), or the final CMS‑published value.']
  risk_score_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric RAF score calculated for the member (e.g., 1.2345).']
  score_components varchar [note: 'Delimited list or JSON string of component scores (e.g., HCC contributions, demographic adjustments).']
  score_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk score becomes effective for payment calculations.']
  score_variance decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric difference between the plan‑calculated score and the CMS‑published score.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system for the risk score (e.g., "Milliman MG‑ALFA").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the risk score record.']
  variance_category varchar [note: 'Root‑cause classification for any variance between plan and CMS scores.']

  Note: 'Authoritative member-level risk score record and reconciliation tracker. Captures RAF (Risk Adjustment Factor) scores, HCC (Hierarchical Condition Category) mappings, CMS reconciliation outcomes, and plan-vs-CMS variance analysis. Stores prospective and concurrent RAF scores derived from actuarial models and encounter data, including score effective dates, payment year, model version (CMS-HCC, RxHCC, HHS-HCC, PACE), score components, CMS submission status, plan-calculated vs. CMS-published RAF variance, root cause category (missing diagnoses, HCC hierarchy differences, demographic factors), corrective actions, and resubmission references. Core SSOT for member-level risk in Medicare Advantage, ACA, and Medicaid risk adjustment programs. Subsumes RAF reconciliation tracking.'
}

Table risk.hcc_mapping {
  hcc_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for hcc_mapping']
  age_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplier adjusting the HCC coefficient based on member age.']
  coefficient decimal(10,6) [note: 'Weighting coefficient used in RAF score calculations for the HCC.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the mapping record was first loaded into the lakehouse.']
  demographic_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Factor adjusting the HCC weight for demographic characteristics (age, gender, etc.).']
  disease_interaction_group varchar [note: 'Identifier for a group of diagnoses that have interaction effects.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this mapping becomes effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this mapping expires or is superseded; null if still active.']
  gender_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplier adjusting the HCC coefficient based on member gender.']
  hcc_code varchar [note: 'CMS-assigned HCC identifier linked to the diagnosis.']
  hcc_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the HCC.']
  hcc_mapping_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the mapping record.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the HCC within the hierarchical structure (0 = top level).']
  icd10_code varchar [note: 'Standard ICD-10 diagnosis code associated with the mapping.']
  icd10_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the ICD-10 diagnosis.']
  interaction_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the diagnosis interacts with other HCCs (yes) or not (no).']
  is_excluded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the diagnosis is excluded from the current model year.']
  is_mapped boolean [note: 'True if the ICD‑10 code has a valid HCC mapping in this version.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the mapping was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that performed the last update.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the mapping record.']
  mapping_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the mapping data.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the CMS HCC model version to which the mapping applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or audit notes regarding the mapping.']
  parent_hcc_code varchar [note: 'Code of the immediate parent HCC in the hierarchy, if applicable.']
  plan_type_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Adjustment factor for the HCC based on the member\'s health plan type (e.g., HMO, PPO).']
  region_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Adjustment factor reflecting geographic cost variations.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent review process.']
  risk_score_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'Weight applied to the HCC when aggregating RAF scores.']
  source_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the source CMS model (e.g., V24, V28).']

  Note: 'Maps ICD-10 diagnosis codes to Hierarchical Condition Categories (HCCs) per CMS model version. Stores the ICD-to-HCC crosswalk, HCC hierarchy relationships (parent/child HCC hierarchies), coefficient values by model year, disease interaction flags, and demographic adjustment factors. Supports RAF score calculation, RAPS/EDPS submission validation, and RADV audit defense. Reference entity managed per CMS annual model updates (V24, V28 transition). Source: CMS annual HCC model release files.'
}

  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: RAPS submissions are filed per plan year for CMS risk adjustment. Linking to plan.year enables proper plan-year-based RAPS reconciliation, submission deadline tracking against cms_plan_submission_dead']

Each record represents a RAPS batch transaction submitted via the CMS RAPS gateway. Source: Milliman MG-ALFA and EDI gateway.'
}

Note: This product should be renamed to cms_submission after the RAPS+EDPS merge.'

  Note: 'Represents an underwriting decision record for group or individual market applicants, including the group-level risk profile used in underwriting evaluation and renewal rating. Captures underwriting case number, applicant type (group/individual/small group/large group), LOB, underwriting tier, risk classification, medical underwriting status, final decision (approved/declined/rated), effective date, underwriter ID, decision rationale, and applicable underwriting rules/criteria. For group cases, includes aggregate risk profile: rating period, total member months, age/gender distribution index, morbidity factor, industry risk factor, prior year claims PMPM, expected claims PMPM, large claimant count, chronic condition prevalence rates, and overall group risk score. Supports fully-insured and self-insured (ASO) underwriting workflows, group renewal underwriting, and stop-loss pricing.'
}

Business justification: Reinsurance arrangements are negotiated at the health plan level — a plan cedes premium to a reinsurer for stop-loss protection. Plan-level reinsurance reporting, premium_ceded allocation, and stop-lo']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Reinsurance treaties require state regulatory filing and approval under insurance codes. Linking each arrangement to its governing regulatory obligation enables compliance tracking of treaty filings, ']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: Reinsurance arrangements are structured per plan year for annual treaty renewal, premium cession calculations, and stop-loss reporting. Linking to plan.year enables year-based reinsurance cost trackin']
  aggregate_attachment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary threshold for aggregate stop‑loss coverage.']
  aggregate_attachment_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of aggregate losses that must be reached before the reinsurer pays.']
  arrangement_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned by the insurer or reinsurer to identify the treaty.']

  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the reinsurance coverage terminates; null for open‑ended treaties.']

  premium_ceded decimal(18,2) [note: 'Premium amount transferred to the reinsurer for the treaty.']
  reinsurance_arrangement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reinsurance treaty.']

  stop_loss_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Loss amount that triggers stop‑loss coverage.']
  stop_loss_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the stop‑loss is specific per‑member or aggregate across a group.']
  treaty_terms varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the treaty\'s contractual terms and conditions.']
  treaty_type varchar [note: 'Type of reinsurance treaty governing the risk transfer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reinsurance arrangement record.']

  Note: 'Master record for reinsurance and stop-loss arrangements protecting the health plan against catastrophic or excess losses. Captures treaty type (quota share, excess of loss, aggregate stop-loss, specific stop-loss), reinsurer name, attachment point, specific deductible amount, aggregate attachment factor and amount, corridor percentage, maximum liability, coinsurance percentage, maximum recovery limit, premium ceded, effective and expiration dates, LOB coverage, and treaty terms. Includes stop-loss threshold parameters for self-insured employer groups. Supports both individual specific stop-loss and aggregate stop-loss for self-insured groups.'
}
