Table risk.score_hcc_contribution {
  score_hcc_contribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the risk_score_hcc_contribution association']
  hcc_mapping_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific HCC mapping record (ICD-10 to HCC crosswalk entry) that drives this contribution.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the member risk score record to which this HCC contributes.']
  contribution_weight decimal [note: 'The weighted coefficient applied to this specific HCC for this member-score combination, reflecting any member-level demographic or plan-type adjustments applied on top of the base HCC coefficient.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this HCC contribution became effective for the member\'s risk score, typically aligned to the diagnosis encounter date or CMS model effective date.']
  hcc_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of HCC codes contributing to the score. [Moved from member_risk_score: This pipe-separated multi-value field encodes the M:N relationship between a risk score and its contributing HCC codes in a non-relational format. With the risk_score_hcc_contribution association table in place, each HCC contribution is represented as a discrete row with proper FK linkage, making this denormalized field redundant and structurally incorrect.]']
  is_primary_hcc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this HCC is the dominant driver of the member\'s RAF score for this scoring period. Used in RADV audit defense to identify the highest-impact diagnosis.']
  score_contribution_amount decimal [note: 'The RAF unit amount this HCC contributes to the member\'s total risk score for the payment year. Equals contribution_weight multiplied by applicable adjustment factors.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the actuarial contribution event between a member_risk_score and an hcc_mapping. It captures the per-HCC contribution to a member\'s RAF score for a given payment year and model version, including the weighted coefficient amount, primary HCC designation, and effective period. Each record links one member_risk_score to one hcc_mapping and carries attributes that exist only in the context of this specific contribution — essential for RAPS/EDPS submission accuracy, CMS RADV audit defense, and RAF variance root-cause analysis.. Existence Justification: In Medicare Advantage risk adjustment, a member\'s RAF score is mathematically computed as the sum of coefficients from ALL applicable HCC codes that map to the member\'s diagnoses. A single member_risk_score record aggregates contributions from multiple HCC mappings (e.g., HCC 85 + HCC 96 + HCC 108), and a single HCC mapping (e.g., HCC 85 — Congestive Heart Failure) contributes to the risk scores of every member diagnosed with that condition. This is not an analytical correlation — it is an operationally managed actuarial construct that health plans actively track for RAPS/EDPS submission accuracy, CMS audit defense (RADV), and variance reconciliation. The business explicitly names this concept \'risk score HCC contribution\' and tracks per-contribution data (contribution weight, score contribution amount, effective date, primary HCC flag) that belongs to neither the score record nor the HCC mapping alone.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_obligation {
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the regulatory obligation record.']
  audit_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of audit observations related to the obligation.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the regulatory obligation.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the regulatory obligation record.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the obligation.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to remediate any compliance gaps.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the obligation becomes effective.']
  exemption_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an exemption from the obligation is possible.']
  exemption_criteria varchar [note: 'Conditions under which an exemption may be granted.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the obligation expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required filings.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the obligation must be satisfied.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority that issues the obligation (e.g., CMS, OCR, NAIC, State DOI).']
  is_federal boolean [note: 'True if the obligation applies at the federal level.']
  is_state_specific boolean [note: 'True if the obligation is limited to a particular state.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the obligation (state, province, or federal).']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance assessment.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User or process that performed the most recent update.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Date the next compliance action is required.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the regulatory obligation.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the regulatory requirement.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty for non‑compliance.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the penalty amount.']
  reference_url varchar [note: 'Link to the official regulation or guidance document.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Framework or statute under which the obligation falls (e.g., HIPAA, ACA, Medicare, State Law).']
  reporting_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required reports.']
  risk_impact varchar [note: 'Potential business impact if the obligation is not met.']
  risk_likelihood varchar [note: 'Likelihood of non‑compliance occurring.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score (likelihood × impact).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the obligation data.']
  submission_deadline date [note: 'Final date for filing required reports or documents.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the obligation definition for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record for each regulatory obligation the health plan must fulfill — HIPAA Privacy/Security Rules, ACA market conduct requirements, CMS Medicare/Medicaid mandates, state DOI filings, NCQA/URAC accreditation standards, ERISA requirements, SOC reporting obligations, and state Medicaid fair hearing compliance. Captures the governing body, regulatory framework, obligation type, effective date, jurisdiction, frequency, accountable business owner, compliance status, and obligation risk score (likelihood × impact of non-compliance). Includes tracking of state fair hearing obligations for Medicaid adverse benefit determinations under 42 CFR §438.400. Serves as the authoritative registry of all compliance requirements and the anchor entity linking to submissions, attestations, policies, and corrective actions that demonstrate adherence.'
}

  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why a filing was rejected by the regulator.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the submission record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each regulatory filing or submission — from scheduled deadline through actual filing. Captures submission type, regulatory body, due date, lead time days, reminder schedule, submission date, filing period, submission method (EDI, portal, paper), status (scheduled, draft, submitted, accepted, rejected), and confirmation identifiers. Covers CMS annual notices, state DOI rate filings, NAIC financial statements, NCQA accreditation submissions, OCR breach notifications, MLR rebate filings, ACA marketplace submissions, and ERISA filings (Form 5500, Summary Annual Report, Summary Plan Description, COBRA notices, QMCSO responses). Supports the annual regulatory calendar view used by compliance officers to manage deadlines across CMS, state DOIs, NCQA, OCR, ERISA, and DOL obligations. Links to the regulatory obligation it satisfies.'
}

  Note: 'Master record for each Corrective Action Plan (CAP) developed in response to audit findings, regulatory violations, or accreditation deficiencies. Captures the triggering finding or violation, CAP type, remediation strategy, responsible owner, target completion date, and implementation status. Includes granular milestone tracking with individual action items, assigned owners, planned/actual completion dates, milestone status, evidence artifacts, and reviewer sign-off for each milestone. Tracks the full CAP lifecycle from submission to regulatory body through milestone completion, validation, and closure. Critical for CMS audit readiness and NCQA accreditation maintenance.'
}

  cap_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the corrective action plan milestone record.']

  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance domain that the milestone addresses. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hipaa|aca|mlr|ncqa|urac|state_doi|fwa — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone record was first created in the system.']
  escalated_flag boolean [note: 'True if the milestone has been escalated due to delays or issues.']
  evidence_documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the artifact or document that provides evidence of milestone completion.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone is deemed critical for compliance or business impact.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the milestone for external reference.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work or outcome required for the milestone.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the milestone within the corrective action plan.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Category that classifies the nature of the milestone activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the milestone.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date by which the milestone should be completed.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the milestone to begin.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level indicating the importance or urgency of the milestone.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the specific regulatory requirement, audit finding, or standard tied to the milestone.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the milestone\'s impact on compliance or operations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the milestone record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking individual milestones and action items within a Corrective Action Plan (CAP). Captures milestone description, assigned owner, planned completion date, actual completion date, milestone status, evidence artifacts, and reviewer sign-off. Enables granular tracking of CAP progress and supports regulatory reporting on remediation timelines. Links to the parent CAP and provides the audit trail required by CMS, state DOIs, and NCQA for CAP validation.'
}

  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group. Business justification: HIPAA requires health insurers to notify employer group sponsors when a breach affects their covered members. Linking breach_incident to employer.group enables group-level breach notification tracking']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: HIPAA breach notifications to HHS and state regulators are plan-specific. CMS requires plan-level breach tracking for Medicare Advantage. Breach incidents affect members enrolled in specific health pl']
  provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Required: PHI breach incidents are reported against the specific provider contract responsible for the data.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: PHI breach incidents are governed by specific regulatory obligations — primarily the HIPAA Breach Notification Rule (45 CFR §164.400-414) and state breach notification laws. Linking breach_incident di']
  affected_phi_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of PHI categories impacted (e.g., demographic, clinical, claims).']
  audit_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of audit findings related to the breach.']
  breach_cause_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the cause of the breach.']
  breach_discovery_date date [note: 'Date the breach was discovered by the organization.']
  breach_incident_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the breach incident.']
  breach_occurrence_date date [note: 'Date the breach actually occurred (if known).']
  breach_report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the external breach report document.']
  breach_resolution_date date [note: 'Date the breach was fully resolved and closed.']
  breach_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the breach (internal staff, external actor, partner, unknown).']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the breach investigation.']
  breach_type varchar [note: 'Category of the breach event.']
  business_associate_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Business Associate was involved in the breach.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach incident record was created in the system.']
  hhs_notification_date date [note: 'Date HHS was notified of the breach.']
  hhs_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the U.S. Department of Health & Human Services was notified.']
  notification_content_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the notification content used.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date the notification was sent.']
  notification_delivery_confirmation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether delivery confirmation was received.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Delivery channel used for the notification.']
  notification_obligation varchar [note: 'Regulatory notification obligations triggered by the breach.']
  notification_recipient_count varchar [note: 'Number of recipients who received the notification.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Type of notification sent.']
  number_of_individuals_affected varchar [note: 'Count of distinct individuals whose PHI was compromised.']
  number_of_records_affected varchar [note: 'Total number of PHI records impacted.']
  regulatory_filing_date date [note: 'Date the breach was filed with the regulator.']
  regulatory_filing_status varchar [note: 'Status of required regulatory filings related to the breach.']
  risk_assessment_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used for risk assessment.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score resulting from breach risk assessment.']
  state_notification_date date [note: 'Date the state authority was notified.']
  state_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the relevant state authority was notified.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the breach incident record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each PHI (Protected Health Information) breach incident identified under HIPAA Breach Notification Rule (45 CFR §164.400–414). Captures breach discovery date, breach type (unauthorized access, theft, loss, improper disposal), affected PHI categories, number of individuals affected, breach cause, business associate involvement, risk assessment outcome, and notification obligations triggered. Includes full notification tracking: notification type (individual, HHS/OCR, media, state AG), notification method (mail, email, substitute notice), notification date, recipient count, content version, and delivery confirmation. Drives the 60-day OCR notification workflow and annual breach report to HHS. Critical for HIPAA compliance and OCR audit readiness.'
}

  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: HIPAA privacy requests are governed by specific regulatory obligations under the HIPAA Privacy Rule (45 CFR §164.524 for access, §164.526 for amendment, §164.528 for accounting of disclosures). Linkin']
  appeal_deadline date [note: 'Final date by which an appeal must be filed.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the appeal process, if applicable.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the privacy request record was first created in the system.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the privacy request record.']
  denial_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when a request is denied or partially granted.']
  disclosure_authorization_basis varchar [note: 'Legal basis authorizing the PHI disclosure.']
  disclosure_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the PHI disclosure, if one occurred.']
  disclosure_logged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a PHI disclosure related to this request has been logged.']
  disclosure_phicategories varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of PHI categories disclosed (e.g., diagnosis, treatment, payment).']
  disclosure_purpose varchar [note: 'Business or legal purpose for which the PHI was disclosed.']
  disclosure_recipient_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity receiving the disclosed PHI.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Outcome of the request (e.g., granted, denied, partially granted).']
  is_appealed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has appealed the request decision.']
  is_confidential_communication boolean [note: 'True when the member requests that communications be handled confidentially.']
  request_channel varchar [note: 'Medium of the request (digital, paper, or phone).']
  request_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description provided by the member detailing the request.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the privacy request.']
  request_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the privacy request was initially received.']
  request_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the request was received (e.g., member portal, email, phone).']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the privacy request.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Category of the privacy request as defined by HIPAA (e.g., access, amendment, accounting of disclosures, restriction, confidential communication).']
  response_date date [note: 'Date on which the organization completed its response to the request.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which a response must be provided (typically 30 days from receipt).']

  Note: 'Transactional record for HIPAA privacy operations — member privacy rights requests and PHI disclosure tracking. Covers access requests (right to access PHI), amendment requests, accounting of disclosures, restriction requests, and confidential communication requests under 45 CFR §164.500. Captures request type, member identifier, request receipt date, response due date, response date, request disposition (granted, denied, partially granted), denial reason, and appeal status. Includes PHI disclosure logging: disclosure date, recipient organization, recipient type (public health authority, law enforcement, research, legal), purpose of disclosure, PHI categories disclosed, and disclosure authorization basis. Excludes treatment, payment, and healthcare operations disclosures per HIPAA exemptions. Tracks compliance with HIPAA response timelines and supports OCR audit documentation.'
}

  baa_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each Business Associate Agreement record.']
  delegation_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.delegation_agreement. Health plan compliance officers must link each BAA to its governing delegation agreement for NCQA delegat']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.facility. Business justification: HIPAA Business Associate Agreements are executed with facilities (hospitals, labs, imaging centers) that handle PHI on behalf of the health plan. Compliance teams must link BAAs to the specific facili']
  group_practice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.group_practice. Business justification: BAAs are executed with group practices that access or process member PHI (e.g., multi-specialty groups, IPAs). Compliance teams need to link BAAs to group practice entities for HIPAA compliance tracki']
  provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_provider_contract. Business justification: HIPAA requires a BAA whenever a provider contract involves PHI sharing. Health insurance compliance teams must trace which provider contract necessitated each BAA for HIPAA audit trails, contract term']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each BAA supports compliance with a regulatory obligation; linking enables obligation‑centric reporting.']
  tpa_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.tpa_arrangement. Business justification: HIPAA requires a BAA with every TPA that handles PHI on behalf of a covered entity. Linking baa to tpa_arrangement enables tracking which BAA governs each TPA relationship — a direct HIPAA §164.308(b)']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the BAA by the health plan.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the BAA.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of amendments executed against the original BAA.']
  audit_rights varchar [note: 'Rights granted to the health plan to audit the associate\'s compliance with the BAA.']
  breach_notification_requirements varchar [note: 'Procedures and timelines the associate must follow to notify the health plan of a PHI breach.']
  business_associate_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the vendor, TPA, PBM, clearinghouse, or other entity that receives PHI under the agreement.']
  business_associate_type varchar [note: 'Category of the business associate indicating the nature of the relationship.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BAA record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  data_disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method the associate must use to dispose of retained PHI after the retention period.']
  data_retention_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months the associate must retain PHI before disposal.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the BAA expires or terminates; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the BAA becomes legally binding.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction governing the agreement (e.g., state law, federal HIPAA).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic jurisdiction (state or territory) applicable to the BAA.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment to the BAA.']
  permitted_phis varchar [note: 'List or description of specific PHI categories the associate may handle (e.g., claims, enrollment, clinical data).']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation(s) that the BAA satisfies (e.g., HIPAA, HITECH).']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiration that the health plan must provide notice to renew.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the BAA renews automatically, requires manual action, or does not renew.']
  scope_of_phi_use varchar [note: 'Narrative description of permitted uses and disclosures of Protected Health Information under the agreement.']
  security_obligations varchar [note: 'Detailed security controls and safeguards the associate must implement to protect PHI.']
  subcontractor_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the business associate may engage subcontractors to perform PHI‑related services.']
  subcontractor_details varchar [note: 'Description of any approved subcontractors, their roles, and required safeguards.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing conditions under which the BAA may be terminated by either party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the BAA record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Business Associate Agreement (BAA) executed with vendors, TPAs, PBMs, clearinghouses, and other entities that handle PHI on behalf of the health plan under HIPAA (45 CFR §164.308). Captures business associate name, agreement effective date, expiration date, PHI use permissions, security obligations, breach notification requirements, subcontractor provisions, termination clauses, and agreement status. Serves as the authoritative registry of all BAAs and supports HIPAA compliance audits and vendor risk management.'
}

Table compliance.fwa_case {
  fwa_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the FWA case.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.broker. Business justification: Broker fraud (false enrollment submissions, commission manipulation) is a recognized FWA category in health insurance. Linking fwa_case to employer.broker enables structured broker FWA investigation t']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group. Business justification: FWA investigations in health insurance frequently target employer groups submitting fraudulent enrollment data or inflated headcounts. Linking fwa_case to employer.group enables structured group-level']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: CMS requires plan-level FWA program reporting and case tracking for Medicare Advantage and Medicaid managed care plans. FWA cases are investigated within a specific health plan\'s claims context. A com']
  premium_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_payment. Business justification: FWA cases involving premium fraud schemes (ghost enrollment, fictitious payments) reference specific premium payment records as evidence. This FK enables SIU investigators to directly link FWA cases t']
  provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_provider_contract. Business justification: FWA cases are opened against providers operating under specific contracts. Linking enables contract-level FWA reporting, supports contract suspension/termination decisions, and satisfies CMS and OIG r']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: FWA cases with subject_type=\'PROVIDER\' reference a specific provider. subject_reference is a generic text field serving multiple subject types. A proper FK enables fraud investigators to retrieve all ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: FWA cases are governed by specific regulatory obligations — CMS FWA program requirements, ACA Section 1128J, and state insurance fraud statutes. The fwa_case table currently has a compliance_reference']
  vbc_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vbc_contract. Business justification: VBC/ACO arrangements are subject to FWA investigations including manipulation of attributed member counts, upcoding to inflate quality scores, and fraudulent shared savings claims. CMS requires health']
  allegation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the alleged fraud, waste, or abuse.']
  audit_log_url varchar [note: 'Link to the immutable audit log for this case.']
  case_disposition varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the case after investigation.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the case for external reference and reporting.']
  case_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case was officially opened.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the case.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the case as fraud, waste, or abuse.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date the case disposition was recorded.']
  estimated_exposure_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected monetary loss associated with the alleged activity.']
  evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Link or identifier to supporting evidence stored in the evidence repository.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the case is considered high risk based on internal scoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by investigators or reviewers.']
  recovery_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount recovered or expected to be recovered from the subject.']
  referral_date date [note: 'Date the referral was received and the case was created.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the case referral, such as claim edit, data mining, tip line, law enforcement, or employee report.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case has been reported to a regulatory body (e.g., CMS, state fraud bureau).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned during triage (0‑100 scale).']
  subject_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subject (member, provider, or employer group) under investigation.']
  subject_type varchar [note: 'Entity type that is the subject of the investigation.']
  triage_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the initial triage review, indicating next steps.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the case record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Fraud, Waste, and Abuse (FWA) investigation — from initial referral intake through case disposition. Captures referral source (claim edits, data mining, tip line, law enforcement, employee reports), referral date, allegation description, supporting evidence references, triage outcome, case type (fraud, waste, abuse), subject type (member, provider, employer group), case open date, investigation status, assigned investigator, estimated financial exposure, recovery amount, and case disposition (substantiated, unsubstantiated, referred to law enforcement, civil monetary penalty). Supports CMS FWA program requirements, state insurance fraud bureau reporting, and annual FWA reporting to CMS.'
}

The mlr_calculation table alr']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.fee_schedule. Business justification: MLR calculations require the fee schedule used to determine allowed amounts in the incurred claims numerator.   group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group. Business justification: ACA §2718 requires MLR rebates for employer-sponsored plans to be distributed to the group sponsor. Linking mlr_calculation to employer.group enables group-level rebate disbursement tracking and ACA M']
  primary_mlr_health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the health plan for which the MLR is being calculated.']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.rate_schedule. Business justification: MLR calculations use earned premium data derived from specific rate schedules. This FK links each MLR calculation to the applicable rate schedule, enabling premium earned-amount reconciliation and sup']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: MLR calculations are performed to demonstrate compliance with regulatory obligations; linking ties calculations to obligations.']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: ACA 45 CFR 158 requires annual MLR reporting tied to specific plan years. Linking mlr_calculation to the plan year entity enables MLR reporting workflows, open enrollment deadline tracking, and plan y']
  calculation_date date [note: 'Date on which the MLR calculation was performed.']
  calculation_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the MLR calculation for reporting and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MLR calculation record was first created in the system.']
  earned_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total earned premium dollars for the reporting period, forming the MLR denominator.']
  incurred_claims_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of claims incurred for the reporting period.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line (e.g., HMO, PPO, Medicaid) associated with the MLR calculation.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the market segment (e.g., individual, group, Medicare) to which the calculation applies.']
  mlr_calculation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the MLR calculation.']
  mlr_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated MLR ratio expressed as a percentage (incurred claims ÷ earned premium).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or explanations entered by analysts.']
  quality_improvement_expenses_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount spent on quality improvement activities that count toward the MLR denominator.']
  rebate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the rebate owed to members or the market segment.']
  rebate_disbursement_date date [note: 'Date on which the rebate payment was or will be disbursed.']
  rebate_disbursement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the rebate payment process.']
  rebate_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'True if the calculated MLR exceeds the regulatory threshold and a rebate is required.']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the MLR is being calculated.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that generated the source data for this calculation (e.g., HealthEdge, Milliman).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the MLR calculation record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the calculation record for audit trail purposes.']

  Note: 'Medical Loss Ratio (MLR) calculation and rebate processing record per ACA Section 2718 requirements. Captures reporting year, market segment, LOB, incurred claims, quality improvement expenses, earned premium, MLR percentage, rebate determination, rebate amount, and disbursement tracking.'
}

Table compliance.accreditation_program {
  accreditation_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each accreditation program record.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: NCQA Health Plan Accreditation and URAC accreditation programs are scoped to specific health plans. CMS requires accreditation status for Medicare Advantage plans. A health insurance compliance expert']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Accreditation programs are driven by specific regulatory obligations; linking clarifies compliance scope.']
  accountable_owner varchar [note: 'Full name of the person or team accountable for the accreditation.']
  accreditation_program_level varchar [note: 'Level or tier assigned by the accrediting body (e.g., Level 1, Level 2).']
  accreditation_program_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the accreditation (e.g., accredited, provisional, denied).']
  accreditation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the accreditation program (e.g., health plan, provider, pharmacy).']
  accrediting_body varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that issues the accreditation (e.g., NCQA, URAC, CMS).']
  applicable_standards varchar [note: 'List of standards, guidelines, or criteria the program must meet.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Chronological notes capturing audit actions and decisions.']
  benchmark_thresholds varchar [note: 'Target values for performance measures used in the accreditation.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Progress of the accreditation process expressed as a percent.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the accreditation\'s compliance focus.']
  conditions varchar [note: 'Any remedial actions, monitoring, or conditions required for continued accreditation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the accreditation record was first created.']
  decision varchar [note: 'Final decision made by the accrediting body.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the accreditation starts to be binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the accreditation ends; null if open‑ended.']
  escalated_flag boolean [note: 'True when issues have been escalated to senior management.']
  evidence_documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to uploaded evidence files used in the accreditation review.']
  final_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (e.g., 0‑100) resulting from the accreditation evaluation.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the accreditation is essential to business operations.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or system that performed the most recent update.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the accreditation was last reviewed for compliance.']
  measure_thresholds varchar [note: 'Target thresholds for each evaluated measure.']
  measures varchar [note: 'Key performance or quality measures assessed during the accreditation.']
  next_survey_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next accreditation survey cycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations about the accreditation.']
  preliminary_findings varchar [note: 'Initial observations and issues identified during the survey.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned by the accrediting organization.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the accreditation program (e.g., NCQA Health Plan Accreditation).']
  rating varchar [note: 'Star rating or equivalent tier based on the final score.']
  recommendations varchar [note: 'Suggested improvements or corrective actions from the accrediting body.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific regulation, statute, or standard governing the accreditation.']
  renewal_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required renewal surveys.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating reflecting potential impact of non‑compliance.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Description of the functional and geographic scope covered by the accreditation.']
  survey_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the accreditation survey window.']
  survey_start_date date [note: 'First day of the accreditation survey window.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Type of survey associated with the accreditation (initial, renewal, focused).']
  surveyor_contact varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary surveyor or accrediting liaison.']
  surveyor_team varchar [note: 'Name(s) of the surveyor(s) or consulting firm performing the review.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the accreditation record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each accreditation program the health plan participates in — NCQA Health Plan Accreditation, URAC Health Plan Accreditation, NCQA HEDIS, CAHPS survey programs, and CMS Star Ratings. Captures accrediting body, program name, accreditation type, current status (accredited, provisional, denied), accreditation level, effective/expiration dates, accountable business owner, and applicable standards/measures with benchmark thresholds. Includes full survey/review cycle tracking: survey type (initial, renewal, focused), survey date range, surveyor team, scope, preliminary findings, final score/rating, accreditation decision, and conditions or recommendations. Serves as the authoritative registry of all accreditation relationships and their evaluation history.'
}

Business justification: An accreditation survey conducted by NCQA, URAC, or CMS is a formal audit engagement — it is an examination conducted against the health plan with findings, scores, and outcomes. Linking accreditation']
  delegation_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.delegation_agreement. Business justification: NCQA delegation surveys evaluate specific delegation agreements to assess whether delegated functions meet accreditation standards. Health plan accreditation teams must link surveys to delegation agre']
  accreditation_decision varchar [note: 'Final determination of accreditation status.']
  accreditation_survey_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the survey.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the survey record was first created in the system.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the accreditation survey was concluded.']
  evidence_documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the repository containing supporting evidence and documents submitted for the survey.']
  final_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score assigned by the accrediting body after final evaluation (e.g., 0.00 – 100.00).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the surveyor team.']
  preliminary_findings varchar [note: 'Initial observations and findings recorded during the survey before final evaluation.']
  rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating tier derived from the final score (1‑5 stars).']
  recommendation varchar [note: 'Recommendations or conditions issued by the surveyor for future compliance.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with identified deficiencies.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the accreditation survey officially began.']
  survey_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the survey was performed.']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the survey (e.g., onsite visit, remote review).']
  survey_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the accreditation body for this survey cycle.']
  survey_region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the region of the survey.']
  survey_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the functional and geographic scope covered by the survey.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the survey purpose (e.g., initial accreditation, renewal, focused review).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the survey record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each accreditation survey or review cycle conducted by NCQA, URAC, or CMS. Captures survey type (initial, renewal, focused), survey date range, surveyor team, survey scope, preliminary findings, final score or rating, accreditation decision, and conditions or recommendations. Links to the parent accreditation program and drives corrective action planning for deficiencies identified during the survey. Tracks the full survey lifecycle from scheduling through final determination.'
}

  document_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored policy document.']
  document_size_bytes varchar [note: 'File size of the electronic document in bytes.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic version of the policy (e.g., S3, SharePoint).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the policy becomes operationally binding.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the policy ceases to be in effect; null for indefinite policies.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy is currently in effect.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'True if the policy has been moved to archival storage.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'True if the policy is marked confidential; aligns with confidentiality_level.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the subsequent review.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Business code or alphanumeric identifier used to reference the policy across systems.']
  policy_document_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping such as HIPAA Privacy, FWA, ACA Compliance, Credentialing, Claims Adjudication, etc.']
  policy_document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the policy.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the compliance policy, procedure, or SOP.']
  policy_owner_department varchar [note: 'Business unit or department that owns the policy.']
  regulatory_citations varchar [note: 'List of statutes, regulations, or standards referenced (e.g., HIPAA, ACA, CMS).']
  review_date date [note: 'Date the most recent review was performed.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the review process.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the review (scheduled annual, triggered by event, or regulatory change).']
  revision_summary varchar [note: 'Brief description of changes made in the latest version.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the policy record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier (e.g., 1.0, 2.1) tracking revisions.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who initially created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each internal compliance policy, procedure, or SOP — including version history and review tracking. Covers HIPAA privacy/security policies, FWA program policies, ACA compliance procedures, credentialing policies, and claims adjudication standards. Captures policy name, category, owner, effective date, version number, approval status, regulatory citations, and distribution scope. Tracks periodic reviews with review date, reviewer, review type (annual, triggered, regulatory change), outcome (approved, revised, retired), revision summary, next review date, and approver sign-off. Serves as the authoritative policy registry supporting NCQA, URAC, and CMS audit readiness.'
}
